description:
You are a band of mighty heroes delving deep into a dungeon rife with treasure and troubles. The skills of the Wizard and the Rogue allow you to navigate the mazes, puzzles and traps with ease. The mighty Fighter decimates the weak goblins and orcs in your path, and the Cleric eliminates the undead with blasts of holy light. But every now and then a more troublesome foe presents itself, and you must work together to defeat the most
dangerous creatures in the dungeon. It is time to Roll... or Die!
In Roll or Die players take turns rolling 5 polyhedral dice (d6,d8,10,d12, and d20)and taking actions to adjust or reroll. After 3 actions the dice are considered "locked" and a result must be filled in on the player sheet, in a series of specific requirements.
After 33 turns all boxes are either filled in or crossed out, and each player will have a final score. The highest scoring player is the winner.

—description from the designer

The action gets bigger and louder with two new trucks and a crew of experts.

More players at the table = more fun. Expand the player count of BIGFOOT: Roll & Smash to 6 Players AND get two more trucks from TEAM BIGFOOT's history: BIGFOOT #5 and Ms Bigfoot.

Every driver needs a great crew. With this expansion, you'll get a chance to draft Monster Truck experts to help you plan and execute your run.

MODS Expansion Includes:

     5 - 6 Player Rules
     CREW expansion - Draft Monster Truck Experts to your team!
     *BONUS* - 8 new Obstacle Tiles


Featuring two fan-favorite playable BIGFOOT trucks:

     World Record Breaker -  BIGFOOT #5
     Queen of Crush - Ms. Bigfoot
     Player boards and Style decks for each truck


—description from the publisher

A rug pull is a degenerate act in the world of cryptocurrency trading where developers scam investors and traders alike by pumping up the price of a coin and later dumping as much as they can for profit before abandoning their project.

Rugpull is a cryptocurrency trading board game that simulates what it feels like to facilitate or be the a victim of a rug pull. Players will compete to out trade and out mine other players to end up with the highest profit. By managing your risk properly or by being risky biscuit, you must take the necessary actions to come out as the most profitable degenerate.

The main object of the game is to attain as much victory points by collecting money tiles and money cards throughout the game.

Each player will start with a secret coin as well as 30 money tiles that corresponds to the colour of their wallet(player mat)and take turns in a clockwise direction. There are 6 different actions but players may only perform up to 5 different actions during their turn (some actions can be performed more than once per turn). These actions can be performed in any order:

Buy a coin (Players who choose to buy cannot sell a coin on the same turn)
Sell a coin (Players who choose to sell cannot buy a coin on the same turn)
Mine money cards
Draw support cards
Activate support cards (You can do this action more than once per turn)
Rug pull (You can do this action more than once per turn)

Money tiles that are collected and taken away are placed on the area of the player mat(wallet) where it says "Total Balance". For the end game scoring money tiles are worth different amount of VP depending on their colour. Money tiles that are the same colour as the wallet is worth 1VP and money tiles that are not white and are not the same colour as the wallet are worth 3VP.Money cards collected are worth 2 points each. The Player with the highest VP wins!

When there is a tie, the player to memorize Proverbs 13:11 ~ "Wealth gained hastily will dwindle,
but whoever gathers little by little will increase it." first will win the game.

The battle for Ahasa has reached its apex: as armies breach its sanctums, banners are once more crossed, awakening old rivalries. Where once the glorious Nuptian Embassy ruled, now only a crater dwells, made of crumbled walls and smashed doors. Through its halls covered by debris, Heroes still fight to claim dominion of the Ashen Fortress.

Embassy Ruins is a new and quick-to-play 3-lane battlefield supporting 4 heroes per side. The box contains a neoprene playmat with stitched edges measuring 35x53cm (14x21in).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Hero: Volume One – Starter Set
description:
Dungeon Hero is a simple, fast, solitaire RPG game in a print-and-play "zine" format.

Create a hero using eight defining characteristics, like Archer of the Realm or Chainmail or Stealthy.  Assign them dice ranging from d12 to d6. Note your Health and Stamina levels, and then select an adventure 'zine.

Each adventure 'zine packs a lot of adventure for such a little size! Using a clever new exploration mechanic, each foray into the dungeon will offer different challenges using a simple combination of two dungeon elements.  Select two of your own traits and roll off against the two dungeon elements, and see if you can beat the dangerous situation.  If you roll well, you survive to continue deeper.  If you fail, you will take damage. But don't despair – you can spend Stamina to re-roll dice before you apply their effects!

If you can make it through the dungeon, you win! Your score is how many treasures you manage to bring out of the dungeon – see if you can beat your high score by playing again!

Volume One of Dungeon Hero contains three eight-page 'zines that you print out, cut, and fold yourself:


     Dungeon Hero Core Rules – The core rulebook for playing Dungeon Hero. Explains the rules for character creation and gameplay, and provides an example of play.



     Castle Charon – Robed cultists have been seen filing into the ruins of King Charon's castle. Chanting has been heard, and demons have been sighted. Can you set things right?



     Fortress of the Frost Giant Jarl – Freezing and starving, you find a Viking outpost, but to stay with them for the winter, you must help them put down the Frost Giant jarl.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Dungeon Hero: Volume Two – Giant Beasts
description:
Already played all the adventures from Dungeon Hero: Volume One – Starter Set?  Went straight to Volume Three: Bump in the Night and skipped Volume Two?  Well, more adventures await! Print these out, fold 'em up, and get questing!

Dungeon Hero: Volume Two features three new adventures centered around a theme of Giant Beasts:


     Web of the Spider Queen – The Queen of Spiders seeks to conquer the sunlit world above. Can you stop her before she rises?



     Tower of the Terrapin – The Terrapin Cult hoards the only cure a beloved mentor needs. But can friends made in the City of Scoundrels be trusted to help infiltrate the tower?



     The Lost Island – Shipwrecked on an island that was nowhere to be found on the maps, you struggle to survive on an island stuck in time.


Bonus Adventure! Also gain access to the bonus adventure:


     The New Hill – A massive mound of dirt and sand appears in a farmer's field. What terrors does it herald?


Note: You will need the core rulebook from the Starter Set (Volume One) of Dungeon Hero to play these adventures.

boardgame: Dungeon Hero: Volume Three – Bump in the Night
description:
Already played all the adventures from Dungeon Hero: Volume One – Starter Set and tore through all the challenges in Volume Two: Giant Beasts?  Well, maybe it's time you face off against the terrors that dwell in dark places! Print these out, fold 'em up, and get questing!

Dungeon Hero: Volume Three features three new adventures centered around a theme of paying homage to silver screen horror movies:


     Full Moon Frenzy – Something is stalking the night under the light of the full moon. Can you stop its rampage?



     The Terror of Talonberg – A fortune-teller has read to you her cards, but can her prophecies really help you destroy the evil terrorizing the town?



     The Pyramid of Semerkhet – Dehydrated and exhausted, you see the pyramid on the horizon. If not water, at least shade may be found – but what other horrors await you in that awful tomb?


Bonus Adventure! Also gain access to the bonus adventure:


     Creature of the Ebon Fen – A group of scholars have hired you to escort them to a lagoon of biological interest, but are the rare fish there worth the risk of entering the unknown jungle?

boardgame: Fable on Your Table: Realm of Goblyns
description:
Realm of Goblyns is a new realm for the Fable on Your Table print-and-play miniatures game which adds new enemies and challenges to the base game.

The threat of the goblyns has always been near; the creatures live in the nearby wilderness, but they typically kept to themselves. But something sinister is driving them into the civilized lands, arming them, training them, coordinating their attacks, and stoking hatred. What was once an uneasy truce has devolved into bloodshed. The mothers of Bandi look to you, hero, to do what is necessary to avert this imminent threat.

Realm of Goblyns includes:

Several new base enemy types: Goblyn warriors, Goblyn archers, Goblyn shamans, Hobgoblyns, evil presences, and assassins.

New large enemies: Goblyn archer towers and the terrifying Apex Goblyn.
Some new scenery to spice up your table play.

The companion web app has been updated to generate dungeons and quests with these creatures.

Download the PDF, print it (we recommend on card stock), assemble the miniatures, and get ready to delve into the dungeon!

—description from the designer

The game theme refers to a tale by La Fontaine: The grasshopper and the ant.
Each player starts with his ant tile in one corner of the board designed as a 9 by 9 grid. On your turn, place one tile of your hand on the board to extend your route and advance your ant on it. Alternatively, with a grasshopper tile, you can block the route of an opponent. By paying a grain of wheat, you can deblock your route. By landing on a space with a grain of wheat, you collect it. The player with the most grains wins.

—user summary

Science officers brought back a strange alien fungus from a survey mission to an asteroid. Following the usual protocols, the fungus was placed in quarantine, but its explosive growth when exposed to the warmth of the ship caused it to break out of its containment area, shattering the glass and releasing spores into the ventilation shafts.

Now, the alien fungus, dubbed the "Phage" by the ship's scientists, is infecting the crew, turning them into lurching, zombie-like horrors that are taking over the ship! The central power grid has failed, and half the crew are either infected or still in cryosleep.

As captain, it's your job to make the life-and-death decisions. You need to get as many crew members off the ship as possible, but you cannot allow the Phage to escape. You'll have to wake the remaining crew members from cryosleep, power up the lifeboat hangar, keep the oxygen flowing, and initiate the self-destruct sequence. Consult your science officers to gain insights into how the Phage works. Send your engineers to jury-rig mechanisms to keep the Phage at bay. Task your operations personnel with keeping the oxygen flowing and the power online. Order your security officers to cover the retreat of your crew. How many can you save from humanity's first encounter with the Phage?

In this strategic solitaire game, you play cards that represent your crew members to different areas of the ship. The game also plays "Phage" cards representing the ever-advancing influence of the alien fungus to the same areas. Using a simple trick-taking mechanic, you quickly determine whether the humans successfully achieve their goals (such as powering up the Bio Lab or restoring the oxygen supply by repairing the oxygen scrubbers), or whether the Phage have killed off the humans attempting to do so.

Each "station" on the ship provides different winning and losing effects, and managing these effects in the face of the onslaught of the Phage is the key to rescuing crew members from the ship. You win by activating the self-destruct sequence and destroying all of the Phage. You lose if the Phage escapes the ship. Your score at the end of the game is how many of your crew members escape.

In Tiny Epic Vikings: Ragnarok, you will have to sharpen your axes and your skill as you explore new islands and experience firsthand the battle of the Giants (Jötunn) vs. the Gods and the cunning of the Valkyries. The Ragnarok expansion introduces Giants that will control the islands and weaken the god's fury. It also introduces Valkyries and Giant Slayers that will join forces with your Vikings to combat the Giants! Also included are 6 new gods to further expand the strategies and variability of Tiny Epic Vikings!

-description from Kickstarter campaign
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: No Soldier Left Behind: Battle of the Bulge
description: No Soldier Left Behind is a cooperative wargame which puts each player into the shoes of a single American soldier. By choosing what to focus on – firing on the enemy, being stealthy, or even summoning your courage by looking at a photo of the girl back home - you test your mettle against the forces of the German army. Can you complete your mission, and bring back all your buddies in the process?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Learn to play Welcome Aboard, Captain with this exciting adventure that leads you through all the core mechanics of the game.

Welcome Aboard Captain is a solitaire print-and-play tabletop roleplaying game where you play a starship captain, gather a crew, outfit a ship, and head into the galaxy to complete missions.

Charon Wormhole is a special document designed to teach you how to play by introducing the rules step by step as you go through an adventure.

To play, you will need:


     This document, accessible in a reader. Do not print it – it's a hyperlinked "choose your own adventure" style document intended to be played on your device.



     Paper and pencil. (Index cards recommended.)



     At least one set of polyhedral dice: d4, d6, d8, d10, d12.  Four sets recommended, but not necessary.

"Welcome Aboard, Captain" is a solitaire science-fiction tabletop roleplaying game.

In this game, you take the role of a starship captain, recruit a crew of bridge officers (science, engineering, etc.), outfit your ship, and head out into the galaxy to go on missions.

The game is a print-and-play game; you only need to print the starship sheet and the bridge officer roster to play, although there are some handy reference sheets to print as well.  The game comes with a pre-made ship and crew if you don't want to create your own.

The game features a procedural mission generator, where you roll dice to define a mission for your starship captain, and then you perform a series of "command decisions" that the generator reacts to, allowing a dynamic adventure to unfold based on tables you roll on in the book.

Challenges are met by rolling dice from the ship and crew against threats that the procedural generator creates; whichever die is highest is the one that determines what happens.  As you work your way through the mission, you'll be rolling to advance your mission until it is completed, but surprise revelations and other unexpected challenges can throw plot twists into the narrative.

In addition to the core gameplay, there are three "gamebooks" with the game that let you play the game with the same mechanics but through pre-written adventures. One of them, "Charon Wormhole", is a learn-to-play adventure where you are introduced to the mechanics of the game while playing through a tense adventure about taking your first command.

The core game also includes optional extension materials for advanced play, adding excursion vehicles, companions, vignette missions, and nemesis enemies.

Tablero di Berona is a quick-playing dice game for two players, with all the look and feel of a classic pub game. The game uses a simple 3x6 board, two dice, and twelve stacking pieces per player.

Players take turns rolling two dice. With each die, they may either place a new chip on that location in their home row, or move a stack from their home row if they already have a stack in that space. Movement is similar to games like Backgammon, Kalah (Mancala), Veritas, or Tak: a stack of chips is dropped one piece at a time, covering other stacks as it goes.

When either player runs out of options, the other player gets just one more turn, and then the winner is the player who captures the most points in the center.

-description from publisher's website

Lurking behind the thin vale of sanity, ancient beings of imaginable power lurk, forever ready to bring the world to its knees. These godlike beings from beyond time and space can warp the mind, break the will, and obliterate the body. Now, as they stand against each other, the world will be plunged into a maelstrom of horror and madness. Will your hand guide the victors - or will your eternal legacy be wiped out by a being more cunning, more ruthless, and more malevolent than you?

Welcome to a world of unspeakable horror inspired by the works of Howard Phillips Lovecraft, and the Call of Cthulhu roleplaying game, where beings older than time itself manifest on Earth and fight each other, as they prepare to dominate our world.

During the game, you will take on the role of an alliance of two nightmarish creatures from the Cthulhu mythos. Your cards will generate Horror that will allow you to power especially formidable abilities and effects, including summoning your Avatars to the field of battle.

At the beginning of your turn you rotate the Power Cores on the Character cards and other cards in play area, dealing damage to the opponent, gaining defense and raising horror - a special resource used for powerful actions. Then you can play up to two actions, chosen from: rotating a chosen Power Core again, playing a card (ally, ability, item or maneuver), drawing a card or sometimes summoning an Avatar. At the end there is an obligatory card draw. You win if you deal enough damage to the opponent's Characters or if your opponent has to draw a card from an empty deck.

-description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
An educational game for coffee-traders produced for the biggest coffee trader in Germany "Tchibo".
Try to get the biggest income -
answer questions -
includes 6 beautiful designed car-minis.

boardgame: Golden Annual Number 3: Great War at Sea – South Seas Mandate
description:
Great War at Sea: South Seas Mandate is based on what US Navy planners foresaw as the opening stages of War Plan Orange, the plan for a Pacific naval war against Japan. It is set in the years just after the First World War and features ships planned but never built: the American Design 150 battle cruiser, the Japanese Type X light battlecruiser, the original design for Japan’s Furutaka-class light cruiser and more.

Great War at Sea: South Seas Mandate includes 50 die-cut and silky-smooth playing pieces, thirty double-sized “long” ship pieces and 20 standard-sized square ones. There’s a 22x17-inch map (on heavy rigid stock), and a booklet with ten scenarios and the background story of the campaign.

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Tinderblox Sunset puts players in a campfire setting in which each player attempts to keep the fire burning, but if you place the blocks badly, you risk burning down the camp!

Each turn, players draw a card from the campfire deck which instructs them to place a log, an ember, or any combination of them in various orientations on top of the campfire. Players use tweezers to "play with fire" and if you drop anything, you're considered a fire hazard and you're removed from the game. The player who is the most careful with fire wins!

Differences between Tinderblox Day, Night and Sunset:
Tinderblox Day is the original game.
Tinderblox Night is a limited edition version which includes more challenging cards.
Tinderblox Sunset includes the Marshmallow mini-expansion (otherwise sold separately) and is made from FSC-compliant materials.

Alphabet Mix is a word game for English- or French-speaking children. It has 78 letter cards and 2 wild cards, the cards being more like cardboard tiles, 2" x 2". It is a variant of the traditional game Memory.

The 80 cards are laid out in a 8x10 grid face down. The first player states a word of 3+ letters. Then each player in turn reveals two cards, and if either of both of them spell the next letters of the stated word, they add them to the word and score their point values; otherwise they replace unused tiles face down. The word must be spelled in order. The player who finishes the current word states another word, and play continues spelling the new word. Once 5 words have been spelled, all the cards are reshuffled and re-dealt.

The player who achieves an agreed-on total -- 50 is recommended for beginners -- wins the game.

Wordy Birdy is a turn-based card game version of "word find" puzzles. Its deck has 66 letter cards and 3 "Wordy Birdy" wild cards that stand for any letter. Letters have a point value; wild cards have 0 points. One copy of each vowel is a "Double word score" card.

Each player is dealt 5 cards, then 40 more cards are dealt into an 8x5 array, face up.  Each player in their turn makes a word out of letters in the grid with one card from the hand. The letters in the grid must make a chain of adjacent cards that doesn't overlap itself; the card from the hand can be inserted anywhere in the word, including the first letter.  The player keeps those letters and their score, and the dealer fills the holes in the array from the deck, until it is used up. A player may pass if they can see no play.

The games ends after each player has made 5 words, or each player passes in turn. The highest total score wins.

A booster train line for Touched On, allowing 1 extra player to join for a fashionable festive season…

-description from the publisher's website

A set collection game for 2-4 players. Players are pirates out to sell the most valuable jewelry to the self-crowned king of the Caribbean over the course of one year. Players assemble cards representing jewelry of seven different types with seven different gems. There are an assortment of bonuses to be earned when the jewelry is sold. The princess will pay for the number of pieces in the set (1 Real for 1 piece up to 21 Reals for 6 pieces). The queen will pay a bonus for three different types of either setting and/or gem that change each month. And the king pays for sets at the end of the year; his demands are revealed one-at-a-time at the beginning of each month. After a year of selling jewels to the family, if you can sail away with the most doubloons you win.

The Exploding Words deck has 69 letter cards and 1 rules card. Each vowel has 3 normal cards, 1 double word score card, and 1 Kaboom card.

Each player is dealt a 7-card hand, and then over 5 rounds, players draw a card then discard a card, trying to have the best words to play.  After 5 rounds players in turn lay down the word(s) from their hand.

Kaboom cards cannot be discarded, and they blow up any word they are in, giving it no score. A Kaboom card can be played in one of your words, blowing it up but leaving the other words okay, or you can keep it in hand and play it on top of an identical letter in a word someone else plays after you. If you don't play a Kaboom in any word, yours or someone else's, your whole hand explodes and you get 0 points that hand. 7-letter words are immune to explosion.

After all words are revealed and exploded, players score for their remaining words and another hand is played. The first player who gets a total score of 100 or more wins the game.

Crokinole Imperium brings three brand new experiences to the classic game of crokinole, by introducing new cards and rules that are combined with rich Medieval-Latin lore.

SUB ROSA meaning, “Under the Rose” is a Latin phrase that has been used in the middle ages to denote secrecy or confidentiality. In this game each player will earn points by completing their unique objective card and by outscoring their opponents.

FREE LANCES refers to mercenaries whose lance was free of any lord’s service. Each mercenary will offer you a single use ability that could tip the round in your favor, but be careful not to let their valuable skills go to waste.

TERRA NOVA meaning, "New Land" brings about a new land card each round that applies to all players requiring you to significantly alter your strategy. Learn the terrain and use it to your advantage or fail to adapt and see an abrupt end to your thoughts of victory.

—description from the designer

Secret of John Nebthos A Card Game of Adventure and Fame!

John Nebthos, the world's most eclectic explorer, has mysteriously sent a letter to exploring guilds around the world; an invitation to McGuffin Mountain to rediscover the lost treasures of history locked away in Nebthos' hidden mountain vault. Race against other guild members in the pursuit of fame and treasure as you travel from the four corners of the earth to McGuffin Mountain. Make new discoveries, thwart your opponents, and enjoy some good espresso along your journey, until you breach the mountain to uncover the treasures within...and of course before the mountain inevitably explodes.

A fun ice breaker card game for 2-4 players Ages 12+ 30 Minutes Playtime

—description from the publisher

Conspiracy is a card game released in spring 2023, on Kickstarter and was chosen Project We Love by KS. It is a fun, fast-paced tableau-builder for those who wanna try what it's like to be a conspiracy theory believer.

Chose your core conspiracy (card) and build your unique theory around it by winning news (cards) and explaining how those fit your theory. (Don't worry explanation is optional and you can skip it by playing the game in Flat Earther variant.) Each conspiracy card gives a special ability to its believer. Use it wisely to become more influential than your rivals and their childish tales.

In Conspiracy, each player acts simultaneously during the two rounds of a turn. There are three types of action they can choose from: action card drawing, playing an action card, and flipping a news card. Action cards give you some useful abilities and influence among the believers. The most influential wins the actual news (card) at the end of the turn. By your clairvoyance ability, you can flip your news cards to see the hidden message between the lines and get a useful bonus.

A player wins and the game ends when any player reaches the given number of followers and is able to keep them until the end of the turn.

Conspiracy offers a cooperative and a solo mode as well where you can work together (or alone) to uncover the shadow government before it's too late. Merge your conspiracy cards to the ultimate great grandfathers of all conspiracy theories and play action cards side by side against the shadow government cards to win the actual news (card), protect your theory and your hands.

In cooperative mode, the game ends when the players reach the given number of followers and keep them until the end of the turn or when the shadow government deck is depleted.

The core mechanics of the game are: hand management, flipping a card, bid, tableau building, variable player powers, and narrative building.

boardgame: My Words Are Better Than Yours
description:
This game is a card-based adaptation of Scrabble game-play. The deck has 69 letter cards, a majority of which are basic cards and just have a letter score.  For each vowel, there are 3 basic cards, and one each of x2, x3, and x4 letter score cards, which multiply adjacent consonants in their word by that amount.  There are also 15 consonant cards that multiply the score for the entire word by x2, x3, or x4.

Each player is dealt an 8-card hand. On their turn, a player plays a word from their hand that must use one basic card from the previous player's word if there is one; they tally the word's score and then draw to fill their hand back to 8 cards. A player may also just pass and draw one card.

The game ends after the deck runs out and all players pass in turn. The highest total score wins the game.

boardgame: Chachapoya: SNAKE Adventurer Promo Cards II
description:
The base Chachapoya game is expanded by 2 new different adventurer cards. We shuffle the SNAKE Adventurer promo cards into the adventurer's deck, then prepare the other cards as its written in the game rules.

THE BLUFF: The player leads The BLUFF card and immadiately collects all the played money that the players have already bet.

THE PARASITE: The player leads The Parasite card and chooses one player who has to discard one of the played adventurer cards. The attacked player takes all of their cards back and bets again.

Enjoy playing the game and have fun with treasure hunting!

boardgame: 王宮の飾り絵師 (Painter in the Royal Palace)
description:
In 王宮の飾り絵師 (Ōkyū No Kazari Eshi), roughly translated as Royal Palace Decorative Painter, the players are each painters who have been hired by the royal family to paint the Monochrome Palace.

Each player receives a die showing different colors and one showing different numbers.  The players also get a sheet which shows the outline of a painting which they will be coloring in during the game.

On a turn, the players simultaneously choose the faces for their dice and then reveal.  Beginning with players who chose the smaller number, players can color in sections on their sheet corresponding to the number and color chosen as their dice values.  However, as the resolution proceeds to higher valued die, you will not be able to fill in colors that were chosen by players with lower numbers, and other similar concerns.

The game includes royal request cards that can grant points at the end of the game (e.g. have the most sectors of a certain color) or during the game (e.g. be the first do have one section of each color).

The game ends after 12 rounds, and the player with the most points wins.
designers: Kosuke Zaitsu (ザイツ コウスケ)

description:
This is a pair of children's card games to celebrate and advertise the release of Cadaco's Countin' Sheep line of plushy sheep dolls. The games are:

Sheep Sweep

A version of Crazy 8s. In turn each player plays a card that matches the previous card in either color or name of the plushy  doll on the card. If no play is possible, they draw a card and play it if possible.  Lucky cards can change the color; Sleep cards make the player to your right skip their next turn. The first player to play all their cards wins.

Go Graze

A version of Go Fish.  There are four cards for each of 13 ranks. In their turn a player asks another player for cards of a certain rank; if they have any they must give them all, otherwise they say "Go graze" and the asker draws a card, showing and counting it if it's the requested rank.  A successful play lets the player play again. A complete set of 4 of a rank are placed on the table. After all the cards are placed in sets, the player with the most sets wins.

The American victory at Saratoga in 1777 and their Treaty with France in 1778 transformed the war in America. The potential influx of fresh French troops to swell the ranks of General Washington’s Army drove the British to rethink their colonial strategy. As a result the British moved their efforts from the middle colonies to the southern colonies where it was believed sentiment for the crown’s cause ran strong. Having secured Savannah in 1779 the British turned their eyes on Charleston and South Carolina. When the British crossed the Savannah River South Carolina became the focal point of the revolution.

The years, 1780-1781, in South Carolina saw some of the most intense fighting that occurred in the revolution as both Loyalists and Patriots both rose up in numbers to fight for or against the crown. The struggle for independence during those two years saw over 200+ actions, skirmishes, and battles fought in South Carolina resulting in the names of Marion, Morgan, and Tarleton and places such as Kings Mountain and Cowpens entering American lore.

Crown and Crescent models the two years of intense, fierce fighting in South Carolina adopting and modifying the unique and innovative command and control mechanics created by the Ragnar Brothers for ANGOLA!  These mechanics keep players guessing, create plenty of tension, and reward the right balance of boldness and caution as you guide your forces towards independence or crushing the revolting colonists. The map is divided into seven districts, encompassing 6 to 7 key locations each, used to show control of the district. Control enough key locations and you’ll then control the district and therefore advance on the victory point track towards winning the game.

The counters represent military units and auxiliaries such as artillery, rifled and light troops, grenadiers, dragoons, and cavalry. A unique command and activation system has players planning in secret the order in which they will activate a limited number of their units for the turn. The players then take turns activating their units in the determined order. The result is a tense game, with players trying to analyze the map and out guess their enemies.

Crown and Crescent can be played with between two to four players (Continental and Whigs vs British and Tories).

Components:

        One 22”x34” full-color map
        Two full sheets of counters (352 5/8" counters)
        One full color rule book
        One deck of 55 cards
        4 player aid cards
        20 dice


—description from the publisher

description:
In Interval Initiate, players clash in 3 epic rounds of Note playing and Interval claiming! At the start of each round, each player drafts an Initiate card and draws Note cards equal to the combined health of all the Initiates on their team.
During each round, players take turns placing Note cards to either the left or right Notes stacks. Be the fastest to SLAP the correct Interval card that matches the interval between the Note cards to claim it! Beware, slapping the wrong Interval causes you to lose Notes! The player with the most intervals at the end of 3 rounds wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lord of the Chords: Who Let The Chords Out? – 2nd Booster Pack: Composers
description: What is the best way to commemorate the greatest classical composers of all time? Pun-ify their names and crack jokes about their past of course! It's your Rachmanin-off day! Ravel in the Satie-sfaction of not needing to go Bach to work with puns that you'll never be Hayden!

boardgame: Lord of the Chords: Who Let The Chords Out? – 3rd Booster Pack: Modulation

Grapple is a word game where players combine word roots with prefixes and/or suffixes to make valid words.

There are three decks, for Prefixes, Roots, and Suffixes, each having 50 cards and 2 blanks.  Each player is dealt 2 cards from each deck. 
During their turn, a player: 
- Draws a card from any deck
- Does any of the following, as many times as they can:
--- Lay down a word made from 2-3 cards in the hand
--- Add a card from the hand to a word on the table to make a new valid word which is moved to be in front of them.
--- Take a card from a word on the table, if it leaves a valid word with 2 cards, to make a new word using the stolen card.
- If the player is unable to make any plays during the turn, they discard back down to 6 cards.

The player to empty their hand first wins.

description:
"Play: Playing is the highest stage of a child’s development." (Friedrich Fröbel)

This Fröbel Tower is a cooperative game for 2-16 players aged four and over. It is also well suited for adult team building.

The game consists of:

    6 beechwood blocks
    1 bracket with metal rod
    1 plate with 8 holes and 16 cords (each 2 metres long)


For the Fröbel Tower, a large outdoor or indoor play area is necessary.

Object of the game
The construction of the Fröbel Tower is possible only by means of joint, coordinated action. The Fröbel Tower can also be built in a competition between different groups.
The group that can build the highest tower wins the game in this version.

Preparation
Form a circle and place the six beechwood blocks in the middle with the groove facing upwards.

Rules
Each player takes one or more cord ends in his or her hand, depending on the number of participants.
The cords were previously passed through the 8 holes in the plate. Now you tighten the cords and try to hook the bracket into the groove of a wooden block, lift it up carefully and place it gently on another wooden block.
It is important that you hold the cord only at the very end. It must not be shortened by reaching forward.

Together you try to stack the wooden blocks on top of each other until the Fröbel Tower is completed. You have won the game as a team if you manage to use all the wooden blocks and the tower does not fall over during the building work.
When you hook in the bracket, this may cause a wooden block to fall over. Therefore, you should decide before the game starts whether this wooden block can be put back in place or whether it is eliminated from the game.

Game variants

    The game is not over until you have dismantled the tower as a team.
    You are not allowed to speak during the game. Naturally, this makes building the tower more difficult because you cannot give each other any instructions.
    You are only allowed to guide the cord with one hand. This also adds another level of difficulty.
    The most difficult way to build the tower is to guide the end of the cord with your "weak" hand. For right-handers it is the left hand, for left-handers the right hand.


—description from the publisher

A misterious collector wants to get all the incredible creatures on the planet. She has selected us to complete her collection, taking us to an island of unknown whereabouts where the strangest beings on the planet live.
Became an explorer in a truly beautiful and unique tabletop game experience.

SUWANGO is a highly thematic game where players must fulfill the SUWANGO Code to capture the creatures they have to get to fulfill their secret objective. Beware of the rest of the explorers, they will not hesitate to sabotage your mission to be the first to get the coveted loot.
The first to complete their board will end the game and the one with the most points will win.

Lupercalia beckons. Through decadence and debauchery these lustful figures can be called from the depths of Earth’s history. Espers made manifest only through esoteric quiet whispers shared amongst the elite. Such coveted creatures are called upon for far more than combat. Yet, their true names and seals are clutched closely to the hearts of those who covet them most. Just like our Cross Promotion Pack, our Resin Lupercalia Kits from last year are now available in plastic. The Lupercalia 2021 Pack includes a plastic set of all seven of the miniatures we made in Resin from our last Kickstarter. This also includes their Item Cards, Combatant Cards, and Adventurer Cards.

Profane Occultists Made Immortal

“Toxie scrubbed the blood from her hands, noting idly that she had nailed the color when choosing her crimson fingernail polish. In the far corner of the abandoned warehouse, a half dozen corpses, all still bound hand and foot, lay in a heap as testament to her night’s labors. Her final victim lay sprawled on the concrete floor behind her, still breathing shallowly through his broken, twisted mouth. She hadn’t actually been planning to pull out his teeth when she started questioning him, but then Luperci and Tixie had radioed in that they cleared the enemy’s hideout, and that Mena was one of the captives they had freed. Threatening the Faun was one thing; dragging an innocent child into it was something else altogether.

Toxie dried her hands gingerly, then turned around to consider her captive. He was definitely dying; even if someone found him now, there weren’t enough surgeons or drugs on Earth to save him. But Toxie had to meet up with her pack to run down the last of the heathens who had escaped with the Faun, and she didn’t like leaving jobs unfinished.

She hunkered down next to him and saw with satisfaction that his eyes were still open, though only just through the swollen wreckage of his face. She blew him a kiss.

“You get a little mercy now,” she crooned as she gently stroked a blood-free patch of skin on his forehead. “I’m late for an orgy, so I can’t really wait around for you to die. You know how it is. . .” 
She turned to her gear bag and considered her options. She wanted to use her favorite, but, of course, it had to be the messiest of the lot and she had just finished cleaning up. Eh, what the hell, she thought. Grinning, Toxie grabbed the pink handle and turned back to finish the job.

Outside the warehouse, an early morning train rumbled past, just loud enough to mask the roar of a chainsaw.”

Breaking the Luftwaffe is a scenario and campaign book for the popular Check Your 6! Air Combat and Campaign Rules. The book provides three campaigns and 26 scenarios under one cover. The first 9 scenarios follow the beginning of the US 8th Air Force’s escorted strategic bombing campaign over western Germany between October 1943 and January 1944; the next 9 scenarios recreate the intense combat during the “Big Week” air offensive in early 1944; the final 8 scenarios bring together the desperate fighting during Mission 250, the first daylight mission to Berlin. The book also features some of WWII’s top aces including Robert S. Johnson, Gabby Gabreski, “Bud” Anderson, Heinz “Pritzl” Bär, Anton Hackl, and Heinz Knoke. The scenarios can be played individually, linked to form three mini-campaigns, or combined as one grand campaign.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering — Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate
description:
This expansion for Magic: The Gathering reintroduces the Commander Draft format originally seen in the first Commander Legends and fuses it with the Dungeons & Dragons theme of Adventures in the Forgotten Realms, using the Forgotten Realms city of Baldur's Gate as the setting.

Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate is designed to be drafted. Each player opens a 20-card booster pack, selects two cards from it, and then passes the pack to the player sitting next to them. They then pick two more cards from the pack they've been handed, and so on, going around the table until all cards are picked. This is repeated with a second booster pack, and again with a third, until everyone has picked a total of 60 cards. Players then choose a commander (or a commander/background pair, if the commander has the Choose a Background ability) from among the cards they drafted, add any number of basic lands, and build a 60-card deck matching the color identity of their commander(s). Then everyone shuffles up their decks and battles in a free-for-all! (A larger pod of drafters splits into two groups first.)

The set is sold in draft boosters (individually or in displays of 24), set boosters (individually or in displays of 18), collector boosters (individually or in displays of 12), and bundles, as well as three-pack prerelease kits, which were made available a week in advance of the set's full release. In addition, four 100-card tie-in Commander decks were released alongside the main set.

Pitch Out 2 is a box with new teams (Nomads and Seeds) for the Pitch Out system. The two boxes can be played separately (2 players), or combined (3 and 4 players).

It is a disk-flicking game with tactical elements in which players try to take out or isolate the opponent's leader.

The game is played with a bunch of disks (5-8 for the base sets) and five "walls". In turns, players place their pieces and two walls on their side of the field. Then, they start flicking one disk per turn, trying to push the opponent's disks out of the field.

Each disk has a special power, for example the Assassin just needs to touch an opponent's piece to take it out, so players need to play tactically.

The first player to take out the opponent's leader or all their other pieces wins the game.

boardgame: A game about quickly grabbing creatures that are totally different and counting your RADISHES!
description:
This is the 'superhard' version of "A game about quickly grabbing creatures that are totally different and counting your beetroots." and "AGAQGCTATDACYTurnips."

Players race to grab creatures, but each creature you grab must have a different head (number of eyes), different body (number of arms), and different number of legs.

You can put vegetables in between creatures, but if you grab more than 5 portions, you have been deemed too greedy and lose the game.

In this 'super-hard' edition, the creatures are wearing things, looking away from the 'camera', contorting in weird positions and doing other things that might will add a few seconds of confusion, even for experienced players!

This game is NOT recommended for anyone who hasn't played the earlier editions.

To make it even more visually confusing, most of the vegetable cards have additional items on them. Monocle, sketchbook, or even stuffed toys of the creatures! (But they aren't creatures, they are only stuffed toys of creatures.)

One deck plays up to 3 players. Combine decks to play with more (2 decks for up to 6, 3 decks for up to 9)

There are 2 versions of AGAQGCTATDACYRadishes (coloured in, and line art) for easy mixing and separation.

Mondriaan is a game in which colors and colored areas as well as their number play a very special role.

As simple as monochrome rectangles may be, they can be used in many different ways, which is why totally different game variants await you here.

In one of them, you'll have to keep track of the colors and be quick to react. In the second, on the other hand, it won't hurt if you think a little ahead so that you end up where the most points are.

GAME VARIANT: DICE STUDY

For this variant you need one die and five chips or coins per person. The 12 Mondriaan cards are laid out in the center of the table in a 3x4 grid.

One person rolls a number, while another randomly reveals one of the color cards, if possible at the same time. The two together determine what you need to look for in the cards.

So, if there is a 6 and a red card, you have to look for a card that has six red color squares on it and quickly put one of your chips on it. Whoever succeeds first gets to discard the chip.

Since each combination occurs exactly twice, you can also get rid of two chips per round. If you have no more chips, you win the game.

GAME VARIANT: SPACE COMPOSITION WITH 6 COLORS

For this variant you need two dice and three differently sized game pieces or markers per person, each in one color.

The six color cards are placed face up on the table as a circle. The shuffled Mondriaan cards are distributed evenly to everyone.

Take turns placing one marker on each color card. Never place two markers of the same size on the same card, but place several markers of the same size on the same card.

When both dice are rolled, you take turns performing one of the following actions:
-  place a card from your hand on a color card matching the value of a die (if you roll a 6, you can place a card with six red squares on the red color card)

- place one of the upper Mondriaan cards already on the table on another color card, if the conditions there are right.

- place one of your own point markers on another color card

When a person puts down their last card, you calculate your points:
For the color with the most cards on it you get three points, for the next two and then one.
You then multiply these points by the values of your markers, from small to large with one to three.
Whoever has the most points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

description: Today is the big hatching day: the cute baby turtles in Turtle Bay are finally hatching from their eggs. And of course they want to get into the ocean as fast as possible. The second supplement for Stargrave offers a host of new scenarios and rules for gaining political backing as you fight across the ravaged galaxy.

The Dahlgren Belt was once a vast asteroid mining operation that supplied the entire sector with rare metals and various elemental fuels. However, the isolation of the last war caused the corporation to wither, and the various outposts and colonies were eventually left to look after themselves. Some became true collectives, or limited democracies, while others have fallen to the rule of gangsters, warlords, and despots. In all cases, the limited resources of air and organic material has made life difficult.

In this supplement for Stargrave, the crews must travel from asteroid-to-asteroid, and even down to the system's single, barely inhabitable planet, searching for a man who claims to have discovered a ‘big score'. During their journey, players will fight through numerous different environments - including asteroid mines, zero gravity, and even hellish jungle. Captains must curry favour with the various small political factions that hold power in the Belt. Only through a combination of wits, diplomacy, and weapons can players hope to reach the last prospector and gain a share of his claim…

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Book of Skulls: Slayers of Eragoth
description:
Heavy Metal meets Fantasy in Book of Skulls: Slayers of Eragoth

A huge dungeon-crawling adventure where two teams battle to save the continent of Eragoth.

For 2-4 players in 2 teams. Teams act as the others’ opponents, whilst one team controls the heroic Slayers, the opposing team will control the Demons they encounter until it is time to switch to their own Slayers.

The objective whilst controlling the Slayers is to clear the overworld and defeat the final boss first, whilst controlling the Demons your aim is to defeat the opposing team’s Slayers.

Replay value and combat is at the heart of Book of Skulls. During your adventure, you will have access to over 150 unique characters, each with their own movesets.

A game of high interactivity as players have no down time as they cross the continent, always engaged and always with high stakes.

Set your overworld using 10 interchangeable modular maps (levels) that each represent a unique region of Eragoth. A different adventure awaits with each game as any number of levels can be selected and placed in any order, allowing you to customise the duration of your game.

Book of Skulls is fully soundtracked with an original Metal soundtrack which perfectly sets the mood for battling the Demons of Eragoth.

—description from the publisher

Illeria is a fantasy wargame, in which players control small parties of former prisoners, who fight for survival on the defunct prison island of Illeria. Illeria plays like a cross between a skirmish-style wargame and 3D-chess.  Battles are fought on two parallel boards: the harsh Illerian desert of the physical world, and the mystical and surreal badlands of the spirit world.  Characters exist in both worlds, and actions in one world often affect the other.  For example, characters can spend actions in one world to boost their counterpart, and killing a character in one world greatly harms them in the other.

Illeria is a miniatures-agnostic game, in which you can play with any models you have.  It can be played as either a one-off game, or as part of an ongoing campaign.

This game is based on a paradox.  The fact that two armies went to war with modern equipment, and tactics based on experiences of another century. The belief that the bayonet and the massed charge of men and horses was mightier than machine guns and rapid firing field artillery, that stuck in the minds of the Generals and statesmen, even when proved wrong. The beginning of the 1914-1918 saw a "mobile war" of sort, a planned "blitzkrieg" of marching men and railway timetables that broke down. Could it have succeeded?  This game cannot give an answer, only a hint.

Game includes 1 tabloid-size map, 3 pgs. of rules, 88 counters (unmounted), and charts. This appears to be a game taken from Combat magazine. Description verbiage is taken verbatim from the game.

description:
«…With time and the Atavians’ forced collaboration, they learned to better exploit the Planets by installing powerful Proton Cannons and Orbital Bases to work as shipyard-shuttles. They also discovered the special riches in some Moons, and they established a commercial network by building Enclaves linked to the Stars…»

There are three types of special structures in the expansion: the Proton Cannon, the Commercial Enclave and the Orbital Base. Each player can only build one of them per turn, as long as there are tokens available and during any phase of their turn. They can be captured along planets (or stars in the case of Commercial Enclaves)

Essen 2022 preorder link

6 Sun Set action cards, that have more focus on the Voyage action.

The New Edition of Newton & Great Discoveries already include many promos, but not this one.


This Newton add-on provides 6 new Action cards (+1 rule card). Each player starts with one of these cards (they are assigned through a draft during setup): these are all travel actions, linked to a specific city on the map. Whoever plays such cards gets a very interesting power, but only if have visited the showed city (so providing a mid-term game objective).


Rules :

SETUP: Shuffle the Sun Set cards, then draw a number equal to the player count + 1 and place them face up on the table. Following the reverse turn order, each player chooses 1 card and place it in their hand together with their Starting Action cards. Place the remaining cards back in the box.

GAMEPLAY: The new cards are used normally to perform a Travel action. In addition, if you have already a Travel cube on the indicated University - City - Ancient Land, you will get the indicated bonus effect. If you don't have the cube there, you only perform the main action without the bonus.

(Sun Set cards are considered starting action cards for the objectives.)

Newton: New Horizon is a new game board that provides a new technology track. You can find spaces that
give VP in special conditions and objective spaces that give double VP.

In more detail, as long as you have at least one student on a triangular space, you receive 1 VP every time you perform the indicated action. You receive 1 VP if you perform it as a Basic Action and/or as a Special Effect from an action card and/or as an effect from an Invention tile or a Master card. The bonus is always 1 VP even if you have more students on the same space and even if the action's value is more than 1.

Two Objective spaces on the board show a x2 symbol and require 5 Volumes. If you have a Student here at the end of the game, you will receive double the VP provided by that Objective tile (so, if the tile shows a "max. X" limitation, also that limitation is doubled).

La bataille de Wattignies (AKA Wattignies 1793) covers the battle fought on 24 et 25 Vendémiaire an II (15th and 16th octobre 1793) between the French "Armée du Nord" under the command of General Jourdan and an Austrian army under the command of the prince of Coburg during the siege of Mauberg. It is one of the main victories of the French army during the wars of the French Revolution.

La bataille de Wattignies is the sixth game of the series "les soldats de la République".

3 scenarios are provided for this battle : the historical two-day battle, the battle without historical constraints and a shorter scenario covering only the second day.

Scale : Units: Brigades, regiments or battalions. 1 strength point equals 500-1000 infantry, or 300-600 cavalry
Map : 600 meters per hex
Turn : 90 minutes
Complexity : 6/9
The game includes an A2 map and 216 counters and markers.

description:
アトラスの後釜 (Atlas Junior) is a card game from Japan.

The publisher says:
Atlas, the giant that has sustained the world since mythical times, is no match for age.
The time has come to hand over the role to his successor. You are the young ace giants who have put up their hands as candidates for his successor.
The next Atlas is the one who has supported the world as it is gradually passed from one Atlas to the next, standing in his place until the end.
Now, who will show the most outstanding strength?

神話の代から世界を支え続けた巨人アトラスも年には勝てません。
役目を後任にたくす時が来ました。あなたたちは後任候補に手を挙げた若きエース巨人です。
アトラスから徐々に渡される世界を代わりばんこで支え、最後まで立っていた者が次のアトラスです。
さて、最もすぐれた力を示すのは誰でしょうか？
publisher's summary

As with the other editions of Tick Tack Bumm (aka Pass the bomb), speed is the most important factor in the pocket version. Here, however, no bomb goes from hand to hand, but everyone always plays at the same time! The bomb at Tick Tack Bumm Pocket can be found on some of the cards to be turned on: In this category it is important to find a word as quickly as possible that contains the letter combination you are looking for. Poetry fans have the advantage of looking for a suitable rhyme word. And with the magnifying glass symbol, a word that matches the respective category is required. If you play fast-paced and with brains, you quickly get closer to the goal of first discarding all cards and thus winning the game.

—description from the publisher

A new executive officer for Barrage, originally distributed at Modena Play 2022.


Franz De Lasse

When you perform a production action and fulfill a Contract with a lower value than the energy produced, move your marker on the Energy Track only by the exceeding value.

During the End of Round Phase, move your marker back on the Energy Track to the lower limit of the current round section (only if vou have reached it). Otherwise, don't move your marker back.

boardgame: Dark Matter: A Game of Strategy and Survival
description:
Dark Matter is a competitive sci-fi strategy board game for three to six players. Players will use strategy and diplomacy to earn victory points through expanding your empire with StarBase construction, researching dark matter that will populate in the center of the board through the game, through diplomacy with your rival players, construction and protection of your flagship, and by completing objectives. Offers board and event randomization so that no two games are the same.

—description from the designer

description:
In the year 2511 in a society run through Augmented Reality; omnipotent corporations have replaced each continent. A fast paced, cutting edge game known as BANKS is the neo-gladiator approved sport on Earth, with teams embodying each of their corporate spheres. As players you fight for your sphere's personal freedoms and to gain corporate favor.

In BANKS you face up to five various opponents. With the disks distributed over the light rails, your goal at the end of the game is the product of the number of disks in each captured color. Highest score wins, AMASS!

On your turn, move one of your disks in a straight line over any number of open spaces, possibly banking off edges or corners of the board, (like pool). You may stop at any point after moving at least one space.

If, after banking off at least one edge, your disk lands on a space occupied by any other disk (even one of your own disks), capture that disk by replacement. At the end of the game, your score is the number of disks in each captured color multiplied.

—description from the designer
Help Pio get the postage right. You'll need a little luck and, above all, some good math skills to get the job done. The player who can correctly calculate postage for the most mail wins.

description:
ほしあわせ (Star Memory) is a card game from Japan. The cards show highly detailed photographs of constellations. The constellations are from the 12 zodiac signs.

The deck has 36 cards. 12 are reference cards, naming the constellation and giving a description (in Japanese). The remaining 24 cards are pairs of photographs, mostly being massed starfields with a few bright spots.

The game can be played three ways:

ほしあわせ (Hoshi awase) - a memo game, the 24 starfields are shuffled and dealt face-down. Players take turns turning cards up to make pairs.

ほしかるた (Hoshi karuta) - the description cards are read out, and players try to pick the correct starfield card from 12 face-up.

みつぼしあわせ (Mitsu bo shiawase) - a memo game using all three cards.

黄道十二星座の美しい写真で作られたカードで遊ぶ、星座カードゲーム。

全写真カードを裏向きにして神経衰弱として遊ぶ「ほしあわせ」、各星座の写真カード1枚ずつを表向きにして読み手が読み上げた星座を探す「ほしかるた」、説明カードも含めて各星座3枚ずつを使って神経衰弱として遊ぶ「みつぼしあわせ」の3種類の遊び方があります。
user summary
In Happy Little Trees your goal is to be the first player to discard your personal deck of cards by matching and planting trees according to color and shape.

Cowbell is meant to be a gentler introduction to many of the contracts played in the more elaborate, traditional card game, Vira. Most of the same contracts remain (presented in a different form) but some contracts have been added (to make the game a little easier for those just starting out) and some have been removed (for the same reason).

Cowbell is as much about risk-taking as it is about trick-taking.

So, be bold: try to ring the Bell of Fame! and never earn the Cow of Shame...
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Agent Cleo works for the mysterious organisation known as the Ministry of Time... and she’s trapped in Egypt in 1922! This looks like foul play... She’s sent a package through time, desperately hoping it falls into the right hands. Can you save her?

The Egyptian Enigma is an escape room challenge contained entirely in one exciting package. Search through a mysterious series of envelopes, solve the puzzles and outsmart your enemies; all before the timer runs out!

Everything is in the package; documents, clues, secrets and traps that you’ll have to navigate to save the day.
All you’ll need is a device with an internet connection, a pen and paper to make notes, and maybe some friends or family to play with!

This immersive, storytelling experience lasts around an hour. You’ll interact with characters and solve head-scratching challenges in a thrilling historical adventure that takes you all the way back to the time of the Pharaohs.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: はないちもんめ (Hana Ichi Monme)
description:
はないちもんめ (Hana Ichi Monme) is a bluffing card game from Japan for 2 or 3 players. The title means something like 'flower position'.

Each player has a set of seven cards in their own colour. Some cards are Warabe (children) and some are Ayakashi (weird). If a player can give away all their weird cards by the end, they win. Otherwise, points are calculated.

Each card has a shadow half, and a bright half, on both sides of the card. So a player can look at another player's hand and see which cards are the bright side up and shadow side up. Players arrange their own hands at the start of the game, and cannot change this later.

At the start of the game, all the cards in the player's hand are shadow side up.

Players take turns to offer a card to another player, by drawing the card up a little in their hand. The player offered either takes it or declines it, but either way the card is now turned shadow side down, and cannot be offered away. If the card was taken, the player can place it anywhere they like in their hand. If declined, it stays in the same position as before and cannot be moved.

Once every hand is shadow side down, the game ends. If a player has disposed of all their own Ayakashi cards, they win. Collecting Ayakashi cards from other players does not matter.

Otherwise, players score points. +1 pt for Warabe cards, -1 or -2pts for Ayakashi cards. But if a Ayakashi cards is flanked by Warabe cards, it is worth zero. Then, the most points wins.

The rules allow for a 4 player game using two packs. In this case, it is played as a doubles game, with partners discussing their moves.
user summary

The battle between good and evil is ceaseless. It stretches as far back as the beginning of time, and continues to rage in present day. This is where you come in. It's time to pick a side. Will you be a hero, and command the forces of light? Or, are you a villain, determined to lead those who dwell in darkness? The choice is yours.

Of Light and Darkness is a 2-player game in which you get to play as either good (heroes) or evil (villains). Some cards have special abilities. Others don't. Your goal is simply to have the most of your cards on the table, and connected by sharing a side, when all cards have been used.

Falling Down is an autumn season-themed game, but it also involves the element of "gravity." During play, you endeavour to make matches between multiples of cards that contain the same imagery, to collect as many as possible. However, the animals can only be collected when they fall out of the bottom of the grid of cards on the playing surface. It's a game of matching, strategy, and turn taking.

In Escaping Mars, you role play as one of the last remaining citizens of Colony 7, a settlement on a post-terraformed Red Planet (which has been overrun by a ruthless gang known as The Brotherhood of Broken Skulls), in the year 3254. During play, your goal is to get past the baddies, find the "battery unit," power up the last working drone transport, fend off other citizens who have the same idea, and get out while you still can.

In Zòku, you control one of four groups of characters (Preps, Jocks, Nerds, or Misfits), as you try to be the first to assemble a connected group of your peers on the school playground, and push the other cliques out. Each character card has a number that represents its "personality strength" that, during play, determines whether or not it can be used to push other characters around on the playing surface. It's a fun, quirky, adorably schoolyard-themed card game with 68 original characters!

It's the UNO matching card game everyone knows but featuring favorite characters from Schitt's Creek. Players still race to get rid of all their cards by matching color, number and symbol, but every card has either a 'Love' or 'Money' icon on it. When someone plays a special 'Where Everyone Fits In' card, they call out either 'love' or 'money.' Players then reveal their hands and may have to draw cards if they don't have enough of the chosen icon. And just like always, when a player is down to one card, they must shout, 'UNO!' Makes a great gift for card game lovers and Schitt's Creek fans ages 7 years and older. Colors and decorations may vary.

The classic UNO card game builds fun on game night with themes inspired by Schitt's Creek.
Each game features a deck decorated with character graphics from beloved show!
As in classic UNO game, players match colors, numbers and symbols to the card on top of the discard pile!
A special 'Where Everyone Fits In' rule may force some player to draw cards.
Makes a great gift for kid, teen, adult and family game nights with 2 to 10 players ages 7 years and older!

description:
In this game you rule one fish from Emperor of Japan pond.

Each turn you move your fish (Koi) on hexagonal field, eat and collect food and upgrade your tablet.

•••

В этой игре вам предстоит взять под управление одну из рыбок кои в императорском пруду и довести её первой до вознесения дракона. Рассчитывайте свои возможности, чтобы каждый ход совершать с максимальной выгодой и не забывайте следить за игроками, чтобы вовремя помешать им вырвать победу из ваших плавников. В ходе игры вы будете перемещаться по игровому полю, собирать еду для своей рыбки и прокачивать свой планшет, чтобы каждый новых ход становился всё более эффективным.

boardgame: Escape Quest: Pour une Poignée de Néodollars
description:
Escape Quest is a game book in which you must solve puzzles to advance in your adventure. Each page has its clues: combinations, labyrinths, word games, hidden images ... Your logic and your sense of observation will be put to the test!

Los Angeles, 2062. Nuclear war and economic disaster have left all power in the hands of massive corporations  You are just a young, self-taught hacker making ends meet in the sprawl, until one night you receive a cryptic message from a mysterious sender: “Join us”. You are then suddenly swept into a race for your own survival, and perhaps that of all the other users on the network ! Sides become blurry; which lines will you cross ? Is the ultimate hacking code worth your life, or just a few neodollars more?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Escape Quest: Au delà du Virtuel
description:
Escape Quest is a game book in which you must solve puzzles to advance in your adventure. Each page has its clues: combinations, labyrinths, word games, hidden images ...

Your logic and your sense of observation will be put to the test!

Los Angeles, 2101. ASTRAL, the largest VR online gaming platform in the world is run by Demosthenes, a true AI, and something went terribly wrong. Millions of users suddenly dropped into a virtual coma, and nobody knows why. It falls on you to understand what happened and save the countless lives at stake. Confront the virtual worlds designed by the AI, for their many deadly challenges hold the key to solving this not-so-virtual mystery !

Immerse yourself in the world of new boardgame Hnefetafl: The Viking Tales and lead your troops to victory in a strategic game for up to four players! Play as a various characters from the Viking history and mythology while competing with your friends in various scenarios.

The game of Hnefatafl, often translated as "Kings' Table" began its life in Scandinavia over 1600 years ago. In the traditional version, one player is a king with troops who are caught in an ambush by the forces of the second player.

Hnefetafl: The Viking Tales has been expanded to include a 4-player version, while preserving simple rules that guarantee long hours of fun with countless gameplay possibilities. Each game includes a variety of scenarios with multiple goals to achieve. The stories are presented as Sagas in which you will play as famous Vikings and Gods from Scandinavian mythology.

The first official expansion of Ahau: Rulers of Yucatáns adds 14 Eclipse cards, 6 additional role cards, 5 building tiles and 10 special pyramid tiles, to enhance your Ahau experience. The Eclipse event cards provide more opportunities and challenges for scoring, resource conversion, summoning, and strategic gameplay in each round.

Eclipse introduces us to the history-shaping, cataclysmic and mythical events of the Classic Period.

—description from the publisher

Do you want to sow conspiracy or stop it in its tracks? Lizards and Lies is a 2v2 (or 1v1) battler that lets you play with the push and pull of conspiracy theory moving through social media. Play as one of four characters and use unique abilities and cards to influence the network. Platform Moderators and Educators face off against Edgelords and Conspiracy theorists in this asymmetrical board brawler. Will you convince the world that lizards are taking over, or that birds are surveilling society? Or will you shut down these lies?

Lizards and Lies is designed as a serious game meant to reflect the movement of disinformed content (like conspiracy) through social media.

—description from the publisher

description:
52 BC. The Gallic tribes led by Vercingetorix were defeated by the army of Gaius Julius Caesar near Dijon and retreat to the city of Alesia, convinced to find in this fortress a safe position and the time necessary to regroup the troops and recover morale.

Julius Caesar, sensing the intentions of the enemy, also takes advantage of the particular isolated position of the city, planning one of the most ingenious sieges in history. He built two rings of fortifications, one external and one internal, to defend himself at the same time from attacks from the city and from the tribes who came to the aid of the besieged. The walls were fortified with towers, ditches and traps and sanctioned the victory of the Romans over the Gauls.

In The Walls of Alesia each of the two players takes the role of a Roman Legatus, commissioned by Caesar to supervise the construction of one of the two walls. Each one will have to take care of creating the most effective fortifications, hiring the necessary workers and deciding for each stretch whether to obtain immediate advantages from the palisades or to erect towers that will guarantee future advantages.

There will be a high competition in this constructors' challenge, but during the match there will also be two coordinated assaults by the Gauls, in which the Legates will have to cooperate to limit the damage. At the end of the second assault, it will be verified who has obtained the most prestige in the eyes of Julius Caesar.

—description from the publisher

TOKKA Race is a dexterity game where wooden pieces are used to construct the boundaries of a race track. The players use finger-flicks to send small pucks around the track, a la Carrom.
During the race players can unlock one-use special abilities, e.g. when another puck is moved outside the game boundaries, which give them an extra move or allows them to try to flick another player outside of the game boundaries.
The race lasts five rounds and whoever crosses the finish line first is the winner.

DICE PARK: Dungeons of Doom is a roll&write for 1 to 100 players ages 8 and up.

During 30-45 minutes games, players will have to use their dice to create the most frightening dungeon.

Roll your dice (5 in total) and use them to create a path in your Dungeon, place creatures and score the most points to become THE Dungeon Master!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the universe of ExoTerra, a sandbox strategy game with tactical planning, deep customization, and enemies who are complex and highly challenging.

ExoTerra is played cooperatively over the course of many sorties in a campaign. Each Player takes on the role of a Pilot in charge of requisitioning equipment, personnel, supplies, and, most importantly, Jackets – massive humanoid weapon systems, to be customized and fielded in battle.

Leveraging a new system of enemy generation, battles should always feel different, even if facing the same enemies.  This is accomplished through several factors of enemy customization that force players to assess each situation individually.  You can rely on your experience from past battles, but must be able to change your strategy on the fly based on the variables of the mission.

—description from the designer

Be the first to grab the correct gnome!

A round begins by one player rolling the three dice. Each die represents either a shirt colour, a garden tool or a stylish accessory. Using the provided grabbers, players try and be the first to grab the gnome that matches all three categories rolled.

The first player to grab the correct matching gnome wins the round. Be the first player to grab 5 gnomes and win the game!

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes: Der Tod des Hutmachers
description:
Escape Card Game for up to 4 players

London, 1891.
The highly respected hatter Mr. Kenworthy is found dead. His store shows no signs of burglary, his body no sign of violence. What happened and who is behind the crime?

In this exciting Escape Room game you slip into the role of master detective Sherlock Holmes to solve the case of the dead hatter. Only if you show a sharp mind, ingenuity and a gift for deduction, you can find the murderer and solve the ominous background of this crime.

—description from the publisher (translated)

London, 1891.
Der hoch angesehene Hutmacher Mr. Kenworthy wird tot aufgefunden. Sein Laden zeigt keine Einbruchsspuren, seine Leiche keinerlei Gewalteinwirkung. Was ist passiert und wer steckt hinter der Tat?

In diesem spannenden Escape-Room-Spiel schlüpft ihr in die Rolle von Meisterdetektiv Sherlock Holmes, um den Fall des toten Hutmachers zu lösen. Nur wenn ihr einen scharfen Verstand, Einfallsreichtum und Kombinationsgabe beweist, könnt ihr den Mörder Stellen und die ominösen Hintergründe dieses Verbrechens aufklären.

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Into The Echoside: The Shaggy Show
description:
A single promotional card only available from the Dark Carnival Games Con in 2018.

From the card's announcement on Facebook: "IT’S SHAGGGGYYY TIMMMMEEE! World Premiere: We present to you: THE SHAGGY SHOW promo card!!! DCG Con, May 11-13th, in Denver, is the ONLY place you can cop the Oracle of the Three Rings expansion for Into the Echoside, featuring the exclusive Shaggy Show Promo Card! You’ll get this exclusive card FREE with your purchase of Oracle…ONLY at the Con. Only at the DCG Con. For Juggalos, By Juggalos!"

boardgame: Into The Echoside: Promo Card Set
description:
A set of three promotional cards, one from each faction, for Into The Echoside. They were all included in a single pack available for those who preordered Into The Echoside: Oracle of the Three Rings.

The three cards were Inner City Posse, Wizard of the Hood, and Blahzay Rose.

From the designers of Corruptia, came a new game for 2 players!

Welcome to Corruptia: Second Round, an asymmetrical card game where a business man and a candidate try to obtain the major quantity of personal benefits during the second round of the presidential elections.

As the benefactor, you will try to buy the presidential candidate offering different agendas for your economical convenience in exchange to finance their campaign.

As the candidate, you will try to gain resources for your campaign taken economic agendas that doesn´t affect you too much on your personal reputation with your voters.

After 8 turns of negotiations, the player who obtain the major benefits will win the game.

Any resemblance with reality is only coincidence...
