microCrime is a Roll-and-Write investigation game. You are the detective. And you need to solve the case. It features intriguing game mechanics, ever changing scenarios, a detailed Rulebook and tables.

The game starts soon after the Chief Inspector ends writing the premises of the case on the Case File by rolling dice several times and writing down the results took from the rulebook's tables. At this point, all the players try to solve the case, building up a story which should include and bring to light all the elements, especially the missing ones, of the crimes scene, getting to a final solution, in order to close the case.

Discussion and interpretation are fundamental, during this part of the game. Only with discussion, deduction and compromises they'll be able to meet a point where everyone is satisfied with the story. Detectives discuss their ideas and perceptions of the crime scene in its whole and try to figure out a possible solution, finding a meeting point that could explain the murder. Investigation can vary in duration, based on the ability of the detectives to deal with the fundamental six questions every detective is asked to answer to close any case: How? What? When? Where? Why? Who?
While the investigation goes on, detectives should try to enrich their reconstruction of the case with more and more detailed elements.

When all involved players are pleased with the reconstruction they inform the Chief Inspector which writes down the story, closing the investigation, solving the case and, ultimately, ending the game.

-description from designer

boardgame: 茶の間のきまぐれねこさま (As the Cat Says)
description:
A family-friendly party card game with simple rules & reversal elements.

Here we are is cat café which is topic of the street. You are the staff of this shop. Cats are relaxing around. When the time goes by slowly, one of the staff whispered, "Who is most liked by them?"

You do everything to the cats. Of course, to be the most favorite of the cats in this shop.

--from the publisher

Each round, the staff of this cat café must try and guess what the cat is going to want from them. You'll know which of the four cat decks is going to be drawn from next, and if you've been paying attention, you should have an idea of the cards left in that deck. You'll play one of your four prediction cards, and either gain or lose the cat's favorability.

But be careful which prediction card you play, because you only have one of each! You can play your 'Leave Me Alone' card to avoid losing favorability, and to return all your cards back to your hand.

You also have a 'Full Power' card you can use when you're really confident in your prediction. If you guessed right, you can get a favorability bonus - either +5, +15, x3, or even x4 - depending on where you are on the scoring track. But the 'Full Power' card has to rest for a while before it can be used again, so choose wisely!

The staff member who the cats like best wins!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Toxicimitar Badar
description:
A renewed version of the Badar Promotional Expansion, Badar is now an Indomitable Survivor.

Toxicimatar - Badar is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Toxicimitar Badar hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Solenoglyphous Fang Spidicules indomitable resource
1 x Toxicimitar pattern card
1 x Toxicimitar pattern gear card
1 x Install guide card
1 x Large matte art print

Badar raced among the wobbling projections that held the monster aloft somewhere in the darkness overhead. They worked in pairs, hacking and slicing the chitinous stalks, not pausing to watch them crumble under their monstrous weight. Their heavy hopping steps took aim at the skittering brood underfoot.

The torso landed with a venomous splash before him. He raced at its snarling face, relentlessly boring into the hissing, wriggling creature, aiming for its multitudes of eyes. A keening wail arose from the rocking mass, its horrid grinning mouth grew impossibly wide. A creak of osseous hinge alarmed him. He dove out of the way as the hissing fang pierced the ground where he stood moments ago. He dove, wrestling the caustic bone from the monster's open maw. With a final crack, the monster's expression slackened.

Then the dreams overtook me. I conversed with the thousand poets long dead, they who trod the roads weaving toward eternity and never returned. In the morning’s warm embrace, my limbs felt their old vigor and the undeniable desire to travel. To seize the horizon. To behold hidden wonders. To exhume the words that will give life to my LAST HAIKU.

Last Haiku is a one-player adventure card game with a dose of creative writing. You are the latest in a long line of poets feeling your age, and you know you are not long for this world. Yet you also feel the call of distant, uncanny, and wondrous places where you might seek the inspiration to write one last haiku to leave to the ages to inspire future generations of poets, who may themselves discover stranger, more distant places to spark the words of their own haiku.

This is a “Make-as-You-Play” game, which means that the game starts with a few index cards and a pen. Each game will add new cards, either maps of locations or haiku your poet composed. The rules provide all the guidance you need for creating new cards and selecting the game’s thematic elements, which makes every copy of Last Haiku an evolving, unique world. In addition, it means that each game inherits all components added or changed in the previous games. There is no limit to the places you can visit and the poetry you can compose.

Each turn of the game involves playing cards of the appropriate distance. If the card was created in a previous game, your poet travels deeper into the frontier. If you cannot play an existing card, you create a new site on a card and your poet is inspired to write a line of haiku, these lines recorded on a separate card. The game ends when you have written each of three lines forming your poet’s last haiku, and your score equals the total of the distances of the new sites visited. Each new site and your haiku then enter the deck permanently.

-description from designer

The Ultimate Board Game is an exciting, competitive, fun game for 2 to 8 adults. The game is inspired by Ultimate and reproduces the dynamics of a tournament between two teams, consisting of a Match, a Party and a Final Match. To become The Ultimate Champion Team, collect the most victory points from the three stages of the game.

—description from the publisher

In Metro Domino, you use domino tiles to build metro lines across the city.

Each player starts with nine dominoes from a 9x9 set, and one random domino is placed in the central starting station. On a turn, you can draw a domino from the boneyard (i.e., the supply of dominoes) or place a domino at the end of an active line as long as you can match the number at the end of the line; the starting station can have two lines built off of the starting domino. If you place a double domino, take another turn immediately.

The starting station indicates that five dominoes must be placed in a line before a new station is opened. If you place the fifth domino in a line, draw a random station from the deck, then place it at the end of the line and mark it with one of your tokens. Each station has two lines off of it, with a starting domino half on each line and a length the line must reach before a new station is built on that line.

Players continue to take turns until either one player has emptied their hand of dominoes or the fifth station has been put into play. At the time, players score, earning 1 point for each station they started and losing 1 point for each domino still in hand.

Each city edition of Metro Domino appears to have identical gameplay, but with differing station lengths and values.

In the spring of 1942, the Japanese contemplated invading the British-held island of Ceylon. From there they could strike many strategic targets around the Indian Ocean basin, none of these more vital than the southern outlet of the Red Sea, controlling the sea route to the Suez Canal and on to Europe. Far more important to the Axis cause than Midway Island, Japanese control of the chokepoint at the Strait of Bab-el-Mandeb would have broken British communications with India, Australia and New Zealand except for the lengthy and difficult route around the Cape of Good Hope, and made the entire campaign in North Africa superfluous.

Eastern Fleet: Gulf of Aden is a Campaign Study in the same format as Midway: Rising Sun 1940 or Coral Sea: Defending Australia, with 14 scenarios depicting a Japanese campaign in Gulf of Aden and Arabian Sea opposed by the British Eastern Fleet, heavily reinforced to defend this vital maritime crossroads. To play these scenarios, you’ll need Eastern Fleet, Horn of Africa, Midway Deluxe and Bismarck.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
You are the great dragon slayer and have found the beast. It is cornered and now it is up to you to deliver the final blow.
Unfortunately for you the beast doesn't want to surrender and puts up a fight for its life.

Each turn you will roll your dice, assign damage to the dragon and prepare your defenses. Then the dragon will attack back, reacting with a determinable but variable sequence of actions.
The fight end when one of the opponents is slain.

—description from the designer

In the land of eagle hunters and herbalists, you represent a nomadic tribe. Face the tasks of the Great Shaman. You will be accompanied by your eagle and a power animal, your moose. The journey lasts 17 rounds, during which you can choose to explore landscapes or play cards to move your equestrian figure. En route, you'll need to use your strength well, as the landscape becomes more and more spacious. When you find fountains and reach them with your figure, you can fill up your water supply and extend the range of your cards. There are also some special actions, such as "deploy water." A highlight of the game are the power places with their mighty stone pines, which rise like peaks from a sea of fog, sink and reappear at another place ... Whoever gains the highest reputation wins.

The basic game is intended for beginners, which can be expanded to an expert game, including a solo variant. Three modules await you, with additional power animals (wolf, bear and snow leopard) and much more. A campaign combine all modules to the "Golden Journey."

-description from designer

The year is 10,000 bc, take control and start your nation. Germany, Great Britain, France, the USA, and the USSR. 2-5 players lead their nations from the ancient era to victory! Explore different routes on the techtree. As you discover stronger and deadlier weapons, your enemies will coward in your power. If destruction isn't your cup of tea you can construct a rocket and leave your competitors in your dust. Trade, diplomacy, and foreign relations have never been more important as you progress and gain potential allies.

This game was created and is owned by S&J games

Wave 7 Expansion for Aeon's End content revealed in the Past and Future kickstarter campaign. Includes:


     1 New mage mat and deck.
     1 Pet Phoenix sideboard and deck.
     4 New market cards.
     1 New nemesis.

Jok dice game:
Each player gets 1 meeple and starts at space 1 of the section of his color. The Players take turns to roll and move. On the roll of a 6 the player gets an extra roll. If a meeple gets on a prime number (framed spaces), the gets a "Jok"-token (joker). Each player may only get two tokens per game. The tokens may be used any time in the players turn to move the meeple up to 6 spaces forward. If a player already has 2 tokens or chooses not to tame one, he may move his meeple equal to the checksum of the prime number it occupies. 
Whenever a meeple gets on a number that can be divided by 11 (black spaces), it gets set back by the product of both digits. The 11 only counts as a prime number.
The first player to exactly reach space 100 wins the game.

Knight's move:
Each player gets 4 meeples which start at spaces 1, 2, 18 and 19. The meeples are moved in turns according to the knight's move in chess. All numbers that can be divided by 11 (black spaces) may not be occupied. The first player to get all his 4 meeples on space 100 wins the game.

—user summary

Each player gets 15 pawns (cups or packs). Starting positions are the first 3 rows above the red stars. The cups are placed on the pink spaces with the corn on one side and the packs on the blue spaces with the cups on the other side of the board.
The goal of the game is to get 6 pieces on the red stars on the opposite side of the game board.

Movement is 1 space per turn diagonally forward. Orthogonal movement is only allowed to reach a red star. Opponent pieces are taken by jumping over them to an empty space behind them. Pieces on a space with a letter are safe and may not be jumped over.

—user summary

Secret Cardinals is a ‘take that’ player elimination game of Vice, Virtues, and hidden Roles within Roles. Eliminate political opponents and survive the power struggle from within the 15th century church through deception, sabotage, and calculated choices. For each turn, clever players will need to strategize their actions wisely to deduce who their political rivals are, acquire gold for its power, or gamble their advantage on the role of the dice. Use gold to purchase indulgences, gain virtues by giving charity, or pay a bribe to find rivals guilty of vice. Each turn brings unexpected dangers and new ways to win.

Rome, 1418. The Papal standoff that had split the church for decades has just concluded, but tensions still linger from when three rival popes simultaneously claimed to be the one true pope. Each claimant was backed by a competing power of Europe: Rome, Pisan, and Avignon. Driven by politics rather than theological disagreement, the church was thrown into turmoil. To find a compromise, a fourth Pope was chosen to succeed as the legitimate head of the church. As a result, the other three claimants were deposed, jailed for heresy, or excommunicated for their disobedience. In order to maintain a delicate network of alliances among the ruling powers, the new Pope has secretly named his choice for a new Cardinal from among these powers to honor this player’s service while keeping them safe from the political machinations of the competing Kingdoms.

Adversaries with hidden agendas and hidden roles are looking to eliminate their opponents and win the game. An upstart with an agenda wants to be acknowledged as the Secret Cardinal and is looking to eliminate the competition. A rival Roman Cardinal is not keen to recognize the influence of the new Pope and the fanaticism of their Devoted Follower creates a dangerous situation for anyone trying to upset the status quo. No one is safe from the Heretic infiltrators, as they work to limit the growing influence of the church, even as the are being hunted by the an overzealous Inquisitor who is determined to root out anyone suspected of heresy. Lastly, interlopers associated with the neighboring monarchy have not forgotten their candidate for Pope was excommunicated and have sent a Royal Spy from Avignon to cause trouble.

Players will see similarities to Bang and One Night Ultimate Werewolf, with unexpected action cards and new ways to win with roles within roles.

—description from the designer

"Battle Array" is a set of 'fast play' wargame rules intended to simulate major battles from the ancient period until about 1500. It is played with miniatures or counters on a table top or board consisting of squares to regulate movement and distances and to speed up play by avoiding interaction and measurement issues. The system places less emphasis on weapons and more on training, quality and morale. Several historical battle scenarios are provided but, as usual in table top gaming players can also select their own armies and play 'random' battles such as Sumerian v 100 Years War French. The game is intended for experienced players and assumes that they know how to collect, paint and base figures and provide their own playing equipment such as dice, terrain and so on.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Shining Shadows Saga is a strategy-based trading card game inspired by Japanese Role-Playing Games (JRPG). Using a customized deck of skills, spells, will, and other actionable cards, players battle in an arena-like fashion to knock out each others champions and become victorious.

Based on the recommendations of the Seer of Sanctuary, Azara, Monarchs can choose up to 3 ancient champions to take into battle. These champions from the past all hail from different realms within the world of Limsia where they once were revered by their respective species. Players take turns controlling their champions and use actionable cards that can cool down to be used again and the first player to deplete the HP of all of their opponents' champions is the winner.

As this is a strategy-heavy game, players can best strategize gameplay by using weapons and potions from their inventory to influence survivability and any attacks that are relevant to their champions of choice. New cards are released monthly so players can build as many unique and versatile decks as possible to vary their gameplay and avoid being locked in to specific play styles. With the number of champions growing, every battle provides a new ground for players of all skill levels to show off their customizations and strategies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Revengineers!: The Reverse Engineering Party Dice Game
description:
Revengineers! is a 3 to 10-player party game.

You and your fellow Revengineers are in a race to reverse engineer computer “viruses”, so that the Cybercrime Division can trace them to their source and catch the hackers in their hideouts.

Each player takes it in turn to become a “virus”, and draws 1 to 3 Program Cards. The other players use dice rolls as inputs for that virus program. The Virus Player looks at the dice rolled, and speaks the output, as described by their Program Cards. This continues until one of the Revengineers successfully determines what the Program Cards say.

With increasing levels of difficulty, this game is exciting for adults and kids alike. If you can read, and do basic arithmetic, you can play this game.

—description from the designer

The second installment of the Scattered Nexus card game, a battle-style card game based on indie comics. Using shuffle building you make a team of three comic factions to defeat your opponent. This set expands player options by featuring 6 new comics: Aurora, Edge, Next Reaper, Little Warrior, and Plume.

Each player chooses his goal. Either he tries to get rid of all his cards or to collect two sets of cards from 1 to 6.

At the beginning each player is dealt 8 number cards and 2 "Ich will nicht (I don't want to)"-cards. The remaining "Ich will nicht"-cards are laid out in the middle of the table. The "ich will nicht"-cards are used as dummies when cards are exchanged to fool the 3rd player.

Each player has the following options to play his turn:


     The player rolls three dice. The result determines which  cards he may give to his left neighbor. He may choose as many cards as he wants as long as their combined value does not exceed the result of his roll.
     The player declares "Ich will nicht" and does nothing.
     The player takes 1 or 2 "Ich will nicht" cards from the Table
     The players places 1 or 2 "Ich will nicht" cards from his hand on the table.
     The player calls a number (1-6) or "Ich will nicht" cards. His left neighbor must show his hand. If he has more than 2 of these cards, he has to give one or both of the surplus cards to the active player and has to pay 5 coins for hoarding cards. In the other case the active player has to pay 2 coins for falsely accusing the other player.


—user summary

boardgame: Human Punishment: Social Deduction 2.0 – The Beginning Promo Cards
description:
7 additional ID cards added as a bonus to Human Punishment: The Beginning. They represent the characters from the prequel and have unique abilities.

Human: Dryden, Jayden
Machine: Feykah, Havoc
Outlaw: Nova, Dee Coy, Kuro

"Join Pikachu and all his friends when you play this Pokemon edition of UNO! Master the game with the special 'Snorlax Traffic' and 'Battle Bond' rules! And when you're down to your last card, don't yell 'pika pika', yell UNO!"

UNO variation where all of the cards have a pokemon, including some of the original 150 and newer pokemon from Sword & Shield.

The deck is the same as normal UNO, but with 4 extra wild cards:

- Wild Snorlax Traffic Cards: you choose a colour, the next player draws 3 cards and then discard all cards of your chosen colour from their hand (their turn ends).
- Wild Battle Bond Card: you choose a colour and the next player draws a card. If their drawn card does not match the chosen colour, they have to draw another card. You can then choose the colour that continues play.

You can choose to play a normal game of UNO without these extra cards, or keep them in for something a bit different.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
In Arixia: War of Essence, players take on the role of a god/goddess. Select one of five thematic and asymmetric factions consisting of multiple tiered units including: common, elite, specialist, general, and a mighty avatar unit class. Throughout the game, players will destroy each others’ units and accumulate their “essence” as well as fight over control points, known as Essence Wells. Harnessed essence is used to activate unique, faction-specific abilities. 
With enough essence, your god will be able to manifest in the physical realm in the form of a massive avatar. Empowered avatars gain access to once-per-game Act of God abilities to swing the tide of battle in your favor. If you destroy the enemy’s avatar, you are victorious!

Gameplay is turn-based and consists of several phases. First, each player rolls an Essence Storm die to determine which way the Essence Storm will blow and which variable effect it will take on during that game round. Next, player one will take their turn. During a player turn consists of two phases, the movement phase and the activation phase. The movement phase allows your entire army to move up to their maximum speed stat. The activation phase allows up to two units to attack and use the active special abilities of those units. Thus, interesting situations arise where some units get into position for future turns, contest Essence Wells from opponents, or keep your options open on the fly.

However, because only two of your units can attack each turn, you must carefully plan not only your current turn, but think about how future turns might play out. Players roll unique-faced dice to simplify gameplay and avoid having to memorize combat tables. Additionally, most powerful units have access to faction-specific Divine Dice, providing a boost to damage, defense or annoying tricks. Additionally, players have the option to complete their turn phases in either order they wish, opening up further tactical options as needed! After players have taken their turns, a new game round begins, repeating this process until one side defeats the enemy avatar!

-description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Librarians: Quest for the Spear
description:
Where the core set is focused on the first season of the TV show, this expansion is focused on the first movie. It contains 3 new adventures that collectively tell the story of the movie. You get to explore the Amazon jungle, hike the Himalaya mountains, and break into the Metropolitan Museum. Each adventure includes fun new mechanical twists on the rules found in the core set. These adventures end up being very fun to play and extremely thematic, but they are also a bit more complicated than those found in the core box.

The expansion also includes 2 playable heroes complete with suggested starting decks. These include Nicole Noone and Flynn Carsen, the character who started the franchise.
family: Game: The Librarians Adventure Card Game

description:
Jenkins is now a playable hero! Complete with a suggested starting deck of all new cards ready to play out of the box. For those looking forward to building your own decks, the expansion has 3 new generic disciplines (groups of 7 cards) that can be included in any hero's deck, so it really opens up the deck building possibilities for all your heroes!

Jenkins is a kickstarter exclusive expansion and will not be available at retail.
family: Game: The Librarians Adventure Card Game

boardgame: Duo Purple (fan expansion for Mega Man Pixel Tactics)
description:
The Duo Purple Box is a fan expansion collection of every Robot Master from the Mega Man Classic series that did not get a color 8-bit release. The expansion features 8-bit sprites from various fan games, and abilities created by users on Board Game Geek.

The 40 cards can combine with the Kickstarter Rewards pack and the Stardroids Pack to create a 4th and 5th full-sized deck.

The Robot Masters are from the following games:

    Mega Man 7 (SNES)
    Mega Man 8 (Playstation 1)
    Mega Man and Bass (SNES)
    Mega Man and Bass 2 (Wonderswan)
    Mega Man the Wily Wars (Genesis)
    Mega Man Powered Up (PSP)
    And a few other surprises

Pavé is an original kiwi strategy game.

A two player path building game played across a grid that is deceptively simple, emergently complex, and ruthlessly competitive, Pavé is a timeless game for all ages.

Pavé is a very simple game to play and understand. It has fewer rules than you expect.

To start the game each player grabs 5 Pavé tokens from the bag
Your goal is to use the paths on the tokens to connect your 2 board edges while your opponent is trying to connect the other 2 edges.

You alternate playing tokens, and top up after each turn.
Tokens may also be stacked to redirect the path.

The first player with a completed path to call "Pave" wins the round.

The core game concepts are:

Randomization
    Hidden Information
    Connections
    A Dynamic Board State

Pavé tests the player’s skills of

Pattern Recognition
    Spatial awareness
    Strategy and Planning
    Calculating Probability
    Bluffing and reading bluffs

A board game based on the children's books by Pedro Mañas.

Princesas Dragon  is a interaction card game where you need to collect 3 different princesses to get one dragon. The first player in get four dragon will be the winner.

You play with three cards in your hand and you must take decision about three different action (use action card, put princess in the table, discard one card and draw a new card).

—user summary

boardgame: The Quest for El Dorado: Dangers & Muisca
description: New adventures in The Quest for El Dorado! This expansion introduces the new modules “Dangers” and “Muisca”. The Muisca is a mysterious tribe that wants to help you find the city of gold. “Dangers” brings new challenges such as crocodiles, rapids and temples with keys to the City of Gold. Combine with new cave tokens for even more variety!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Game: The Quest for El Dorado

description:
Juduku Explosive Revelations contains 480 new cards to reveal your friends' and family's worst thoughts, as well as Sudden Death and Mime cards to spice up your evenings even more: that means more spicy answers and more hilarious situations.

This new opus of Juduku comprises 4 decks to renew the pleasure:

    the quiet "Pépouze"
    the terrible "Confessions"
    the famous "Girl's Night"
    the spicy "X Rated"


The rules remain similar, the players have to answer in only 8 seconds to questions like:

    Name 3 dishes that you often cook but that Philippe Etchebest would spit in your face!
    Name 3 ways to get a drink!
    Name 3 things you prepare before a date!


—Description from the publisher.

Merchants Cove: Master Craft is a big-box expansion for Merchants Cove.

Contained in Merchants Cove: Master Craft is a new expansion (The Faction Festival), new modular content (new Rogues, Townsfolk), as well as four new expert Merchants.

In addition to the new Merchants, the Faction Festival also expands the game in a substantial way. A new board is connected to the bottom of the main board adding tracks for each of the Factions, and brand new mechanisms for each Faction to use. The Wizards will enchant your goods to make them worth more - while the Rogues will help you frame the other players, letting you get rid of corruption by pinning it on your opponents.

There's a new Faction Hall (The Paladins), new Adventurers (Paladins), new Townsfolk, Rogues, and more.

-description from publisher

The Three Little Pigs is a fantastic building game.
Build the houses of the three little pigs like in the fairy tale! Roll the dice to collect all the pieces of the wooden house, the brick house and the straw house. Thanks to the interlocking puzzle, learn to recognize colors and shapes and ... Always be careful of the wolf!

Each player has to build a set of houses consisting of a thatched house, one of wood and one of bricks.

It is played in turns, clockwise. If you are the smallest, you will be the first to play! The goal is to build first all the houses.

On your turn, after sorting the house tiles, roll the die and see what happens!
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
Strange noises come from the house!

Legend has it that on the longest night of the year, in the haunted Whippole Manor, the terrible Ghost Buu and his henchmen wake up and go around the city to scare the inhabitants. Only a brave hero can stop them, finding the only thing inside the house that calms Buu and makes him return to his age-old rest ...

… His ghost teddy bear!

The haunted house is a very simple game, inside the box you will find some tiles that will be used to represent the rooms and corridors of the house.

After placing the tiles to represent a haunted house, place all the characters on the starting tile the last one who was in a haunted house starts playing! Roll the die, make your move and flip the arrival tile, performing the action shown on it.
The objective of the game is to find the tile containing the ghost bear and bring it back to him to win the game.

HALVES is a word game for the ruthless!

At home, the office, band practice, in the bar or on a plane (where this game was conceived!) bring your friends together and find out who is the wordiest of all.  
Be careful though, winning cards is the easy bit, staying in the lead is where things get complicated.

To win the game, simply Guess the word on your opponent’s card to win points. Be the player with the most points at the end of the game!

The active player must choose a word from one card in their hand for their opponents to try and guess. The colour behind the chosen word dictates the game type for the round:
- Yellow is CATEGORIES, 
- Blue is RHYMES WITH,
- Red is PERFECT PAIRS.

The active player must think of a clue for their word. Then they announce the game type and their clue to all players who have 30 seconds to guess it.
Any cards a player has won from a previous round is at risk from TRAP cards.

Guess, Swap, Bluff or Steal your way to put points on the table and be the envy of all your friends and family!

Created by Carter & Richardson the same minds that make up your favourite punk rock and roll band The Rattlesnakes.

Whether it's the intimidating vulture or the imitative mockingbird, most birds still pair off when it's time to build a nest.

A 6-card expansion for Forest Sky, featuring 3 2-card sets that can be added individually or in any combination: Mockingbirds, Vultures, and Nests.


A delightful expansion that can be added in its entirety, or just mixing in some of the pairs and not others with the base game to change up the experience. Vultures are nice, since they score either cards from the discard or from the forest which helps pivot when that card you really wanted for your hand ends up locked down elsewhere. Mockingbirds provide wild abilities to let them count as another bird type or value. And Nests score bonus points if you have exactly two birds of the same type in your chosen forest. These are fun, solid additions to the game and exactly what you want to see from an expansion.

Each player gets 20 playing pieces (cardboard tokens) to represent different army units: 
1 commander, 1 occupying force, 1 bulwark, 2 cavalry, 3 warplanes, 3 artillery, 3 tanks and 6 infantry.
The game board is a square grid with buildings and lakes as obstacles. Only the planes may move over the obstacles. each kind of unit has it's own way to move or to capture enemy units.
A unit is captured if an enemy unit gets on the same space.

The goal of the game is to capture the enemy commander or occupy the two corner spaces (marked with a star) in enemy territory.

The game was published during WW II.

description:
A retro dungeon crawling board game for 2-5 players. Play as one of four heroes or even as the villain!​

As one of the heroes, you will make your way through the catacombs, working together to fend off dangerous creatures and traps as you search for your Wymer Stone. You must use your stone to defeat the Cursed Dwarves and save the kingdoms of Overwyrld from the evil Wymer.

As the villain, you set traps and locks for the heroes to battle. Also, you will control the Cursed Dwarves as you attempt to defeat the heroes and doom Overwyrld to destruction by the Wymer.

DIFFERENT OUTCOMES
Encounters have two outcomes. If the hero wins, the thumbs up side of the card is read. If the hero loses, the thumbs down portion is read. Even the game itself has outcomes based on if the heroes are victorious or not.

SIMPLE SCORING
Encounters are decided by one die for the hero and one die for the villain. They roll against each other, and any bonuses are added to the roll to see who wins the encounter. The math never gets bigger than adding up to twelve, which is good, because math is terrible.

THE BOARD GAME WE ALWAYS WANTED TO PLAY
When we designed this game, we made it for players like us. We wanted it to be easy to learn, simple to play and challenging enough that we would want to play again.

With a lot of effort and fine-tuning, we arrived at a game that we would have loved if it had existed when we were 14. Does that mean you'll love it? We hope so. If you're looking for a complicated, intricate and punishing game, this probably isn't the game you're looking for. If you want a retro roll to resolve adventure with a compelling story that is simple to learn and play, this is the game for you!

—description from the designer

The image of the friar-knight has in its way shaped the history of the Palmela castle throughout the centuries. The monks who inhabited said castle lived periods in which a call-to-arms would make knights out of them. In this game, this duality is present in the split value of cards and the twofold way they can be played: either facing up or down.

Each round, players will play character cards. Simultaneously, they will try to guess the sum of the score of all currently played character cards. The goal is to find out whether the friar’s value or the knight’s value is equal or greater than the history value of the castle card being contested in the current round.

The game is over when the 12 rounds are finished and the players count the points in the Castles cards. The winner is the player with the most victory points.

description:
Bull Run is a tense Military Strategy Board Game where players lead new, green armies in this first major battle of the American Civil War.

Union general McDowell developed a brilliant plan to launch a surprise flanking attack. Confederate general Beauregard was able to reinforce with Johnston’s army from the valley by rail. Many expected this to be the only battle needed to put down the rebellion. Instead, panic gripped both armies of green troops as they closed for combat. This quickly escalated into a full on rout all the way back to Washington, becoming known as “The Great Skedaddle”.

• Huge map (38” x 24”) covers the extended area, so you can explore many different possible strategies to the battle. 
• This map also covers the area fought over in Second Bull Run, to allow for a Add-On, expansion scenario. 
• Hardwood, Kriegsspiel style blocks for Fog-of-War.
• Fast play time. 
• Tremendous Replayability. 
• Excellent for Solitaire play.

Instead of giving players complete control of their units and perfect intell, this system focuses on realistic command and logistical limitations.  In this chaotic and uncertain environment, you must learn to adapt your plans to accomplish your objectives.

This highly acclaimed rules system is based on real combat data, officer experience and training principles from the era:  Kriegsspiel.  It is used to train officers in the US military and in military academies around the world.

Yvonne Sparrowsmith is the developer.

150 cards with hilarious prompts that will make you laugh! On your turn, do what the card says and don't hold back. Can you keep a straight face? Keep a straight face or bottoms up!

HOW TO PLAY


     The funniest person draws a card first. They are now the Joker. The Joker silently reads the card to themselves.
     The Joker then tells the group who they are going to target. The Target is the person they are trying to get to laugh.
     The Joker now has 30 seconds to do whatever is on the card, doing their best to make the Target laugh. Everyone else (including the Joker) is allowed to laugh.
     If the Target laughs, they drink and the Joker keeps the card and gets a point. If the Target doesn’t laugh, the Joker drinks and the Target keeps the card and gets a point.
     The person to the Joker’s left now becomes the new Joker. The new Joker draws a new card and picks a new Target. The same person can’t be targeted twice in a row!
     First to 7 points wins.


Note: The Joker can always pass on their turn if they don’t want to do what’s on the card. They should take a sip of shame if they choose to pass.

description:
Over the course of ten battles, The Grass Crown charted the military evolution of the Roman Republic from the days of the manipular Legion and its Triplex Acies formation to the veteran professionals that fought in a series of bloody civil wars. These battles were carefully chosen by designer Amabel Holland with an eye toward only keeping those that would propel the game's central themes forward. Some otherwise compelling, engaging, and interesting battles didn't make that list, either because they made the same "point" as a battle that did make the cut, or they introduced a level of complexity that detracted from the narrative.

At the beginning of 1793, after the execution of King Louis XVI, France was drawn into a war against a coalition of most European monarchies. On 24 February, the National Convention decreed the mass draft of 300,000 men to defend the eastern borders. Throughout the country, revolts broke out against this conscription, everywhere they were brutally repressed with success. However, an insurgent territory, called the "Vendée Militaire" resisted. Determined to engage in a trial of strength with the new revolutionary regime, the Vendéens gradually organised themselves until they formed a "Catholic and Royal Army".

Vendée 93, a card driven two player wargame, takes you back to this particularly epic period in the French history, which pitted the young French Republic against the Royalist insurgents from March to October 1793.

"White" or "Blue", choose your cause and take command of your troops!
"White": you will have to take the advantage quickly at the risk of seeing the Republican forces inexorably strengthen, forcing you into a sinister "Virée de Galerne".
"Blues": time is on your side, resist the first assaults of the "brigands" to then be able to face the hell of the bocage in order to definitively extinguish the insurrection.

The object of the game is to use the included "indicator" (spinner) and compile a complete picture on your card.

—user summary

boardgame: Mystery Party in the Box: Solsticio Oscuro
description:
In this deductive genre, each player plays the role of a character with the dual purpose of solving a mystery and simultaneously achieving their own goals. The investigation, the negotiations with the rest of the cast and the exchange of information are essential pieces to solve the crime, but do not forget that the murderer is one of you. You can't believe everything they tell you, but you can't take victory without trusting someone either...

Once every ten years, vampires gather in a northern town during the winter solstice for their Great Gathering. Shortly before the official start, however, a charred corpse is discovered nearby: it can only be the remains of the second-in-command of one of the two contending factions of vampire society.

description:
In this deductive genre, each player plays the role of a character with the dual purpose of solving a mystery and simultaneously achieving their own goals. The girls' college that adjoins the high school grounds closed its doors seven years ago, but left in its wake the legend of a mysterious witch who disappeared from the chapel along with the beautiful tolling of the bell that rang from the top of the building.

Compete over ten rounds to claim victory in this fast paced trivia game.

One player hosts the game and everyone else battles it out. Each question has seven clues but only one right answer. The clues get easier, but the less you use, the more points you win! Any player can answer every question, so it's a race to react and slap the "go" card first.

How will you play? Go! for a high score, guess early, and risk crashing out of the round? Or Go! steady, use more clues and cunningly capture more points?

After the ten rounds whoever has the most points wins!

-description from publisher

Find out who’s the goody-two-shoes and whose antics are deserving of a time out in this hilarious game of “most likely to”! Each card lists a different scenario, anything from “who’s most likely to accidentally drop something in the toilet?” to “who’s most likely to claim to have been abducted by aliens?”.

Players take turns to read out the cards and nominate a player using their whiteboard paddles. The player with the least nominations at the end of the game is the winner and can claim their title as the most well-behaved of the bunch!

-description from publisher

boardgame: O Homem que Mordeu a Caderneta de Cromos
description:
The game is based on telling strange and bizarre stories, based on the cards that come out, corresponding to the questions: “where?”, “Who?”, “What?”, “To whom?”, “With what? " it's because?".

In O Homem Que Mordeu a Caderneta de Cromos, players set out in search of bizarre stories to tell when they land in the right house, and try to make their listeners laugh, so that they can earn prestigious coins. The possibilities are endless! Put creativity to the test and tell the best story!

The game takes place with the roll of a dice that moves us forward on the board. Through the house where we fall, we exchange our cards for others of the same color, changing the title of our bizarre news.
When we hit the "Está na Hora" house, we started to invent our story based on the title we were building in front of us.
We have time counted by an hourglass and if we get away with it within that time and we manage to convince the other players, we win a prestigious coin.
At the end of the game the player with the most prestigious coins wins.

It is the same game as O Homem que Mordeu o Cão but with a 70's/80's theme.

New Card Game.

Transparent and waterproof SHOOTHIT Playing Cards.

compound:

Complete set of 54 cards: 52 playing cards and 2 rule cards.

Game based on 4 different suits of 13 cards.

(Suits: Circle, Triangle, Penta, Square)

HOW PLAY

THE GAME
The game is very simple, if you know how to count you know how to play, you just have to add one.

After dealing all the cards that we cannot seen, each player reveals on his turn a card from his covered deck to his right. Witch he will try to mount to the central deck or mount on the other player or himself, if not, he leave the card on his right.

Creating like this on the right the undercover deck.

Ex: the 5 over the 4 (mounting in an always increasing way)

When a player draws an Ace, he leave the card on the center, creates the center deck and distributes a penalty to all players.Only in the central deck the numbers and ALSO the SUIT hve to be respected.

When the player place a card he have the right to reveal the next one.

ONLY 1 RULE: SHOOTHIT have only a important rule, and it's, you have to try to mount the cards already revealed  to your right before revealing another one.

And now you know how play SHOOTHIT!

The rules don't change but only the penalties according to the game mode.

This revolutionary educational card game is winning hearts and minds by bringing out our inner wordsmiths! Last Say is a game that uses player creativity to create phrases from drawn cards. You'll have to get clever to make a great sentence. But be careful! Other players can steal your phrase and win the points!

Objective:
Get the ultimate Last Say by winning the most points!

Contents: 
180 Cards

How to Play:
Set up: 
1. Each player draws 9 cards (3 of connecting words, and 2 each of nouns, verbs, and adjectives). Layout the cards on the table facing up (it does not matter if other players see your cards). After of your turn, replace used cards.

Play: 
2. First player forms a sentence with their cards. When using a connecting word you can choose any of the 3 shown on the card.

3.Following players form a new phrase or steal by adding to a previous phrase. They may also replace their unwanted cards. 
When it's the next turn of a player who's phrase was stolen, the last player who stole it collects the cards and wins the points. If nobody stole your phrase by your next turn, you win the points. It's a battle to get the 
                             
                            LAST SAY!

How to Win:
4. When one of the decks of cards is finished, count your points to see who won.

Points:

+ 1 Point: Does not include all the colors.

+2 Points: You steal by adding on to a phrase.

+ 3 Points: Includes all the colors.

Welcome to Absurdia! This card-based party game for 3-8 players will be sure to challenge you and your friends with absolutely absurd scenarios and arguments, with endless combinations of cards and infinite fun to be had! The goal of Absurdia is to be the first player to win 3 Question Cards. Two players face off each turn and play Hero and Support Cards that best answer the question. Each player gets 30 seconds to give their absurd argument as to why their cards answer the question better than their opponent's. The remainder of the group then votes on the winner, who wins the Question Card! The questions are ridiculous! The heroes are unlikely! The attributes are ludicrous! Get ready to flex your imagination and dive into the oddest of scenarios, because here at Absurdia we live by one motto:
It's time to get weird!

-description from publisher

This is a variation on a ring or ball toss game. The ability to have dimmed headlights was brand new and somewhat controversial in the early '30s, so it made for a trendy subject. The game includes a stand-up board or target with lithographed art showing three cars surrounded by a squad of five motorcycle cops. The headlights of each vehicle are labeled with a point value of 10 or 20 and each has a cupped tin tray underneath to catch a small disc and hold it in place.

The goal is to take your set of heavy cardboard discs - red or yellow - and score points by tossing them at the target and covering the headlights of the three cars. However, misjudging your aim and covering the headlights of the police motorcycles will instead accrue negative points!

The winner of the game is the first player to reach 100 points.

Long ago the Gems of Power were wielded by Heroes and Wizards. Commanding and combining them to subdue the powers of evil, and drive them from the land. Over time the heroes vanished and the Wizards aged, and retired. The evil returned, hiding in the dark depths and causing havoc among the lands above.

The Gems have been found again... it is time to purge the evil once again.

In Dungeon2 (squared) take on the role of one of four characters and face the challenges of a randomly created dungeon.

Brave heroes prepare yourselves for adventure.....

Game Play

Players simultaneously play 4 cards from their 6 card character deck which provides a number of primary colored gems. These gems may then be mixed/added together to produce a number of secondary color gems which are used to succeed at the challenges of the dungeon.

Meet the "Safe Passage" challenge of a room to safely pass through.

Meet the "Treasure" challenge of a room to collect a Treasure Card to add to your Character Deck. Increasing your, or another player's, resource pool.

Meet the "Magic Item" challenge of a room to gain a Magic Item that may provide you more gems or heal your wounds.

Meet the "Gold" challenge of a room to collect Gold, which
count as victory points toward being crowned Champion of the Dungeon!

Each character deck has one card with three gems which may be used for its face value, or for its Special Power which may effect either yourself or another character.

Warrior: Draws an extra card or can "pull" another character along to the next room.

Cleric: Use your protection spell to stave off damage for yourself, or another player.

Thief: Steal the top treasure card from the deck to add to your, or another player's, character deck.

Mage: Reduce the Challenge for another player by 2 gems.

—description from the publisher

This is a card game from Stoll & Edwards. Essentially a set collection game, "Heck" is played with 12 numbered cards dealt out to players at the start. Three special Heck cards are placed face-up in the center of the table. The object is to get a continuous sequence of cards in your hand: six if there are two players; four if there are three players; and three if there are four players.

Players throw three dice, each with 1-4, a star, and a crescent moon on its faces. Whatever number is rolled, they may "call" for that card, and if another player has it, they must give it. Throwing three stars allows a player to call for any card they want to help make a continuous sequence. Throwing three crescents allows them to draw a Heck card and use it as a wild card in a sequence.

The first player to complete a sequence wins the game, "by Heck!"

In Blood of a King the object of the game is to wound your opponent's King 3 times. Each player starts with a deck of 12 cards, 10 Warrior cards numbered 0-9, 1 King card numbered 10, and 1 Witch card numbered 11. A round begins by each player dealing out five card onto the play surface from left to right. The cards have a silhouette of an Axe or a Shield along the long edge of the card. When the edges of the cards line up they will either match axes, shields, or have no match. The number of Axes is your attack strength, the number of shields your defense. If you have more Axes than you opponent has Shields, you have wounded their tribe. See what their highest numbered card is. If it is a Warrior, remove the card from play. If it is the King, give them one of your matching wound cards. If it is their Witch, remove their Witch from play, but also remove your lowest Warrior card.

If the initial draw of cards results in a tie, each player plays another card to the right of the last card played. Count Axes and Shields again, and follow the above results.

If your tribe is going to be wounded by your opponent you may CALL UPON THE BERSERKS! When you Call Upon The Berserks you may draw one card and play it to either end of your row of cards. You may do this only once per round and the Berserk card will be removed from play after the round.

Continue playing rounds until one player has received the three wound cards from their opponent.

—from the publisher
This is a tiddledy winks game in which the aim is to snap the counters into the open mouth of one of three dog characters with oversized heads.

Promo handed at at origins 2021.

ACT Reduce the cost of moon artifacts by 1 diamond this turn
ACT Draw 1 Card

It's time for another Sushi Rush tournament!

Prepare your chopsticks and your determination to overcome your opponents in this game of dexterity, skill and delicious sushi. In each round, players will compete simultaneously in different game modes. Master the art of focus and efficiency to become the next Master Sushiman!

On each round, players will draw a card and compete to grab the right combination of sushi with their chopsticks. Each card feature a unique challenge, be it collecting sushis of the same flavor, shape or even stacking them to build the highest tower.

Players receive honor points depending on their success and the one with the most points at the end of the game becomes the next Master Sushiman.

-description from publisher

This is a miniature golf game from E. E. Fairchild. The board has a "tee-off" space in the center with nine areas - the nine golf holes - radiating out from it. The goal is to play all nine holes in the lowest number of strokes.

The game comes with a miniature metal club (approx. 4 inches long) and two metal balls.

boardgame: The Novel Game of Stop and Go!
description:
The baby is hungry! The first player to get to the Milk Man and then back home with a bottle of milk will win.

Fundamentally this is a movement game, using two traffic cop spinners that tell players how many spaces to move, what direction they can go, and whether or not they can turn. According to the All-Fair catalog, the game "teaches traffic rules with great gusto and lots of fun."

Players use little metal cars as tokens and are allowed to share spaces. Both the Milk Man and the Policeman (the entrance to Home) must be reached on exact spins.

The game was published with a double-sided board that has a checkerboard on the other side.

Kakapo is the version of Scum: The Food Chain Game with new rules, alliance, tactics and bluffing.

What is a kakapo? It is a parrot in serious danger of extinction: only 201 specimens left alive!

Principle of the game:
Kakapo is a game centered on this endangered Pacific parrot. A game sometimes unfair, always fun whose objective is to get rid of all your cards first to be crowned king.

A game of Asshole/President revisited with new rules for more atmosphere as well as more reflection.

A game with simple rules, enhanced by extensions: Rapido, Haka tapé, Vahiné, Putsch.

With this game, the publisher sponsors YWAM, a Kakapo (Kakapo Recovery organization Kakapo Recovery in New Zealand) and would like to multiply the sponsorships/donations according to the sales of the game.

—description from the publisher

Túlélők Viadala is an exciting quiz, trivia game in which survival is at stake. In a playful way, the game provides knowledge for solving unexpected situations that occur in everyday life, for which ordinary people may not be prepared.

During the game, you have to survive disaster situations hidden in the skin of different characters: in the event of a winter power outage lasting several days, after a plane crash in the jungle, or just during a zombie attack. In the first part of the game, the characters test their survival skills with quiz questions about emergencies and disasters. With correct answers, they get tools that they can use during the survival situation in the second part of the game. This is where the misfortune takes place, the stages of which can bring more and more plagues.

La Chacolatería is a board game for 1-4 players in which you'll cooperate to run a café of chacorritos, adorable but very demanding creatures that will come to your place and order from you. Here there are no turns, but all the players will play at the same time, dividing the tasks if necessary and playing the correct command cards on the little ones that ask for them. If you manage to serve all the chacorritos before the time runs out (counted through a stopwatch or the official application), you will have won the game!
In the estimated time, you will have to seat the chacorritos at their tables, give them the complete orders from the hand of one of the players, clean their tables when everyone has been served (separate the order cards and chacorritos from that table) and wash the dishes if necessary (shuffle the order cards when they are finished). All this in a limited time where you will have to think fast and cooperate with your companions!
But that's not all, because many special levels await you with which to test yourselves as a team. You may have to serve the chacorritos in a buffet, or play with one less table, or you may not be able to speak to communicate during the game or even face your friends in the competitive levels. Are you ready? The chacorritos are waiting for you!

—description from the designer

It’s the family favorite card game you love, now enhanced with the power of the Justice League! Players race to get rid of all their cards by matching a card in their hand with the current card shown on top of the deck.

There are two customizable cards and a special new wild card: the Superman X-Ray vision card! That card lets you look at any other player's cards and make strategic exchanges that pump up the excitement!

Score points by being the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. First player or team to 500 wins. When you're down to one card, don't forget to yell "UNO!" Contains 112 cards plus instructions. For 2 to 10 players, ages 7 and older. Easy to pick up, impossible to put down.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
The Barrier Mountains are the site of a strange falling star from years ago! Your mine will strike the earth at the Barrier Mountains, and uncover the secrets of an ancient artifact buried deep below. Will you discover its secrets? Or will your mine fall to the odd creatures that inhabit these mountains? Play to find out, as your mine tells its story!

Barrier Mountains is an expansion to Dwarf Mine (the Dwarf Mine base game is required to play).

Like Dwarf Mine, Barrier Mountains is a game played by drawing a mine on graph paper. On your turn, you will first draw (dig) rooms, hallways, and ladders for your mine! Rooms will benefit your mine, and hallways and ladders let you reach other areas of your mountain.

After drawing, you will make two dice rolls - a treasure roll, and a combat roll. These rolls determine what treasure you find, and what creatures you may encounter in the mountain - after all, mining is dangerous business!

Each mine made has a story, and every story has a beginning, middle, and sadly, an end. Your game ends when your population reaches 0. By the end of the game, you’ll have created rich and unique history for your mine – no two games are the same!

Players are getting VP during the game and trying to see how many VP they can get each time they play. Kind of like a high score.

Barrier Mountains expands on this experience, by offering a sci-fi style of gameplay, which includes:
    A brand new Dwarf Mine map
    4 new rooms for your Dwarf Mine
    4 new final bosses to fight
    4 new Treasure Tables (one for each mine level)
    4 new Combat Tables (one for each mine level)
    New mechanics unique to this map

—description from the publisher

Life in the village is hard, but life here also allows the inhabitants to grow and prosper as they please. One villager might want to become a friar. Another might feel ambitious and strive for a career in public office. A third one might want to seek their luck in distant lands.

In Village, each player takes the reins of a family and attempts to help them find fame and glory in many different ways. There is one thing you must not forget, however: Time will not stop for anyone, and given enough time people will vanish. Those who find themselves immortalized in the village chronicles will bring honor to their family and be one step closer to victory.

In more detail, each turn you take a cube from an area on the game board, then take the action of that area. The board has zones with specific attributes, a market, a travel zone, a crafting zone, a church, and a council house, and each zone is seeded with cubes of four colors as well as black cubes that serve as curses. Many of these areas offer multiple actions, so if for example you take a cube from the crafting area, you can get an ox, a horse, a cart, a plow, or a scroll, or you can convert wheat to gold. Some areas offer short-term scoring, others offer long-term scoring, and still others offer only endgame scoring. The round ends when no cubes remain at any location.  The game ends when either the village chronicle or the anonymous graveyard is full.

Village: Big Box contains the Village base game, the Inn and Port expansions, every promo previously released, and a new "Marriage" expansion, with all of the materials being integrable with one another and with the game now containing a solo mode as well as revised gameplay, dual-layered player boards, and a larger, double-sided main board that eliminates the need for the overlays used in the original expansions.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: DC Comics HeroClix: Justice League Unlimited Booster Brick

This starter set contains everything up to 4 people need to play HeroClix.

boardgame: DC Comics HeroClix: Rebirth Booster Brick

DC Rebirth is DC Comics most recent reboot of the entire DC Universe that re-imagines many of the most iconic heroes and villains in a stunning, new light! This exciting interpretation of the DC Universe includes new iterations of the usual suspects from the Justice League, JSA, and Teen Titans as well as new and exciting themes like Deathstroke’s Defiance, Justice League China, and the Dark Trinity of Bizarro, Red Hood, and Artemis!

Long-time HeroClix fans will be ecstatic to see many never-before-clixed characters like Power Girl, The Signal, Colonel Poison, Bumblebee, Cyclone, Commander Steel, Shade the Changing Girl, Jessica Cruz, and Flex Mentallo alongside their favorite, iconic characters!

boardgame: DC Comics HeroClix: Batman Animated Series Starter Set

The DC Comics HeroClix: Batman The Animated Series Starter Set includes two iconic superheroes from the Bat Family and four of Batman’s most notorious enemies with all-new dials! This Starter Set features the namesake of the set, Batman, as well as his always trusty sidekick, Robin, both ready to take down crime in Gotham! Their malevolent counterparts include Catwoman, Harley Quinn, Poison Ivy, and the ever-sinister The Joker!

Along with these six fantastic figures, this Starter Set includes everything a player needs to learn and start playing HeroClix today! From the figures, to a HeroClix Rulebook, two double-sided Maps, a Powers and Abilities Card (PAC), Object Tokens, Terrain Tokens, Dice, and Character Cards. Get this unbelievable Starter Set today to start playing HeroClix or bolster your ever-growing collection!

The DC Comics HeroClix: Harley Quinn and the Gotham Girls Fast Forces features 6 HeroClix figures with all-new dial designs, focusing on the Thrillkiller storyline from Elseworlds. This pack is perfect for Harley Quinn and Batman fans alike and an excellent product for new and established players!

Fast Forces packs are designed for quick play and easy team building, for both tournaments and casual games.

boardgame: DC Comics HeroClix: 15th Anniversary Elseworlds Starter Set

With the 15th Anniversary of HeroClix, WizKids is excited to reveal DC heroes as seen in the pages of Elseworlds!

The DC Comics HeroClix: 15th Anniversary Elseworlds Starter Set includes the most iconic DC heroes. A great introduction to HeroClix for rookies, a useful teaching tool for experienced stores, and a fresh look at iconic characters for HeroClix veterans!

The DC Comics HeroClix: 15th Anniversary Elseworlds Booster Brick includes the most iconic DC heroes and villains of all time, exactly as you’d expect them to be, and with incredible alternate versions too. HeroClix fans can explore a world in which Kal-El was raised by the Waynes, or Diana Prince as a marshal in the Wild West. See your favorite characters in ways never before possible on the HeroClix tabletop! Themes shift from settings in Gotham to Egypt, the Daily Planet to the basketball court, and more! Explore these destinations and the personalities found in the alternate universe of DC Comics HeroClix: 15th Anniversary Elseworlds.

With 50 figures to collect, DC Comics HeroClix: 15th Anniversary Elseworlds is sure to be on many HeroClix players’ wish list.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Comics HeroClix: The Joker's Wild Booster Brick

The Most Maniacal Madman in Gotham Takes Over in… DC Comics HeroClix: The Joker‘s Wild!
Open the asylum— it’s time for the inmates to run amok! The DC Comics HeroClix: The Joker‘s Wild! set features The Joker, The Riddler, The Penguin, Mr. Freeze and a cadre of other iconic Gotham City villains who have continuously challenged it’s harrowed hero— Batman. The DC Comics HeroClix: The Joker‘s Wild! set has 70 figures to collect. The DC Comics HeroClix: The Joker‘s Wild! Booster Bricks include ten (10) 5-figure booster packs.

description:

Heat your tires with a good burnout, Stage Your car slowly as you pull up to the starting line, wait for the other driver, amber lights start dropping on the Christmas tree……… yellow……. Yellow……. Yellow……. Green……… full throttle as its time to go….go.…. go.

In August 1945, Japan made an unconditional surrender to the Allied Powers in World War II. At the end of the weary eight-year-long full-scale anti-fascist war, the Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) and the Communist Party of China (CPC), temporarily united to resist invasion by militaristic Japan, were once again faced with the crisis of breaking up. An outright war that would soon engulf the whole country was about to come.

This strategic-level wargame applies our innovative game system specially designed for the Chinese Civil War. You will play as the Chinese Nationalist Party or the Chinese Communist Party through the civil war between 1945 and 1949. With a unique attack-and-reaction system and area control system, you can experience different tactics such as mobile warfare and guerrilla warfare in the Chinese Civil War, deploying resources across the whole country. With a political simulation and event system, you can also experience the political gambles of both sides in the civil war.

This is a war that will determine the fate of China's future. Should you concentrate your superior forces to destroy the enemy in one move, or reserve your strength by mobile operations? Should you strive to lead your side to a total victory in the war or conduct negotiations to gain an advantage in the coalition government? The choice is up to you.

boardgame: Thrown Under the Bus: The Game of Office Politics
description:
Thrown Under the Bus, the light hearted Game of Office Politics, TUtB is packed with cringeworthy buzzwords and all-too-familiar office tropes that will keep players entertained while they steal credit for coworkers’ hard work in an attempt to advance up the corporate ladder. But be careful. Fellow associates are attempting to do the same thing, and once you play a dirty trick on them, they now have access to this new trick. Anyone who has worked in an office environment knows there is no more brutal kill-or-be-killed workplace than the modern office setting.

Thrown Under the Bus (TUtB) is designed so that non-gamers could easily learn and enjoy but still has the complexities and advanced tactics that will keep seasoned gamers engaged. TUtB hits that delicate balance while creating a fun thematic experience. An average game takes between 30 and 60 minutes depending on group size of 2-6 players and their experience level. The Ages 16+ is more of a theme suggestion, the mechanics and rules can easily be understood by younger players but we felt why get them jaded about work before they enter the workforce. Let them discover that joy the same way we did…

Don’t let the cut throat theme fool you, Thrown Under the Bus is fun and cathartic release at the end of a long week of dealing with your coworkers quirks and protecting your station at work.

You have a mere 9 months to prepare! Your nest is drab and bare, sticks and leaves won't draw anyone's eye. Collect trinkets and tokens from the forest floor to increase the appeal of your nest. It doesn't have to be perfect, it just has to be better than that other guy's.

Bower is a competitive 2 player, pick-up and deliver game with engine building upgrades and push-your-luck scoring elements. Gameplay revolves around strategic pathfinding and turn optimisation. On your turn, your opponent will first populate the forest with berries and trinkets using the seed deck. You may then alter the paths through the forest by rotating the 7 central tiles, before moving your Bowerbird along its current path, collecting the trinkets you pass over. If you can return your bowerbird to the nest, you score points depending on the number of trinkets it returned with. You also earn a powerful crest to enhance your future turns. The player with the most points from their bower and personal goal cards is the winner.

Note: Great Bowerbirds (endemic to Australia) have a fascination with anything green or white. The Males will collect as many discarded toys, plastic scraps, stones, bones and shells as they can find to decorate their nest (Bower), in the hopes of scoring a partner during the November-January mating season. Young males will even work together on a “practice” nest, to ensure their skills are properly honed.

description:
Each player chooses a set of 6 colored dice & matching speed cup. The game is played in a series of rounds. There are no turns, all players roll their dice at the same time.

Draw the top 3 cards and place them face up in the middle of all the players. These are what cards players will try to win in the round. To begin, players count down to showtime with '3, 2, 1, showtime!'. There are no turns, all players roll their dice at the same time.

After each roll, players place any of their dice next to a card to 'book' it. This indicates which cards they are rolling for. Players continue booking dice until the combination is matched and someone wins the card.
Any player that had dice booked on that card may now take them back.

When all 3 cards are collected the round is over and players add up their scores. Draw the next three cards and place them face up to start the next round. Keep playing rounds until someone collects 1 million subscribers!

How well do you really know your friends – can you find your soulmate?

To find out, you collect Friend-Chips with every other player. You do this by correctly assessing your fellow players based on creative scenarios. The two players with the most Friend-Chips at the end of the game become Soulmates.

In each round, use one of 125 short scenarios with four possible answers.

First you decide for yourself, then you guess your fellow players’ decision.

If you and your partner match, you will each receive a Friend-Chip.

description:
Neko Boss is a fun abstract cat game.  Players need to move their cats around Neko(cat) Town to take control of the streets.  When the cat stack grows those cats can travel further.  Also, the top cat on the stack is the one that controls that stack of cats.

Basic rule for standard edition.

Board is consist of 24 intersections and paths. At first to decide players number, 2 or 3 or 4. Each player has same number of own pieces.

phase 1: placement
Decide players turn and every player places their piece on the board(intersections) alternately.

phase 2: making groups by piece moving
A piece can move one space but it must stack on other piece(s). It can't move to vacant space.
Player who keeps top piece of the group can move them. They must move as same number of stack pieces straightly on the path jumping other piece(s). They can't move to vacant space.
Players can't pass their turn if they have regal take. Passing the turn means finishing their playing.
When all players has no regal take, then the game is over. 4 or more pieces stack is score. The player who keep more 4 or more stacks than opponents wins the game. Score is same, then player who keeps highest stack wins the game. All are same, then player who keeps more stacks wins the game.

-description from designer

Back in 1994, in a demonstration which took place in Belgium, the sentence “If the bee disappeared off the face of the Earth, man would only have four years left to live”  was exclaimed. 
In Mel, you’re the queen bee and your purpose is to collect nectar and produce honey with the help of your swarm. Will you get your swarm of bees to feed you and your larvae for the good of the ecosystem?

The goal of the game is to be the queen bee with more nectar or honey points at the end of the game.

When it’s your turn, you have two options:

-Place one of your bees on the central flower crown (or the beehive) of the game board and move it compulsorily. The movement has to be either vertical or horizontal and has to reach the furthest clear flower crown or beehive of the chosen direction; it’s forbidden to jump over another bee.

-Move one of the bees which is already present on the game board. The possible movements are the ones stated in the previous step.

In either case, when your turn is over, the central flower crown (or beehive) can never be occupied. Now, it’s the turn of the queen bee situated on your left.

The game can end because of two different situations:

- When there are no bees left to play. When a queen bee places her last bee, the game ends for her, but the rest must go on.

-To cause a Mel: when you place a bee in the center of the game board, and you can’t move it anymore.

In that case, you need to add up the obtained nectar (or honey) points and the player with the highest score is proclaimed the winner. In case of a tie, the player who has a bigger group wins. if the tie continues, the player who has the second bigger group wins, and so on and so forth.

-description from publisher
