description:
Alternate faction that can be added to Claim or Claim 2.

After phase 1, shuffle the discarded cards and form a second deck. If you win a trick with an Awakener in phase 2, you draw a card from the second deck and place it in your Score Pile.

Alternate faction that can be added to Claim or Claim 2.

In Phase 1, when the card where the players are competing off is a Satyr, the second player does not have to follow faction. The highest number is considered the winner of the trick.

Alternate faction for Claim or Claim 2.

In Phase 2, if a Tinkerer beats the other card by a value of 4 or more, then the Tinkerer explodes! The other card is discarded from the game. The player who played the Tinkerer still wins the trick and the other card.

Alternate faction for Claim or Claim 2.

If the Valkyrie is played following another faction, it becomes that faction if it is valued 3 or higher than the first card played.

The cameras are on and the microphones are hot! Grab a copy of your best-selling book or snag a press badge to take part in the media scrum. Hope you've memorized your talking points, because we are going live in 3... 2... 1!

In Press Tour, players take on the roles of attendees at the press-launch for a new book release, either as the author or one of the interviewers. Direct the interview to meet your personal goals or the requirements of your network to get the scoop of your career!

Sute Sute Railroading includes several methods of play (Simple, Regular, or Advanced), a few variants, and rules for a bonus game.  The rules include the designer's typical sense of humor, with tie breaking rules related to how many of the designer's games you own and a prohibition on asking them when a certain one of their designs will finally be released.

The "Simple" rule set seems to be identical to their 2010 release, Sute Sute Marketing.

The "Regular" rules involve playing cards to a network of cards on the table.  A player earns money equal to the increase in value of a train's network.  The player then draws 2 cards.  When the deck is exhausted, scores are calculated. The player with the most cards ("shares") of a company in their hand receives money equal to the company's value (stations); the player with the second most shares takes each share which belonged to to the player with the most shares.

After each company's accounts have been settled, players earn 1 yen for each card they have, and the player with the most money wins.

Nephilim Fraternités is the first board game in the Nephilim rpg universe. 
It is a game of cooperation between Nephilim to face their ancestral enemies, secret societies, while trying to acquire as much esoteric knowledge as they can. It takes place in past incarnation periods.
The game play is a mix of dice/worker placement where each player has a dice pool they use to start actions on the game board. Each action is linked to an element and can either allow better fighting against the secret societies or progression in the quest for knowledge.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: Kingdoms Forlorn: Ten Thousand Succulent Fears Expansion
description: A major Expansion for the Kingdoms Forlorn, this set expands on both Core Game Kingdoms and includes a 2 new sub-Kingdoms, Jura the Stronghold of the Saints, and Knight Campaign with over 50 hours of content.

boardgame: Kingdoms Forlorn: Red Kingdom of Eshin Expansion
description: A major Kingdom expansion for the Kingdoms Forlorn, this set includes a new Kingdom (Red Kingdom of Eshin) and a new Knight Campaign with over 50 hours of content.

boardgame: Kingdoms Forlorn: Barony of Bountiful Harvest Expansion
description: A major Kingdom expansion for the Kingdoms Forlorn, this set includes a new Kingdom (Sunken Kingdom of Utrebant) and a new Knight Campaign with over 50 hours of content.

boardgame: Last Chance Saloon: A Murder Mystery Game
description:
It’s last orders in the only saloon in town, and there’s more to kill than just time!
 Will the richest rancher in the Wild West be dead before sundown, or will you be able to save his skin?
 The year is 1885 and death will cast its shadow over the quiet town of Ghost Creek unless your sleuthing skills are able to stop it! Inside the Last Chance Saloon, six people nurse their drinks, each lost in thought. A traveling fortune teller decides to stop and quench her thirst. Suddenly, she’s struck with a vision: Avery Rogers – Ghost Creek’s richest man – will be dead by the end of the day, and at the hands of someone in the saloon! Set in the Wild West, this murder mystery will immerse you and your dinner guests in a world of revenge, blackmail and bandits! Will you be able to unravel the clues and motives to pinpoint the would-be murderer, all before coffee is served?

-description from publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Silver Screen: A Murder Mystery Game
description:
Ruby Bloom is Hollywood’s leading lady. But away from the flashing lights of the paparazzi and her throng of adoring fans, she is a notorious bully, prone to explosive outbursts, vile tantrums and outrageous demands. Ruby is found poisoned in her dressing room on the set of her latest movie. From short-fused director Chuck Demeyer to long-suffering PA Jane Madison, there’s no shortage of people who want Ruby gone; but who finally killed her, and why?
 Set in the Golden Age of Hollywood, this immersive murder mystery will see you and your fellow dinner guests become both murder suspects and detectives. Will you be able to unravel the clues, pinpoint the motives and solve the murder before coffee is served?

-description from publisher

Leave your moral compass at the door and find out what your friends think of you with this outrageous game that’ll get you naming and shaming!

Players take turns to read out a card with a “Most likely to” or “Least likely to” scenario, then vote on which player they think best fits that description.

Discover who needs to rethink their life choices in this game of naming and shaming!

A game of no winners, but one loser. You don't want to be deemed the trashiest!

—description from publisher

boardgame: Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Flames & Frost: Kickstarter Edition
description:
The Kickstarter editions comes packaged with Expansion Pack #03: Agents, Expansion Pack #04: Peasants & Knights, Duke Mico and 10 Alternate Art Starter Cards, which are not contained in the retail edition.

New threats have reared their monstrous heads in the land of Valeria! Scouts have reported seeing fiery hounds to the South and beasts that bring with them winter's deathly bite to the North. The King's heralds have been dispatched to all of the corners of Valeria to call upon new Adventurers for you to recruit while the workers lay the foundation of new Domains.

Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Flames and Frost adds two new powerful Monster stacks, a new set of Citizens to recruit, and new Domains for you to purchase.

Within Stock Monarchy you will play as the ruler of a small Kingdom listed on the stock market. The competition will be relentless. To win, players must bring their Kingdom to dominate the stock market with a savvy combination of warfare, technology and trade.

THE STOCK STATES
The Stock States are a rich empire with a bizarre form of government: a stock monarchy. The Supreme Sovereign wields absolute power, but his election and term in office are determined by the satisfaction of the stockholders, the true masters of the Land.

CORONATION CHALLENGE
The time has come to nominate a new Supreme Sovereign. As usual, the Coronation Challenge is issued so as to find the most talented contestant amongst the richest Houses of the Realm. The winner of the Challenge will be crowned Ruler of the Stock States.
The Challenge will take place on one of the peripheral and uncivilized islands of the empire. The Island is inhabited by Mattoks, somewhat obtuse and naïve creatures with a very basic lifestyle. Mattoks can’t quite discern between good and evil and are therefore easy to manipulate or to corrupt. They are not to be entrusted with complex tasks, but they are excellent at following simple instructions and will comply with whatever command in exchange for a few shiny coins.
At the beginning of the Challenge every participant will be assigned a starting region of equal dimension and three months’ time to showcase their strategic skills and – most important of all – to enrich the stockholders.
The management of the Realms on the Island mirrors the stock monarchy of the Stock States: each scion will be free to act as an authentic absolute monarch, but his choices will be evaluated by the stock market. Dominating the market, however, will not be easy: all shareholders ultimately desire to grow richer and see the value of their shares rise, but some of them also have specific preferences on how this should be done. The two most prominent examples are definitely the conqueror and pioneer currents. The conquerors want a Ruler in power who pursues profit through military expansion, while the pioneers prefer one who will enrich them by investing in technological growth. Therefore, whoever wants to sit on the Throne of the Stock States will not only have to effectively administer his own Realm, but will also have to distribute copious dividends and support, at least in part, the interests of conquerors and pioneers. Ultimately, the winner of the Coronation Challenge will be the Ruler who is able to impose himself on the market and make the value of the shares of his Realm soar.

GAME-FLOW
The Game-Flow has 3 stages: Setup, Round and End Game.

SETUP
• Creating the island: the Island is created, depending on the number of Players (in a 2-Player game it is possible to create more
than 2 billion different Map layouts, in a 4 Player game more than a trillion, and that's without counting Resources and Know-How Tokens).
• Auctioning the Starting Territories: Players bid to seize the best possible starting Territory for their Realms.
• Setting up the Realms: every Ruler autonomously chooses the initial Setup of their Realm.

ROUND
• 3 Game Rounds: The 3 Game Rounds is where the real action takes place. The Kings have to plan and implement their strategies by issuing orders to the Spies and Mattoks of their own Realm. This is done through the 14 Command Cards, which let Players move their Spies and carry out Actions with the Mattoks.

END GAME
• Distributing dividends to the shareholders: the Money, Ingots and revenue from the sale of White Whiskey are used to pay
dividends, which drives up the value of the shares of the Realm.
• Announcing the winner: the Player whose shares have reached the highest value is crowned as new Supreme Sovereign of the Stock States.

-description from designer

In Chicken Hunter, players simultaneously reveal cards from their hand, aiming to score points.   There are no restrictions on what cards players can reveal (e.g. does not need to be a straight, or x-of-a-kind).

In each round, two hands will be rewarded with points.  First, look at the players who revealed the fewest number of cards.  This player earns points equal to the number of cards they revealed multiplied by the sum of the face value of these cards.  (In the case of a tie, the tied players first discard one card each.)

Then, from the remaining players, look at the player with the lowest face value sum of the cards revealed.  This player scores points using the same calculation.

Afterwards, players draw up to 6 cards and repeat the process.

The game lasts until a player achieves a preset threshold and at that point the person with the most points wins.

The players take turns to roll and move. They have to get their 4 cars to the parking lot of their color to win the game. Before each turn the traffic police man in the middle of the board is rotated to block one direction of movement.

West of Souls is innovative solo or cooperative fantasy adventure for 1-6 players. Enter the world of Eloria, plagued by darkness and monsters of a vitriolic nature. You and your party will explore and make decisions that directly affect the world’s infrastructure and future. The vastly branching storylines and numerous NPCs found in West of Souls ensure unique game play and future replay ability.

Enemy encounters and agonizing choices abound in this game and even the simplest of decisions may alter the world as you know it. Players will take up the mantle of an unlikely hero who, through motives of their own, have decided to stop Eloria’s impending doom. Be it gold, valor, or a deep empathy for the world around them, the players must work together to gain victory over scenarios and continue on their journey.

As players progress through the ever-changing world, they will develop their abilities, gain increasingly effective gear, and unlock additional skills specific to their character. Following each completed scenario, player choices will determine the future direction of the party’s quest, guaranteeing player involvement in the development of their own story. 
 The game is split into scenarios that are comprised of an exploration and journey phase, and each turn is split into a time of day (morning, mid-day, dusk, night). In the journey phase of the game, characters with travel throughout the game map discovering locations, triggering events, battling monsters, interacting with the world, and talking with NPCs to gain information about their quest and Eloria’s tainted past. Action point-based turns are used within West of Souls. Each player will have a defined number of actions that may be completed on their turn (unless you have unlocked a skill or found some gear that says otherwise). Once enough information has been gathered and players are in the correct location, the exploration phase begins.

One unique aspect of West of Souls is that the exploration phase is a hybrid of non-linear narrative, card-based battle encounters, and dungeon crawling. In addition, all characters in play do not have to enter the same exploration together, allowing for multiple explorations to occur simultaneously. Within the exploration phase, players will be forced to make harrowing choices, complete skill checks, and defeat beast to move forward. Vital story elements, lore, and history will be revealed to the players as they progress through the game.  
While death is an option in West of Souls, a player is never permanently removed from the game. The main resources in this epic journey are time and stress. If your team does not accomplish their goal within the allotted time frame, Eloria is cast into ruin. Likewise, if the encounters prove too much for your party and your stress level reaches a breaking point, you will lose the game. Victory is achieved at the end of each scenario as players defeat enemies of increasing difficultly and complete the necessary tasks. 
 This flagship game from Sky Monster Games is an ambitious story telling odyssey that maximizes the use of battle mechanics in combination with non-linear storytelling and character customization.

-description from designer

The answer cards are evenly dealt among the players, which take turns to roll and move one lone pawn about the game board. When the pawn stops on a space the player reads the question printed on the space. The player who thinks he has the right answer card gives the answer. If the answer is right the player gest a commendation, if not he gets a rebuke. The game ends when the pawn reaches or passes the last space. The player with the most commendations wins the game. But for each rebuke six commendations are substracted

—user summary.

In Roll & Write Railroads, the players are new employees of a railroad company, having just left their families' bean farms.  They were so good at running the bean farm, they were put in charge of all of the railroad projects. However, they have no knowledge of railroads!  So they made a die out of a soybean their parents entrusted them with, and leave their fate up to it.

On a turn, a player rolls one more dice than the number of players.  Then, in clockwise order, each player selects a die.  The players then take an action based upon the die number.  The actions involve laying track, building stations, and possibly gaining money.  Later, players may unlock ways to enhance these actions.

Players may only build from left to right, and only on train lines they have opened.

After each player finishes their actions, they each take a second action with the unselected die.  Then, the players score points based upon how far their stations have been built on each line.

After ten rounds, players sum their intermediate scores and also earn points for how many of their actions they enhanced.  The player with the most points wins.

Power for the People is a political satire game that recreates the current divisive political environment. Players take on the role of an already powerful party leader. Each party leader is revered and respected by supporters and opponents alike, but are plagued with an insatiable desire for more power. Power is gained by gathering support from the party and by winning the influenceable party members of opponents’.

One party leader is the chairman for the round and takes on certain roles and responsibilities. He or she starts the round by saying, “for the people” at which point everyone picks up the hand they have been dealt and begins to play in an orderly fashion, always abiding by the rules and laws. Proper respect and decorum must be maintained during play including only speaking at appropriate times and only when required by rules and laws. Players keep each other in check by giving a penalty card from the top of the deck for observed deviation from the rules and laws. One player wins the round by playing his or her last card and proclaiming “Power for the People!”. The winning player collects power, creates a secret law, and becomes the chairman for the next round.

Play continues with the next round. All of the existing rules and laws are in force, but now a secret law is in play. Opponents of the new chairman must deduce the action or lack of action that the secret law requires. Doing so will avoid penalties and enable a potential future win allowing him or her to gain power and write a secret law of their own, for which he or she can penalize other players.

By the final round there may be up to 10 secret laws in effect. At this point it becomes challenging to remember all the laws and rules in order to avoid getting additional cards through penalties. At the end of the final round, the player with the most power becomes the Supreme Leader!

To review, in Power For The People, players establish the party platform, gain power, create and follow secret laws, penalize offenders, and become Supreme Leader!

The description on the box says "PLAY GOD!!! Start your own religion! Persecute non-believers! Send followers to heaven! Riotous spoof and FUN for 12 YEARS and older."

It plays like Sorry! with a few twists and turns.

It has a National Lampoon vibe. Adult themes. Irreverent. Edgy.

It was made by the American Publishing Corporation in Waltham, Massachusetts. This famous company no longer exists, but its infamous products live on. They made drug and sex themed board games, puzzles in cans (including Playboy centerfold puzzles), and politically incorrect books about how to fish, how to ski, and how to play golf. For example, one of their games is "LSD Ludicrous Systems Development: A Psychedelic Happening Construction Kit."

—user summary

boardgame: Marvel Lucha Libre Edition: La Lucha Infinita
description:
Marvel Lucha Libre Edition characters fight one another, with a combination of luck and strategy to win 3 falls, and be the last "Rudo" or "Técnico" standing in the ring.

Roll dice to move your pawn, and if you land in a space with a wrestler from another player, you enter the ring... use one of your characters cards, then add the roll of your dice to see who comes out on top, win 3 falls and eliminate that wrestler. Last wrestler standing wins!

With 2 modes to play... Amateur and Pro Wrestler.

Whip out the spatulas and get to the grill! In Patty Stack, your goal is to construct the most delicious hamburger. You’ll score points for well-played cards, but be careful – you’ll lose points for any card you can’t play! Build your burger and then eat your hard work to find out how you stack up against the competition. It’s medium rare to find a game this well done.

Patty Stack is a dice-drafting and card placement game. On your turn, you will select a dice from the table and place a patty card on your patty stack that matches your die value. You can never stack two cards next to each other with matching values or matching toppings. You will score points for the cards you successfully stack, and will have a point deducted for cards you are unable to stack successfully. You spend the game stacking your patties, and then "eat your burger" to determine your final score.

-description from designer

Challenge an artificially simulated player!

Get ready for a new challenge in Brazil: Imperial. The Autômato is an implacable adversary that will put all his experience as the leader of his nation to the test.

It is possible to use the Autômato in solo matches or add it to maps with more players, which helps to create a new gameplay, with an unpredictable opponent that does not accept negotiation!

Brazil: Imperial - Automato is a new module for Brazil: Imperial containing 21 new cards.

—description from the publisher

A competitive fast-paced dungeon crawling game for two to four players.  Game plays in 30-45 minutes.

Each game begins with a maze generated from randomly drawn dungeon cards.  The players take turns exploring and moving through the maze.  They can play cards from their hand to replace explored tiles to make it easier for them to escape or hinder the competing players.

The first to recover the magical Chalice and escape is the winner.

Play one of four different characters with unique attack and defense abilities to help you survive the minotaur’s fury.

-description from publisher

Daiquiri is a middle-weight domino climbing game for 3 or 4 players.

You will need 3 or 4 sets of double-six dominoes depending on the number of players.

There are no teams; it’s every person for themselves. Your score will depend solely on the position you go out so go out early.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Fiddich is a heavy-weight climbing game that uses 4 sets of double six dominoes and is played by strictly 4 players in fixed partnerships, 2 vs 2.

Each player is given a set of 7 blank tiles (their Glen). These tiles can be used to form ordinary combinations and they can be used to form bombs. They offer flexibility and power. Everyone has them. Use yours wisely.

Your goal is to shed 28 tiles (including your Glen).

The game is all about going out early as a team. The only team that will score is the team that goes out first, but their score will depend on when the rest of the team goes out. If your team does NOT go out first, your job is simple: make the other team go out as late as possible.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Both players get the same starting capital and agree on a fixed playing time. Each player gets one meeple and both start at the same starting space. Movement is like a rock in chess, any number of spaces in any orthogonal direction. Each space allows for a business transaction and the players use double entry bookkeeping to manage the ballace of their business portfolio.
At the end of the game the player with the highest profit wins the game.

The game was most likely published in the early 60s.

Escape the Lich King is a cooperative 20 card game of Dungeon escape through discovery and attack. A party of three stalwart adventures have overestimated their worth while exploring the nearest dungeon and find themselves desperate to escape alive.

Pursued by hordes of monsters and the Big Boss, the party must find their lost weapons while fending off the horde.  To escape the dungeon, they must each find one of three potions that will help teleport them out of the dungeon.  If enough monsters set on the trail of the party, they notify the Big Boss who moves in for the final attack to stop them from escaping.

If the party can fend off the monsters while fleeing the dungeon and all drink the three potions together, they can escape the dungeon with their lives.
   
Player Win conditions:
The three players win if they each have a character card, a matching weapon card and a potion card showing.  Then all three players will drink from their potions during the Cleanup Phase to teleport out of the dungeon and win the game.

Big Boss win conditions:
If the total value of all revealed monsters is greater than 10 at the end of the Battle Phase, the players lose and the Big Boss player wins.

-description from designer

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: Gangs of Night City –  Families and Outcasts
description:
The Families and Outcasts expansion adds the 7th district, the Badlands, to the board — a new region for exploration during the game. It also brings 1 brand-new story and 2 more gangs to the game, the Aldecaldos and the Wraiths, also increasing the game's maximum player count to 5!

The Badlands district board is a 4-fold game board that is attached to the bottom right corner of Night City, mirroring the geography of the Cyberpunk 2077 in-game location.

—description from the publisher

Narcopolis is an immersive and competitive board game. The game is played in the South of United States during the year 2000. It's a tactical game with bluffing. You have to be diplomatic and play dirty. Players light on the interactions and have fun around the table. The goal of Narcopolis is to become the new boss of the town. To become the new Boss, you have to be the one with the highest rate of reputation at the end of the Game. The Game finishes either when someone arrive to 20 points of reputation or after 10 turns.

You will play one of 5 characters and will evolve within a city where you will have to attack, conquer and develop resources to gain reputation points. Bluffing, corruption and strategy are key elements of the game.You will have to build casinos, weapons and other resources specific to the gang world to take control of the city.

Each turn every player have to play 2 obligatory actions (take money and move) and can do 5 more actions (play or buy influence card, do or exchange job, develope new Business, conquer a new area by fighting or just Build up new Business, Buy or sale reputation point).  To take territories to an enemy you have to play a dice fight which include a bluffing part. Every round turn the first player will revel a new event card which changes the game that round.

-description from writer

The Kickstarter Exclusive Edition comes with:

- Casting Shadows Base Game in Kickstarter Exclusive Edition Box with alternate art, a magnetic box upgrade, and a removable sleeve with the standard edition art. The box is upgraded to accomodate sleeved cards as well as cards and components in the Ice Storm Expansion Pack.

- 2 Kickstarter Exclusive Player Boards (Iris Papillon / Iris The Obscure; Tusk Swiftwave / Tusk The Tidemaster)

- 3 Kickstarter Exclusive Hex Tiles (Glimmering Woods; Coral Ridge; Crystal Crypt)

- 15 Kickstarter Exclusive standard-sized cards:

     4 Kickstarter Exclusive Companions (Vital Shroom; Dark Flutter; Lil' Shadow; Hammerhead Harvester)
     3 Kickstarter Exclusive Resource cards (Purple Orb; 2 Shadow Fragment; Purple Gem)
     3 Kickstarter Exclusive Attack Spells (Coral Katana; Venomous Strike; Warp Drop)
     2 Kickstarter Exclusive Conversion Spells (Gem Alchemy; Orb Alchemy)
     3 Kickstarter Exclusive Counterspells (Worst Curse; Unexpected Attack; Fly Away!)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
This small promo expansion adds 3 new Characters cards to play with the base game.

B1: Carry out the action of 1 available Rabbi tile.

B2: Move your Golems a total of 2 steps & move your marker up
the Study track I step & activate 1 Golem (lay it down).

B3: Activate all your Golems (either standing or laying) without laying them down OR earn 5 VP.

This is the "Expanded Edition" which was originally available on Kickstarter. It comes with two expansions, the Emigration and Arsenic Expansion, that are not found in the retail edition. They can be obtained in Tinners' Trail: Expansions Box.


In Tinners' Trail, set in 19th century Cornwall, you represent a mining conglomerate at the height of the tin and copper mining industry. You must buy plots of land across Cornwall in auctions and survey them for tin and copper, always managing your "work points" and money effectively.

Once you have a mine in place, it's time to extract the ore and (ideally) make a profit, but the deeper your mine goes, the more expensive the process gets. To reduce the cost of mining, you can place developments, such as ports, train stations, and adits (drainage tunnels), but there's only so many improvements to go around. Once you have made your money — trying to time the market to sell when prices are high — you can invest it in industries outside of Cornwall, which gains you victory points. The earlier you invest, the better the return. Can you outplay the competition and make the most money, or will you be left without two shillings to rub together?

What's changed since 2008?
Increased player count - a game that was once only 3-4 players can now be enjoyed by 1-5 players!

2 new expansions - the Arsenic expansion, which introduces a new resource, and the Emigration expansion, which sees miners travel overseas.

Reduced randomness - but still keeping the variability. The board is now set up with tiles instead of the roll of a the dice.

Improved Auctions - dual-use cards are now an important part of the game, giving you information before an auction or an extra boost after an auction.

Improved artwork and graphic design

Improved component quality - with custom meeples and colour blind friendly colours.

Lord Wallace solo mode - a brand new solo mode.

A combinatorial abstract strategy game played on a square grid.

Players alternate placing stones on empty spaces.

Capture diagonally sandwiched enemy stones (custodian capture), flipping them to your color.

After the board is full, whoever has the longest straight "bar" in their color - horizontal for white, vertical for black - wins. Ties cascade to second longest, third longest, etc.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – Ice and Fire Draft Starter
description:
All cards in this Draft Starter have a set symbol designating it as part of this Draft Starter; a white snowflake imposed over a red sun. All draft card sets are manufactured through Fantasy Flight Games's in-house print-on-demand method, and are packaged in a clear plastic clamshell.

From Fantasy Flight Games's item description page:

"The Ice & Fire Draft Starter features 22 fixed cards that serve as the foundations for your Ice & Fire draft decks. An essential mix of House cards, locations, and plot cards accompany rules for draft play and a “draft only” agenda. The Ice & Fire Draft Starter is made available to you via Fantasy Flight Games in-house manufacturing, and you may reuse the contents of your Ice & Fire Draft Starter through any number of Ice & Fire draft events."

Note that the total card count is 25 cards; 19 game cards, 3 double-sided house cards (one house on each side), and three rules and draft information cards.

To play, each player involved must have one Draft Starter AND its matching Draft Pack. I will add the Draft Pack and the second Draft Starter and Draft Pack if this item is entered into the database.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Take control of one of six unique Corporations and vie for dominance in the Alpha System by utilizing a mix of different actions and resources. Focus on building up your infrastructure and set yourself up for a strong finish or go for fast and immediate rewards. Use your Influence in the council chamber to make sure things progress according to your wishes, or resort to outright aggression to dominate the competition.

A New Frontier is a highly engaging conflict game where your ability to gauge your opponents is key to victory. Since players can interact with all other players, regardless of position, conflicts are plentiful, but also quite undramatic. There is no player elimination, and you also cannot “gang-up” on someone, at least not from a conflict perspective. Conflicts are multi-phased, with ample opportunity to outsmart your opponents.

A New Frontier has nearly no hidden info (the obvious exception being player fleets) and few random elements, and with only 5 turns every decision counts. The goal of the game is to become the most dominant Corporate Republic in the Alpha System, which is achieved by having the most Victory Points by game’s end. All types of actions can potentially lead to Victory Points allowing for multi-pronged strategies. In addition to Actions and Credits, players must balance Prestige, which represents their organization’s goodwill and reputation, and that is essential for certain aspects of the game.

The game is centered around four ore-rich planets, with each player being present at every planet. This is where players harvest ore for income, build ships and upgrade mines, as well as carry out attacks against each other. In addition, there are four support areas/phases (Exploration, Politics, Marketing and R & D), where players can choose to diversify in order to gain advantages and Victory Points. Each Turn starts with a Planning Phase where players hiddenly set their strategic plan for the whole turn by using the resources at their disposal. Players then make tactical decisions during each Action Phase within the framework of their strategic plan.

Depending on chosen strategy, a player will normally amass between 30-60% of their Victory Points during the game, and the rest during end game scoring.

boardgame: Travels in the Land of Glyph
description:
This game uses the Glyph deck to simulate a story-telling, problem-solving experience with simple matching mechanics.

During gameplay, the player will resolve obstacles by matching them with cards that share the same minor suits and that make narrative sense. Items will be picked up and placed in an inventory; characters can be befriended and join the player for a while.

An attempt to recreate the feel of a classic computer graphic adventure game using only cards, relying on your imagination to do most of the work. Also see Quest for the MacGlyphin for a more game-y, goal-oriented approach at a similar goal.

—description from the designer

The players are young Astronauts and they have the important mission to visit unknown planets and make contact with their inhabitants.
They depart from Earth, and by rolling dices, they start the journey. On each planet visited they need to learn the typical gestures of each native civilization.
During the journey, our travelers can find intriguing space curiosities.
The Astronaut who manages to return to Earth first will be the the winner

This is based on a true story: for 16 years, the town of Kringa has been terrorized by an evil Strigoi named Jure Grando. While the families struggle to survive day-to-day, they must gather the courage and tools to defeat the undead fiend!

Kringa is a cooperative horror worker-placement game with a strong push-your-luck component.

Players start with a family of four workers - each with unique abilities. Assign workers to locations to collect resources. Use resources to craft helpful items, hire helpers and overcome dangers. As dusk descends, you can choose to risk danger and stay to gather more resources, or return home to feed and protect your family with blessings and hexes.

As night falls, Grando stalks Kringa. If Grando stops at a location or home, unprotected workers will fall sick or even placed in the village's crypt. If the crypt fills up, the players lose.

Players have a limited time to discover how to destroy Grando by fulfilling the secret holder's objectives. Once a secret is discovered, you will learn one of Grando's weaknesses. Ready the weapons and set up a trap to lure Grando in. If you manage to successfully spring the trap and defeat the evil Strigoi, you win! If you fail, you doom Kringa to a fate worse than death!

—description from the designer

In this simple memory, matching game for very small children, you are a baby Otter, trying to eat as many delicious treats as you can as you swim home to Momma Otter.

Race up the creek, looking for tasty treats. Find a pair and SPLASH! you move towards Momma!

The player with the most pairs of treats, at the end of the game, wins!

—description from the designer

Have the craziest conversation of your life – and provide unforgettable moments to be ashamed with a lack of beat, disturbing behaviour and selected behaviour for coming off as the weird daddy joke-uncle.
 ABSURD SMALLTALK - Make extremely fun conversations with multiple conversation topics per round - at the same time! Score points through thoughtful listening and creative communication

HIDDEN IDENTITY - Play with hidden roles and secret missions that you don't know at the beginning of the round. Even about your own role you first have to find out due to detective sense.

COOPERATION OR BLUFF - If you have pulled the same conversation card, you play in the team and need to find each other. As Mimic Octopus you are about to lie and manipulate and that way secretly sneak into a team and steal points.

Card game without plastic - all films are biodegradable. Board game with 110 cards for 4-8 people, from 12 years, playing time approx. 30 minutes. Can be combined with all Mimic Octopus editions.

Lead the most crazy talk of your life – and enchant each other with desperate urge for making a move, soppy romance and erotic moments you would actually rather like to do without.

ABSURD SMALLTALK - Make extremely fun conversations with multiple conversation topics per round - at the same time! Score points through thoughtful listening and creative communication

HIDDEN IDENTITY - Play with hidden roles and secret missions that you don't know at the beginning of the round. Even about your own role you first have to find out due to detective sense.

COOPERATION OR BLUFF - If you have pulled the same conversation card, you play in the team and need to find each other. As Mimic Octopus you lie and manipulate in order to secretly sneak into a team and steal points.

Card game without plastic - all films are biodegradable. Board game with 110 cards for 4-8 people, from 12 years, playing time approx. 30 minutes. Can be combined with all Mimic Octopus editions.

This sequel to 2020's Double Masters reprises the themes of powerful reprints and extra rares in every pack. Double Masters 2022 contains 331 reprints from across Magic's history (91 commons, 80 uncommons, 120 rares, 40 mythic rares) and is sold in draft boosters and collector boosters. Each 16-card draft booster is guaranteed to contain 2 foils and 2–4 rare or mythic rare cards, and in a booster draft, players take 2 cards on their first pick from each pack rather than 1. Each 15-card collector booster is guaranteed to contain 12 foils and 4 rare or mythic rare cards (3 foil and 1 non-foil).

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar (Third Edition): Chaos Battletome – Skaven
description:
For centuries beyond counting has the race of malevolent ratmen known as the skaven plagued the Mortal Realms. Arrogant, avaricious and utterly without mercy, the skaven seek to annihilate every other sapient creature – expanding, multiplying, and consuming everything in their path until they alone rule over the decaying husk of reality. Those who defy this vision are overwhelmed by swarms of frenzied, red-eyed warriors, weaponized plagues, monstrous beasts and lightning-spitting war machines. Arise, Children of the Great Horned Rat!

Within these pages you'll find everything you need to know to command verminous Skaven hordes in your games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. Pore over the treacherous history of the Horned Rat's many attempts to conquer the Mortal Realms, learn about the internecine squabbling of the various Great Clans, and delve into the gnawhole-riddled depths of their teeming Under-Empire.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar General's Handbook Pitched Battles 2022-23 Season 1
description:
This essential supplement gives you new ways to enjoy games of competitive Warhammer Age of Sigmar for the first season of 2022-23, including updated battleplans and Pitched Battle profiles for every unit and army. This pack includes two books: the 96-page spiral-bound hardback General’s Handbook, and the 34-page softcover Pitched Battle Profiles book.

Inside this season's General’s Handbook, you'll find:


     An introduction to setting up Pitched Battles for matched play games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, including guidance on the Battle Ready standard of painting for your miniatures, and a comprehensive list of the official publications which can be used in Pitched Battles
     Detailed background information for Gallet, the wind-blasted continent of Ghur where the action takes place. You’ll learn all about this arid landscape, the beasts that dwell there, and the tunnels underneath it
     Region of War rules for Gallet, the Splintered Land, with special rules that focus on empowering Battleline troops, a terrifying new Realmsphere spell for all Wizards, and an aggressive Realm Command available to all players

AdvanceQuest is a compact adventure system. Generate a character with any traits you want, and advance your way through the story, rolling dice against challenges and trying to reach the end.

A new chapter in the AdvanceQuest story is coming.

Using a clever new exploration mechanic, each foray into the story will offer different challenges using a simple combination of two elements. Select two of your own traits and roll off against the two story elements, and see if you can beat the dangerous situation. If you roll well, you survive to continue on. If you fail, you will take damage. But don't despair – you can spend Stamina to re-roll dice before you apply their effects! If you can make it through the story, you win! Your score is how many treasures you manage to collect along the way – see if you can beat your high score by playing again!

Spring is coming and the little birds must build their nests. You can help them! Gather the twigs and place the floors so they can build their colonies. There are endless possibilities! Cooperative 3D construction game.

Long ago, dragons and giants fought a thousand-year war...and the human survivors commemorated these battles by adding the giants to their high stakes card game of luck and skill, Three-Dragon Ante.

Three-Dragon Ante: Legendary Edition – Giants War includes more than one hundred cards, featuring a new way to find legendary dragons, new giant gods, and new giant suits that let you split the stakes in two: the dragon stakes and the giants stakes. Players fight for the stakes where their flight is strongest, giving them new routes to victory.

description:
In StarDriven: Gateway, 1-4 players each control their own starship and crew while determining the fate of distant colonies near an ancient alien gateway. The game can be played competitively, co-operatively, or in solo mode depending on which episode you select at game set-up.

Your objective is to be the most renowned and respected Crew by gaining the most points. You gain points by increasing your reputation with the Council Worlds, promoting your Crew, researching Anomalies, recruiting Freelancers, and completing Missions. All of these brave endeavors will score you points and lead to your victory. Running your starship entails assigning crew to different stations on your ship board, which allows you to assign dice to those stations to activate ship actions. Move and explore space, encounter raiders, face anomalies, and use your crew abilities and skills to fulfill mission requirements and activate their bonuses. Manage your ship's power, weapons, and shields, and recruit and promote crew members to enhance your ship and achieve objectives to ensure your side prevails.

Each round of the game, each player takes a turn in clockwise order. On your turn, you must take 2 actions (and any free actions that you want) before ending your turn by passing or spending 1 to recruit a freelancer or promote a Crew on your ship. The round ends with each player Activating the Warbound. When any end game condition is met, players calculate their final score to determine who wins.

—description from the designer

description:
Fallen Flags is a railroad-themed deck building game in which players invest in the early American railroads, lay track, and run trains with the aim of having the highest score after the game end is triggered.

Players begin with a base deck of capital, Resources (an abstraction of steel, fuel, labor, and other things needed for railroad operations), and stock in two of the game's four railroads.  Cards played from a player's hand may be used to conduct Railroad Actions (laying track and moving trains) and to raise capital to purchase more railroad stock and better cards.

Fallen Flags combines the familiar deck building mechanics of games like Star Realms and Dominion with those of railroad games, such as the "18XX" games or "cube rails" games.

Do you have what it takes to be a railroad baron?

—description from designer

description:
Team de Torite is a team-base trick taking game for 4, 5, or 6 players.  Much of the game resembles a must-follow, no-trump, partnership game with bidding for the number of tricks a team will win.  However, the game has a few twists.

In addition to a base of 4 suits, the game includes a fifth "special" suit of cards which constitute the trump cards and the lowest value card.  Trump cards of the same value will cancel each other in a trick if present in even numbers, leaving the last one played to win a trick if an odd number are played.

The teams place their bets on a board where if one team bids a high number, the other team will be limited from bidding a specific low number.  Additionally, each of the values a team can bid has a multiplier for how it will pay out, and the teams are placing chips face down on the different spaces, giving a different weight to their confidence in each of their bets.

The team with the most points when one team reaches 150 points wins.

Originally available during the Kickstarter for Helsinki, the Queenie Box contains Copenhagen: New Facades and Copenhagen: Nyhavn.

These expansions were originally shipped separately and in their own ziplock bag during the Copenhagen Kickstarter campaign.

Indoor Tennis is an interesting mix of darts and the game of tennis. Produced in Australia during the War years, the game sees two players standing either end of a miniature tennis court holding bomb shaped darts ready to throw towards each other.

The court is similar to traditional tennis courts with each side having 3 sections, with a larger "Center Court" and two smaller "Left and Right Service boxes", close to the net.

A player (the server) throws their bomb shaped dart into the others court. If successful the other player (receiver) returns a dart. If the receivers dart lands in the opponents same boxed area they win the point. I.e. Server throws dart into Left Service box of receiver. Receiver then successfully throws their dart into left service box of Server. If the receiver is unsuccessful the point goes to the server.

The game follows the scoring of traditional tennis i.e 15:30, deuce and faults. Play is stopped after an agreed time with the winner winning the most games.

—user summary

Cozy Juicy Real is a conversation-driven board game that deepens connections through fun, engaging, and meaningful interactions. Perfect for game nights, this game brings players closer by sparking conversations that go beyond the surface level—without ever feeling awkward or forced. 

Players take turns answering a wide range of questions that fall into three categories: Cozy (light and fun), Juicy (thought-provoking), and Real (insightful and revealing), making every round different and every experience unique. It’s an introvert- and extrovert-friendly experience - with challenges, fun twists, and collaborative gameplay.

Designed for 3-8 players aged 14 and up, Cozy Juicy Real is perfect for casual gamers and non-gamers alike, and game sessions can range from a quick 30 minutes to a more in-depth 120 minutes.

“There are some really great thought-provoking questions in here and I love the interaction with other players. A great social game to play with friends and to make new ones.”
-Adam, Die Rollin’ Game Reviews

Winner of the 2024 Sparks Play Award for Connection

Following a blackout,  in Neustart you and the other members of a municipal crisis management team need to protect a small town from chaos and destruction and provide the population with the best possible care. You don't know how long it will be before power can be restored. Dealing with uncertainty and insecurity, possibly with failures and losses, the situation will make enormous demands on you.

Each round of a game is a complete day, broken into day and night. Different events happen in day and night, with these events becoming more serious and all-encompassing as the days progress. Initially everyone still has enough to eat, but people might be stuck in elevators, and accidents or fires can occur as the population handles open flame to cook.

Over time, food will run out, people will need more care, and the population needs to be informed about what measures the administration is taking. To solve these problems, the players control five teams — fire brigade, police, rescue service, construction, and administration — each with three members. Each team has important skills, but they can also crossover to handle other tasks, with the construction team also being able to put out fires, for example.

Teams can be used only for a certain amount of work, after which they need to recover. Players must act under time pressure and a lack of information, having only a few minutes to decide how the teams will act, with anything unaddressed remaining undone — and unfortunately unresolved tasks deteriorate the mood of the population, possibly leading to riots and chaos. Teams and time are limited, though, so you must set priorities and ration where needed.

The game lasts 4-7 days, and you can determine how well you do based on the damage suffered by the population and area.

Combining positional strategy, with a card based attack/resource system, ZEL'LoTE is a game that is never the same game twice. Strategy, tactics, and fate; all will play a hand in victory, but when it comes right down to it, you'll probably need a miracle.

boardgame: Winnie the Pooh in the Honey Heist
description:
Overview: Help Winnie the Pooh and his friends collect honey from the beehives

Players: 1 - 100+
Game Time: 25 minutes
Items needed: 2 six-sided dice

The game is played over 12 rounds, with players playing simultaneously, trying to earn the most points by placing numbers into runs and sets in each beehive. One die will be used for Winnie the Pooh, while the other die will be one of his friends coming to help. All players will use the same dice pool for each roll, however each player determines how to use the dice.

Gameplay: Each Round you will roll 2 dice and then use each die to place a number in any open hexagon in any beehive. One die will be used for Winnie the Pooh, using the number on the die for a hexagon space. The other die will be used for a Friend, which will also place a number in an open hexagon space. The goal is to make number runs and/or sets of the same number in each beehive. The longer the run or the larger the set, the more points you will earn.

Round Summary:

- Roll 2 dice - Roll the 2 dice. Write the numbers in the current round’s track. The round tracker is just used as a helpful reference. (Over a game you will mark 24 numbers, filling all the beehives. It will be 2 numbers each round, over the 12 rounds)

- Use one die for Winnie the Pooh
     Choose one of the dice for Winnie the Pooh. Write the number on the die in any open hexagon space in any beehive.

- Use one die for a Friend
     Use the other die for one of Pooh’s friends, using whatever their special ability is. Some friends will give you a choice between two numbers. Some friends will look at Pooh’s die and then make another number based off of that number. Write the number in any open hexagon in any beehive. Also, mark an X over the leftmost heart on the friendship track under the friend you chose. Note: You may choose a friend more than three times, there just is not any additional bonus.

- Repeat this until you have completed 12 rounds

Scoring: The goal is to make number runs and/or sets of the same number in each beehive. The longer the run or the larger the set, the more points you will earn. You can use each number you wrote once for a run and once for a set. You can use the number already in the center of each beehive in multiple runs or sets. A run is three or more numbers that are connected and continuously increasing by one. A set is a group of two or more numbers that are connected and all the same number.

End of Game:
Total all your runs, sets, furthest star on the honey/bee track, and also all stars earned from friends. Total these points and the highest score wins.

It's December 15th, Christmas is almost upon us, but the house is not ready to celebrate yet!
The tree is in its place ... but it's bare!

In these 10 days you will have to decorate your tree in the best possible way, filling it with lights and balls to make it the most beautiful in the city!
But it won't be easy ... as it has to shine the same colors both night and day, the balls you place on the tree will have the same color of the light in that spot.
Remember not to close the curtains! If Santa sees a ball he likes, he will reward you with gifts and decorations!

The game consists of placing the various Christmas lights tiles on the tree.
Depending on the predominant color on each single intersection of the various triangles, players will be able to place balls of a color, ornaments for the room or receive gifts from Santa Claus. All this will allow players to earn points to win the match.

Who will be able to make the most beautiful tree? 
We will find out only after unwrapping the gifts!

Not every shape in the shadows is a rival investigator; there are a host of other dark figures skulking around the mansion. Each has their own purpose, and the tools to accomplish the job.

In this 4-card expansion, you will receive 4 double sided role cards (2 for each player, allowing them to choose any of the 4 roles - including the same role as their opponent). The roles introduce new challenges and provide new abilities, which will mix up games in a fun new way.

In Pilgrims, a trick-taking card game with a number of twists, players are collecting groups of villagers to go on pilgrimage together, but only the most impressive bands of pilgrims get to be commissioned by the bishop and gain prestige!

In each round, two (or sometimes one) sites of pilgrimage are on offer, and players try to build the most impressive band of pilgrims by playing cards of the same suit. Lower value cards may be used to provide relics or manuscripts to add to the prestige gained from visiting a particular holy site. You may even be able to go on pilgrimage on your own as the hermit!

When you go on pilgrimage, you receive a scoring card and discard all the cards you played. Players going home discard only one of the cards they played. Scoring cards give you 5-10 victory points, and there are chances to add to these using lower-value cards you play.

The more you go on pilgrimage, the smaller your band of followers becomes, making it more difficult to compete against your opponents, so you need to choose when to admit defeat and go home early in the hope of attracting more impressive followers to your cause.

The game is over when thirty pilgrimages have taken place, which typically means 16-20 rounds of play.

This game has been in development since 2004, and now is in final stages of refining the artwork.

-description from designer

Here's the magical items expansion. At just two cards, it'll give players a variety of items to use, but there's a catch - any item that you use in round 1 is available to your opponent in the next round.

Here's the solo play. This has been a long requested expansion to Wonder Tales and has been working its way around for a while now. It's basically just a ruleset, but we are making it nicer with 4 cards that can stay out in play.

Discover The Way is a strategic game that requires adaptation, planning, risk vs reward choices, and luck. Each player builds the world as the game is played and the decisions start off right away. Players need to decided if they are going to defend, attack, upgrade, or a combination.

Each player has a relic to grab from another player’s board which means that they will have two relics from other players on their board. Each player also has customizable dice that can be upgraded during game play. The boards start empty and as the players move and do discoveries the world begins to fill out. The first player to retrieve both of their relics and bring them back to their base wins.

On your turn you will roll three dice (more can be purchased later). Each die has one gold, one wood, one stone, and three movement. You can only bank the gold and the others need to be used that turn. Anything rolled could be used for movement instead of the resources rolled. You use movement to create pathways to your relics. You can use gold to discover more gold, wood, and stone Tetris like pieces which can also be used to block other players. After you make a discovery you can take that resource and modify your dice to improve your future rolls. Other combinations of resources are used to build walls, bridges, and more soldiers. When you have your relic you can only move at half speed to bring it back to base. After someone drops both relics off at base the game immediately ends with that person as victor.

-description from designer
