Summer is here and that means one thing for you and your crab buddies: Scuttleball! Scuttleball is just like volleyball but for crabs! You’ll need skill, luck, and guts if you want to win this tricky little dice game.

Scuttleball is a 2-player dice-placement game that fits in a mint tin. Despite its small size, short rules, and bunch of dice, it offers a surprising amount of tense tactical moments and thematic tie-ins. At the beginning of each round, or volley, both players roll nine dice concealed behind their hands. Players alternate making a play on the ball by placing a die on their side of the court that exceeds the value of the last played die. The volley continues until one player can't make a play. Here's the exciting part: every space on the court has a special action that is activated when a die is placed. Actions are things like reroll a die, boost its value, or mirror an adjacent die. You’ll have to activate the right ability at the right time if you want to keep the volley going!

Scuttleball contains three different courts to accommodate different skill levels and game preferences: backyard (casual), beach (standard), or gymnasium (expert). There is also a free downloadable file to design your own court!

description:
An autobiographical game about the real face of poverty.

Playing solo, you need to balance your physical, emotional, social and financial wellbeing while making the rent each month.

This game asks players what they need to sacrifice in order to pay their rent. The difficult decisions that come with the cost of living versus living a good life. Will you be able to have savings? Maintain relationships? Afford basic necessities? A serious game about what it means to survive.

boardgame: Ginormopod 2050 A.D.: Attack of the Giant Bug Monsters
description:
GASP! at a startling vision of the world yet to come! CHEER! for the brave people of tomorrow fighting a desperate struggle for survival! SCREAM! at uncanny creatures too horrifying to describe, the children of the nuclear age who now wish to claim our dying planet as their unholy birthright!

As waters and temperatures rise, and coastlines sink, gigantic insects sweep across the western United States. The only thing holding them back is the Fusion Fence built along the mighty Mississippi. But will it buy us enough time to complete work on the anti-mutagen – humanity's last hope? Ginormopod 2050 A.D.: Attack of the Giant Bug Monsters is a retro-futuristic treat, presenting a 2050 as it might have been imagined by Hollywood a hundred years earlier.

One player controls American forces and their allies, choosing carefully where to defend, when to fall back, and how to counterattack. Their resources are limited, and every decision has a cost. Their opponent controls the Swarm, throwing an endlessly respawning supply of mutated ants, spiders, and worms.

Will humanity survive?

description:
Use the powers of your koalas to get rid of your cards first to win! Invented by Sofia and Luca, two siblings under 10 who mostly get along.

Get everyone to the table! Koala Kraze is for 2-8 players ages 6 and up, and you'll learn to play in 2 minutes. The game's 100 cards fit in a small box, so you can take it with you anywhere you go!

We commissioned some amazing hand-painted koalas just for this game, and their personalities burst through the cards!

Ready for Extreme mode? Start with the Junior mode to keep it simpler for younger kids, or try Extreme mode for older kids and adults!

Koala Kraze is a family-friendly game where the goal is to use your Koala Friends to discard all your cards first. If you've played Uno, Crazy Eights, or any other card-shedding game, you already know how to play!

Shuffle the deck, deal some cards out, and you're ready to play! Each turn, you'll play a Number card or a Koala card. Number cards can be played if they're one above or one below the Number card on the discard pile. If a Koala card's on top, play any number card you like! Each Koala card has a special power to help you play or discard more cards. Use the Burglar to switch hands with another player, or play the Big Boss to make a player discard a Koala card.

You can play two modes with the same cards. The simpler 'Junior' version is all about getting rid of your cards first, which is great for younger kids. A game like this is great for teaching colors and numbers, or for recognizing colors and numbers as the game gets fast!

If playing with older kids or adults, the 'Extreme' version adds a bit more challenge while keeping the core gameplay the same. As you play, pay attention to the scoring icons which say 'plus' or 'minus'. Sometimes the card you want to play will cost you points, so be careful! Some Koalas (like the Disk Jockey) will help discard those cards so they won't hurt your score, and a waypoint system ensures you can't go below certain milestones once you've achieved them.

—description from the publisher

The game board consists of four pyramids broken down into individual small pyramids. At the beginning of the game, each player makes a throw and places his piece on the small square of one of the four pyramids that has the same number. This space, occupied at the beginning, forms the foundation stone of the pyramid to be built by the player and the number indicated on it shows the "base number". The player who rolled the base number 1 is king or queen and begins. There can be multiple kings in the first game. However, if no 1 is thrown, the next lower number begins. Corresponding to the rolling, the game piece is then placed as many spaces further sideways as eyes have been thrown. When the end of a row is reached, the player moves on to the next higher row until he reaches the top of the pyramid. If a player throws his basic number, he is not allowed to move, but rather has to wait until it is his turn again. If the player rolls more points than he needs to fill the capstone, he must move back from the top of the pyramid by as many squares as he has rolled points too many. This is repeated until he manages to get to the keystone. The king or queen make an exception to this. These may occupy the capstone immediately as soon as they reach it or have rolled beyond it. Whoever is the first to finish building his pyramid receives the first prize and becomes king for the next game with the basic number 1. Whoever throws the basic number 1 in the next game must exchange it for 2, since the king number is then only one player may own. If several players finish in a round, the one who threw the lowest cornerstone at the beginning of the game has priority, i. h. started building the pyramid with 1 or the next lowest square.
Mix these 100 cards in with your Joking Hazard game for a bigger, girthier deck. Size matters, enhance your deck.

The classic game of jacks has been reinvented for a new space and time. This is Galactic Jaxx! Work to fill up the empty star spaces on your constellation cards to earn points and abilities. Spin the Drop Zone Spinner for an added challenge to your grab. Bounce the ball and try to grab the correct number of Jaxx.

Each turn players try to complete their Constellation Cards by collecting and placing the correct colors of Jaxx onto their cards. In order to collect Jaxx players have to bounce the Space Ball and grab Jaxx before the ball hits the table a second time. A player's Star Gear will tell them how many Jaxx they need to collect in a turn. Take too many or too few Jaxx and your turn is unsuccessful; drop all of those Jaxx back into the center. The more a player succeeds the higher their Star Gear Tracker will go, but one wrong move and their Star Gear goes back to the start.

Every successfully grabbed Jaxx a player gets goes onto their Constellation Card. Each constellation card requires a certain number and set of colors to complete. Once all those requirements are met, that player scores their card and gets a new Constellation. The first player to get 15 or more points, WINS!

–description from designer

Once upon a time dodos were living carefree in the realm of Draghan. Especially the little dragons saw the funny birds as cuties, but everything changed when the Dutchmen arrived. With their ships they brought many animals that neither the dragons nor the dodos had set their eyes on before: rats, dogs, pigs... All these creatures hunted the dodos and ransacked their eggs. Unworldly and peaceful in mind, the dodos hadn’t been able to stand their ground against these reckless invaders. Can you help the dodos to hatch as many eggs as possible and save them from extinction?

'O Dodo!' is a push your luck game in which you must constantly choose between low risk and high reward. During your turn you alternate in playing a card from your hand and a blind card from the draw pile. You add the cards to one of the nests and the value of the cards you play must go higher and higher. Make the nests as big as you can, so you can hatch as many eggs as possible at the end of your turn. But also know when to stop. When the card you draw from the draw pile is too low or when your hand is empty and you can't hatch the eggs in one of the nests your turn is over and you’ll see another player profit of your hard work.

Thanks to the many included variants, the dodos continue to surprise and challenge you even after a long time. A few of the included variants:
- Add ruse cards that will activate a special effect.
- Gather the loot so you can return cards from the discard pile to your hand.
- Add effects to the nests that you can use once during your turn to activate a special effect.
- ...

The game also includes a solo variant and a solo campaign where you have to earn enough points each game to buy a ruse card that you can use from the next game on. The more ruse cards you buy the more chances you have to increase your score. Try to unlock them all and go for the magical score of 100 points to save the dodos from extinction!

-description from publisher

Rather than risk their agent being discovered by the Frontists by bringing in Inquisition agents from all over the city, they have decide to risk the lives of an expendable group of mercenaries. Of course, the question of payment rears its ugly head, and rather than chop it off (the usual Inquisition tactic), the mecenaries will be paid.

In this turn-base strategy game, you and up to three Mage companions savvy in an elemental form of magic work together to ascent the Tower of Rogue Mana. Mages will battle devious enemies and tower chaos magic to find treasures and new spells. As a collective, the Mages work to ascend the Tower and receive the highest marks of wizardry!

Rogue Mana is a classic dungeon-crawler, great for newbies and veteran gamers alike. The growth of power format allows Rogue Mana to be played as a campaign over seven sessions. During each session, the Mages enter a new floor of the Tower that they must conquer until they defeat the 7th and final floor to receive their marks. There are 14 levels for variation and deployability.

During each turn, players collect Mana Crystals which are used to cast spells in your hand. These spells may harm an enemy, allow you to assist a fellow player in need, or manipulate the environment around you. As players draw mana or cast spells, a Rogue Event may be triggered by the Tower, which is known for its chaotic tendencies. Fate (i.e., dice) then decides whether a boon is given the mages, spikes fly out of the wall, or a barrel of fish randomly appears (for example). Once the players turn is done, then the enemies have their turn. They target the mages with unending ambition, though they may have weaknesses that the mages can exploit.

In Rogue Mana, each floor has its own unique layout, enemies, and challenges. Beyond just surviving, the mages can try to collect 3 additional accolades (up to 4 total); two by collecting specific treasures, and one for collecting a magical orb. The more accolades collected upon completing the level, the more talented and powerful wizards they will become once they defeat the tower.

The campaign/game ends once the mages defeat the final level in the tower.

—description from the publisher

Each player pays 5 coins in the till and take turns to roll and move. Only one die is used. If a player rolls 6 eyes, de don't move any further, but pays a penalty because he is too eager. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. Only the one who throws 1 and thus comes to space 1 can start the journey.
The first to the summit of the Zugspitze gets half the contents of the till. Those who have reached the summit can start their way back without any obstacles. Only those who get to 5 are eliminated from the game due to fatigue. Whoever gets over space 1 first gets all the rest of the till.

Each person has different elements of their personality, from introversion and extroversion; to different types of temperament, from composed to volatile.  In Emotional Intelligence, each player becomes a personality detective, and aims to deduce the personality traits of the other players before their own personality is revealed.  The catch: players can only provide subjective This or That clues regarding their personality type. The result is a fast-paced, sometimes contentious social deduction game perfect for players looking for both mystery and social examination.

HOW IT WORKS:

You’re given a unique personality at the beginning of the game - you will play from the perspective of this personality for duration of the game. Every turn, you will take turns asking other players to answer This or That questions based on one element of your given personality.  The clues, which are subjective This or That answers, may only be as helpful as how well you know the person. Is seeing the future Volatile or (K)omposed? Up to you!

You will be given a Deduction Pad, where you will jot down your notes and make your decisions on whether another player is more Extroverted or Introverted, Logical or EMpathetic, Concrete or Abstract, or Volatile or (K)omposed. Once you’re sure about all 4 elements of another player’s personality, you can instigate guessing their personality. Everyone else will guess as well, but you will get double the points for each correct guess.  Additionally, you get points when other people correctly guess your personality, so it's a win-win!

The game ends when everyone's personality has been investigated. The winner is the player with the most points.

—description from designer

Quick Urban Integrated Combat Kriegsspiel (QUICK) is a semi-abstract Tactical level board-wargame about opposing modern-day forces engaging in kinetic conflict in a large city. In the game, players take on the role of Division or Group Army commanders, vying to gain control of critical terrain within the city known as Objectives. To succeed, they must successfully manage Enablers, the array of skilled troops and machinery that exist to support and augment the power of the main Maneuver Units in the forces they command and maneuver these units for victory.

The game is played in rounds. Each round both players will draw or select colored cubes and then take turns using them to perform actions. The color of a cube determines what can be done, and with what unit. During play Enablers will be brought onto the battlefield or returned to it by being allocated to larger Maneuver Units, and these reinforced Maneuver Units will engage the enemy assisted by the special powers and abilities of their allocated Enablers.

The player who is best able to exploit the mechanisms and timing of movement, combat and appropriate design of task forces will win. The game system also permits construction of interesting scenarios.

—description from designer

Forest Run is an easy to learn, fun to play strategy game equally fun for kids, adults, and families. Below are the 5 quick steps to learn, master, and play this game


     Shuffle the game cards thoroughly and form the draw pile. Set aside spell cards and resource tokens to be earned by the players. The youngest player goes first
     On a player's turn, the player has two choices - either draw a game card from the draw pile and follow the instructions on the card or cast a spell card in possession and follow the instructions on the spell card
     Spell cards are earned two ways - either by stepping on the Spell tile or buying spell cards using the resource tokens in possession at the end of the turn. Resource tokens are earned by stepping on the Resource tiles
     The first player to cross the Victory tile wins
     Cage , Diffuser, Serenity spells can be played out of turn when the player steps on a Monster tile, Diffuser tile, to counter a Rage spell from an Opponent respectively

RockBottom is all about having fun while drinking and chatting with friends.

Two young college students came up with the first matching-card game that never repeats, and can get you drunk as fast or slow as you want to. The rules are simple, yet super entertaining.

Perfect game for groups of 5+, but three players will also do. Up to 10. Just grab a drink with friends and spend a funny evening chatting and drinking.

Have a great time playing with friends and share your playing-drinking experience with us!

Will you hit RockBottom?

In this game, the first player to collect four identical cards (e.g., 4 Rich Pig cards) wins the round and scores points. Watch out—there are a few more good conditions ?

Players drink a sip every round, except the winner. Each card type has its own score number printed in a corner, which determines how many sips your opponents must drink in a round and adds up to your game score. Whoever holds the Joker card must drink double in that round. The Joker card is always visible to others and, once received, it cannot be passed until the next round. The first player to score 25 or more points (if there are no threes and fours in the game, recommended for groups of 3-5 players) or 50 or more points (if there are threes and fours in the game, recommended for 5 or more players) wins the whole game and forces others to drink double. Whoever holds the Joker at the end of the game drinks quadruple. The Joker also reduces your score by -6 points (if there are no threes and fours in the game) or -8 points (if there are threes and fours in the game).

—description from the publisher

Brood is a polyhex-matching game for two players – Black and White – played on an initially empty hexagonal board.

Rules:
• On the very first turn, Black places one stone on any hex. 
• From then on, starting with White, the players take turns placing exactly two stones each on empty hexes. 
• A player is allowed to pass instead of placing their two stones. 
• When the players pass in succession, the game is over, and the player with the higher score wins.

Scoring and the definition of a brood:
• The score is a function of the players' broods. 
• A brood is a set of groups of the same shape, size and color.
• Shapes are considered identical even if they are rotated or mirrored.

Example: A black group consisting of 3 stones in a line makes up a brood together with all and only black groups of 3 in a line.

• The point value of a brood is the number of stones in it multiplied by the smallest of the following two numbers:
(i) The number of groups in it.
(ii) The size of the groups in it.

Example: If Black has 4 groups that are 3 stones in a line, this brood is worth 36 points. That's the number of stones in it (12) multiplied by the size of the groups in it (3).

—description from the designer

This game was inspired by various mini-games which used to be published now and again in White Dwarf Magazine.  I have a fondness for the Orc and Goblin Army in Warhammer Fantasy Battle. One of the most powerful units was the Goblin Ball and Chain Fanatics which were available to Night Goblin troops.  The inspiration and some of the base mechanics totally come from this game.  It is random as hell but would make a great convention game.

Every now and again, in-between Waaaagh’s, the Gobbo’s get restless – what better way to kill time than by setting the Ball and Chain Loonie’s loose in a make-shift arena?   Six goblin fanatics are placed into a small arena.  Once the fanatics are let loose nobody has much control, but carnage shall happen, and if that fails then perhaps exhaustion at the very least.

Don't take things too seriously.  Fanatics will die. Enjoy the ride.

This is a free to download game and can be found online inside the HeroHammer Fanzine in Issue #3.

A fast-paced tabletop wargame for 2-4 players (expandable to 8 players with two copies). This accessible game features army building, a real-time move phase, simultaneous player turns, dice rolling, and beautiful myth-based art all combining into destructive and action packed gameplay.

In Myth Wars: Battle for Midia, players build their own armies, then team up or engage in free-for-all combat, moving their units during the move phase, then rolling and assigning dice during the attack phase. The goal: destroy all opposing creatures. The last player or team standing wins the battle.

The game features double sided tokens for army building. Each side of the tokens (and their cards) features unique colors including the character art and as well as the token's border. This means that with two copies of the game, the player cap goes from 4 to 8, as each player is able to uniquely identify their own characters in their army.

The real-time move phase in conjunction with the thoughtful attack phase creates great pacing, as players jump back and forth between quick bursts of action, then thoughtful strategy and decision-making.

–description from publisher

The Magic Forest needs you!

The naughty trolls have mixed up the crystals that protect the magic forest. You and the other players must take them where they belong. Be careful – the trolls will try to get in your way, so pick your route carefully. The player, who rescues all three of their crystals first, wins the game!

–description from publisher's website

description:
In Mission Space players compete to be the first to get over 25 points by completing missions. Missions are either swinging around the Moon, orbiting the Moon, or landing on the Moon; the more difficult a mission is the more points are scored for successfully completing it. Points are also awarded for having fuel remaining and for rescuing players who run out of fuel.

The game is played by randomizing a mechanical "computer" and proceeding through a series of checkpoints - players landing on the Moon go through all checkpoints (there are 21) and players doing simpler missions skip some of them. At each checkpoint players simultaneously choose lettered navigational points and then the computer dial is turned to show the correct answer. Players who choose the correct answer skip one (in some cases two) checkpoints, while other players have to spend fuel to get to the correct location.

At certain checkpoints players consult a probability table to see how likely it is they can complete their missions given their remaining fuel. At these times they can choose to downgrade their missions.

A card game played over several hands where you are trying to empty your hand first.

To aid Heroes, Monsters of Nairu also adds a new deck of Mercenaries cards which produce resources and provide additional Victory Points.

Al-Khazneh is an Arabic word for treasury. Become an adventurer exploring the natural treasury, collect map pieces, discover the treasure first, and get your hands on its power. You have to reach out to other adventurers, who are colleagues and competitors, sometimes with a negotiating hand, sometimes with a sharp sword. The goal of the game is to get the most treasures.

The game has card drafting and set collection mechanism.

Each player has five cards (a piece of map) in their hands. In your turn, you trade cards in your hands with cards in caravans or with other players. When you place your cards in the caravan, you can stack them up to prevent other players from taking them. Or you can play the 'Duel' card to rob other players, but you should always be prepared for counterattacks.

Collect three pieces and complete a map set, and you excavate hidden treasures and jewelry from the game board. Treasures have different effects, which makes interesting changes in the game. When all the treasures are excavated, the player with the most jewels wins.

One player is the ball mom or party giver. Prepared are a number of giant-size flowers made of cut colored tissue paper, and also all kinds of headgear: pointed caps, hats, helmets, etc. The former for the participants who are to dance as ladies, the latter for those girls who are to dance as gentlemen. There are also a number of cards on which the names of the players are written. The mother of the ball is given a large cap of tissue paper, stands at the table on which the game is spread out, all the others stand in a large circle around her, each a few paces from the other; every second player gets a ball. The mother of the ball counts: 1, everyone throws the ball to the neighbor to the right at the same time, quickly turns to the left and catches the ball from the neighbor to the left, if the space is too narrow, one is enough the ball to the third or fourth. If you caught, the ball mother sets your marker on space 1; it means the first step to the ballroom; all players who drop their ball remain outside. The ball mother counts: 2, and the ball game is repeated, having previously determined the type of catch; once, twice, etc. clapping hands, jumping, spinning, hitting the ball with the wrong hand, etc. Each time she has to pass the marks of the lucky catchers, whoever drops the ball stays on the same space. If you drop three times in a row, you have to go back and start over, if you drop twice, you step back 2 spaces.

Whoever is first to number 50 wins the game.  He or she dances as a gentlemen, receive a hat and opens the ball with the ball mother. The second party entering the ballroom dances as a lady, they have a large flower stuck in their hair. This continues alternately till the ball mother determines that there are enough couples in the ballroom and the ball game is to end.

After the game those who are left behind are wallflowers and have the obligation to sing or play the music for the others to the dance to.  The ball begins with a big polonaise, the music moves ahead, the dancers in pairs behind. As the conversation progresses, the dancers now and then have to throw balls to the musicians at the command of the ball mother; if the catch is successful, they swap roles with the dancers on one or two tours. Finally, a large cotillon with the distribution of paper flowers and medals previously made of tissue paper.

The kränzchen-sisters have decided to pay a joint visit to the kränzchen-aunt and, since they live scattered all over the world, to pick each other up in order to arrive together in Stuttgart. Whether this is successful will be determined at the destination. The journey begins at space 1 at home. At the beginning of the game, each player pays 20 tokens to the till; one or two dice are thrown in the usual manner and one advances from number to number.
One of the travelers is elected as chairperson. As soon as she continues her journey, she has to ask each of the people throwing the dice 3 questions about the last route covered: which countries she passed, which mountains or rivers she crossed, what curiosities could be seen, what natural or artistic products the countries contain, etc etc. Whoever answers a question incorrectly must pay a fine of 2 tokens; who misses 2 questions 4 tokens and goes back to the previous station; if you leave all questions unanswered, you have to pay 6 tokens and go back to your home town. If the chairperson is guilty of an oversight by asking a question incorrectly or failing to judge the answer, the same penalty applies.
If you go abroad, you will receive a travel allowance of 5 to kens from the til, but has the obligation to answer the questions in English or French. Anyone who gets close to the border or port has a one-day stay due to customs clearance and is not allowed to play the dice once. In the cities marked with yellow numbers, the passenger missed the connection; this holds the same until she is replaced by another traveler who also stops here. But on the next roll she has as many spaces to go back as the dice show eyes. In the residence cities, whereby only Berlin applies in Germany, travelers receive 10 tokens to buy a travel gift for loved ones at home. Should the till ever go bankrupt, the players are obliged to contribute 5 tokens again. Whoever arrives in Stuttgart first receives the contents of the till.
At the end all the players write a poem from the town where she got stuck, in which she describes the journey and the gift she bought. It happens in such a way that the winner begins with a long piece of paper and, after writing it down, folds it in such a way that only the two final words of the last two lines remain visible, to which the next one has to rhyme and so on. After the last one has finished her verses, the chairperson reads out the entire poem, which, if it is well done, may in fact be sent to the aunt so that she can see whether the dear maids have had a pleasant journey.

Race. Hack. Win.

In Reality Shift, race across large magnetic Reality Cubes while hacking into them to change their positions.

Move your light bike across any side of the cubes to find the best path to the finish.

Shift reality by hacking the track to slide, rotate, flip and tilt the Reality Cubes. Block your opponents’ paths, make your path to the finish shorter, or crush your opponents!

Reality Shift is a fast-paced 3D racing game for 2 to 4 players set in a retro virtual reality world. The racetrack is made of cubes whose magnetic facings have different paths on them. Your light bike is not constrained by gravity. Your magnetic figure can use any accessible path on the tops and the sides of the cubes.

Being fast is not enough though. You will be able to play a card each turn that will move a Reality Cube, creating a track that is always shifting. Don’t have a path to the finish? Transform the board to make one. Is an opponent in the lead? Change the track to block their way or just crush them with a Reality Cube, forcing them to respawn at the start.

Be faster, smarter, and more ruthless than the other racers to win.

—description from the designer

Deluxe Components included:
- 1 White Light Bike and summary card
- 21 Power Cube counters
- 1 Double-sided grid board
- 18 Power-up cards
- 1 Vortex Cube
- 1 White Hole Cube
- 1 Portal Cube
- 1 Deluxe Rulebook

The cubes can be added to a base game setup providing you with new hazards and options while the Power-up cards provide new variants to include in a race.  The grid board allows for a basic side or one with track movement layouts that can be used in conjunction with cube movement.  The White Light Bike is powerful if chosen but comes with a few built-in setbacks as well.

The deluxe rulebook comes with 11 new track setups for use with the deluxe set or in combination with the retail version allowing for larger tracks.  It also details all the variations and power-up card descriptions as well as rules for including a 5th player using the white Light Bike.

UNO themed around the sport of cricket.

WHAT MAKES IT DIFFERENT FROM TRADITIONAL UNO: 
Two special wild cards:

"Wild Draw 4/6" card - acts as a Wild Draw 4 card, but when played on top of any other wild card becomes Wild Draw 6

"Wild Bouncer" card - whoever plays this card names a color, and the next player must play an Action card of that color. If they cannot, they draw one card and skip their turn.

Think you know your celebs? In this game of famous, faces each player picks a card, then by asking your opponent questions, you have to try and guess which celebrity card they are holding. The difficulty is that they can only answer yes or no!

Celebrity Who Is It? is a two-player game suitable for everyone aged 6 years and over. To start you separate the cards into three packs. There is a purple pack, a red pack and a blue pack. Each pack should have 24 cards. Each player should take one pack and the third pack should be shuffled then placed in the centre of the table. Each player lays out their pack of cards picture side up on the table in front of them so that each of the celebrities is visible. The youngest player should take the top card from the stack in the centre of the table then the second player should then take the next card from the stack. Then the guessing begins. The first person to guess the opponents' card correctly wins, but be careful as an incorrect guess means an automatic win for the other player.

Everything you need to play Celebrity Who Is It? comes supplied in the printed gift box, which is handy for safely storing the cards when the game is not in use.

boardgame: CATastrophe: A Game of 9 Lives – Paws Out, Claws Out Expansion
description:
Get your cattitude on in this wild expansion that is sure to leave you clawing for more! The Paws Out, Claws out expansion for CATastrophe: A Game of 9 Lives adds 52 all-new CATastrophic events, 16 epic Power Cats, and 7 brand new Characters.

Keep your paws out and claws out - or else you might find yourself eliminated! Whether you are clawing your way to victory as the Mad Catter or as Tacocat, you're sure to have a pawsome time!

CATastrophe: A Game of 9 Lives is a competitive game as spontaneous and unpredictable as cats themselves, featuring iconic cats from around the world!

Start with 9 lives and try to survive the chaotic mayhem. The last cat standing wins. But beware of the villain, the Grim Reapurr, who is plotting your untimely demise and attempting to be the sole victor!

-description from designer

Felisi is a new hero for One Deck Dungeon.

Felisi’s heroic feat allows her take a daring leap, rolling three heroic dice, and collecting any double or triples. But if all dice are unique, then she takes damage and uses time. A very press your luck feat.

Her 1 player skill allows her to reroll 2 dice, and 4 if she used a daring leap (regardless of daring leap success).

Her 2 player skill allows her and her partner to reroll 1 dice, and 2 if she used daring leap (regardless of daring leap success).

This promo was given out at Gen Con 2022 to promote One Deck Galaxy.

The Cultists of Cathulhu have gathered: their Great Eldritch Ritual nears its end. There is just one thing left: choose one of them to Ascend and finish the ritual. It’s a simple choice, really. They are all the same — any of them will do! There is just one problem: some Investigators have infiltrated the ritual, pretending to be Cultists. And choosing any of them would ruin it all!

Cult of Cathulhu is a fast-paced hidden role game for 4-11 players. Engage as a Cultist, trying to find trustworthy brethren with your unique skills and solve the lie-filled riddle -- or as an Investigator, trying to sneak you or your comrades in to halt the Cultists’ world-ending plans.
Each player has two hidden cards: their alignment (Cultist or Investigator), and a separate skill card. Investigators are in the minority but know each other, while Cultists have to use their otherworldly skills in a search for just one Cultist they all can trust.

The game consists of three phases: Mission briefing, Council, and Voting.
Mission briefing lets the Investigators know each other, as well as other Investigators' skills. 
In Council, players can freely discuss and use their skills. Most skills either give information, e.g., by seeing others' cards, or help other players use their more powerful skills. 
Investigators can use their skills just like Cultists to fool them, with an extra advantage: they can use skills from any card, not just their own. Skill cards are always discarded after use, face-down. The Council is timed; after the time runs out, Voting begins.

In Voting, all players simultaneously vote for another player. All players with the most votes are chosen to Ascend, and the game ends. If all chosen were Cultists, the ritual succeeds and all Cultists win; if any of them was an Investigator, the Investigators win, instead.

–description from designer

The ruins of an unknown civilization have been discovered and archaeologists from all over the world begin their work to unearth all the treasures and stories that the island of Bako has hidden.

In this roll and write game, you will take the role of the prestigious archaeologists Kai and Koro. During each round, ruled by the result of the dice, all players at the same time will draw fences all over their enclosure to try to complete the figures that the cards determine and thus unearth the different fossils, treasures and ruins that are hidden.

The bigger the discoveries, the more points you earn, but the greater the risk because the other archaeologists can ruin your strategy by placing a hole.

After 15 rounds, the player who has achieved the most points for his discoveries wins the game.

-description from designer

boardgame: Foundations of Rome: Roads of Fortune
description:
Cunning Architects need to be mindful of more than just what’s within the boundaries of Rome, but what lies beyond them as well. The edges of the city, previously considered by your fellow Architects to be the least valuable real estate, are now connected to the outside world. Traveling merchants, diplomats, philosophers, soldiers, and vagabonds approach the gates, eager to exchange their knowledge and services! Control of Rome’s borders means control of untold riches entering the city.

In Roads of Fortune, players are rewarded for maintaining majority control over the edges of the play area by unlocking powerful new abilities on a variable set of Road boards.  As the capstone expansion to the Foundations of Rome experience, the Roads are compatible with any combination of existing modules, including the Gardens of Ceres solo challenge. Roads of Fortune also features a number of quality life improvements, such as component tray upgrades and new player aids.

description: This expansion came with White Dwarf issue 134 published in 1991. The Halls of Durrag-Dol is a quest for MB Games HeroQueset. Included with this quest are new rules for using Skaven in your HeroQuest games and an option to include Kili Thekkrsson as a Dwarf Trollslayer player character.
Roll five coloured dice and two white dice to cross out values in a five coloured columns, in a game very much like Qwixx.

Yahtzee style game that uses coloured dice.
Scores of individual dice are recorded in columns for each of the dice colours.
Bonuses and scores depend on the scores per colour column.

Roll 7 dice, then each player in turn picks one or two dice to cross off the boxes on their player sheet, following restrictions for the different colours (eg blue scores go in descending order).
Play til one player has filled in all the boxes of one colour.
Each colour scores differently and awards different bonuses.
The games uses six coloured dice which are rolled to try scoring the most “money” (points), by crossing off tetris shapes on a player sheet to fill lines, fill coulours and cross off bonus squares.

boardgame: Betrayal: The Werewolf's Journey – Blood on the Moon
description:
Betrayal at House on the Hill: 3rd Edition gets a frightening addition with the release of The Werewolf’s Journey: Blood on the Moon, an expansion that allows players to choose from an additional character at the beginning of the game. Sara may look like your typical teenager, but that will all change in the most dreadful and terrifying way as she transforms into feral werewolf when the haunt occurs!

The Werewolf’s Journey: Blood on the Moon include two highly detailed miniatures as well as five specifically designed haunts to include the werewolf into the story. Also included are four game cards, two room tiles, and a custom scenario card.

—description from the publisher

description: This is expansion came with White Dwarf issue 145 published in 1992. The Eyes of Chaos is a quest for MB Games HeroQuest. Included with this quest are some new monsters and Henchman who will assist the Heroes in overcoming the perils of the Tomb of the Phalanx.

This expansion includes new cards and components to play Mint Knight as a two-player cooperative game.

This expansion includes:

     Ability to play 2 player co-op with another player.
     Bonus Terrain from Galactic Conquest
     Miniature + more cubes to support 2nd player
     8 page fold out rule book to replace base game rules. No rules have changed, just bigger font, better explanations.
     Fix/errata cards


Fix/errata cards:
Some earlier orders of Mint Knight had printing errors and or errata to make cards more clear. This expansion contains several cards to replace the troubled cards in the original game.

Cosmic Coding is a fun board game designed to introduce children to coding. Compatible with Key Stage 1 of the National Curriculum, Cosmic Coding teaches the basic concepts of Computer Science, including algorithms and programming language. A full detailed guide is included to help your child become a Cosmic Coder and complete their mission to collect all their stars.

Guide your spaceship around the galaxy by drawing a card each turn and following the conditions set out. Each game you'll be trying to collect three different colours of stars, collect the right combination, complete your mission and win the game!

WWII Campaigns: 1940, 1941, and 1942 is the faithful recreation of not one, but THREE classic GDW Series 120 games, in this all-encompassing package. Each of the three games is intended as challenging, introductory-level games; the “120” reflects each game in this series uses no more than 120 counters, and all are intended to be played to completion in two hours or less without sacrificing historical quality or gaming excellence. Classics in every sense of the word for their day, relive the battles with these old classics in a new and updated format (larger counters and map size).

The games included are:

1940, The Battle of France and the Low Countries is a two-player,  operational-level (division/corps) — loosely based on the old SPI Battle of Moscow game system — of the first 52 days of the German offensive against France and the Low Countries in spring of 1940. The game focuses on the critical period — May 10 to June 30 — during which the Allies essentially fought and lost the First Battle for France. Units are corps/army, with a time scale of 5 days per game turn, and a map scale of 26 kilometers per hex.

1941, Operation Barbarossa, the invasion of the Soviet Union covers the initial onslaught into the Soviet Union, beginning in the summer of 1941. Units are division/corps, with a time scale of one month per game turn, and a map scale of 65 kilometers per hex.

1942, The Japanese Campaign against Malaya, Java, and the initial attack in the Philippines covers the Japanese opening moves at the onset of the Pacific War, and the succeeding operations conducted against the various areas. The map covers the entire expanse of Southeast Asia at 85 nautical miles per hex. The gameplay is a true challenge for the Japanese player who must try to “run the table” and clear the map of Allied units.

Complexity: 5 out of 10 (Low to Medium)
Solitaire Suitability: 6 out of 10 (Medium)
Time Scale: Varies by game
Map Scale: Varies by game
Unit Scale: Varies by game

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A light and very quick shedding game for two with an ordinary. Players take turns exchanging cards with the deck to improve their seven card hand. But, in a game of chicken, either player can jump-start things by “running” and starting the race to play combos to shed all their cards.

part of the "two-player triology" of climbing games: Eat or Run, Hedges, Tuhao.

Explore the mystical world of Japan in the Heian Period, in this solitaire adventure card game. Journey across ever-changing lands and meet fellow Travelers from history and legend. Encounter tricky and treacherous yokai and gain arcane, powerful items. Learn the many Magics that form the forgotten practice of Onmyodo!  Historically, Onmyodo was a practice of magic and mysticism that incorporated aspects of Shinto, Buddhism, Chinese Elemental Magic, Astrology and much more. The game references much of this rich history and tradition, mixing characters from legend with real-world archetypes from the Heian Period.

Designed as a solo adventure and storytelling game, Onmyodo can also be played with a small group of players, who collaborate to undergo the challenging journey.

The game employs the use of Tani cards (regions) that are arranged in a diagonal pattern to form a constantly evolving scroll. By forming certain patterns of card features, players can trigger various special items, weather effects, heavenly blessings and more. How long can your Travelers survive, what adventures will unfold, and what missions may they undertake along the way?

While the game is played as an open-ended story, specific Quests can also be integrated for even further challenge.

You are a candidate trying to become the state's next governor.  You will be competing against five other candidates in an election that uses the ranked choice voting election method.

Each turn represents a week of campaigning.  You will direct your campaign manager to select 3 actions in the game.  You will choose to either court voters in a specific block of voters (by class, by political spectrum, or location within the state), all while increasing your name recognition, raising funds, and trying to maintain your popularity.  At the end of four quick weeks of campaigning is election day.  You will determine the winner and you will learn how easy it is to conduct a ranked choice voting election count.

This is a very fast paced dynamic game, playing in 30 minutes, with quick set up and take down.  It has straight forward rules which produce many deceptively complex interactive decisions, and high replayability.

Will you be the candidate that can balance all the elements of the campaign, out maneuver our opponents, and become the next governor?

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: The Diamonds, The Dagger and One Classy Dame
description:
An entry in the Murder à la carte mystery dinner party games.

Gamse includes: 

     Party planner with recipes and menu including music and decorating tips
     Character Booklets for all suspects, includes their roles and background information


The story is set in October 1942 in Casablanca, an oasis of peace in a world at war. Civilians, combatants, and refugees of all nationalities live together in a state of tense suspicion but not even Casablanca is immune to personal tragedy. Last night the Mayor of Casablanca was murdered in his own home and now the killer is coming to dinner! A Hollywood Star, a Gestapo Officer, an American Journalist, a Danish art dealer and a French bureaucrat are just a few of the dinner guests suspected of his murder!

The anticipation, mystery and excitement begin before guests have even arrived as each of the guests, or suspects, will receive their invite and suggestions for suitable dress. This all starts to set the scene for the forthcoming crime - and by the end of the evening, one of the guests will be exposed as a killer!

Into the Rift is a 3v1 tabletop game where three players (the heroes) battle their way through custom layout dungeons created by the fourth player (The rift dweller).

Hero's winning objective: Defeat the rift dweller at the very end of the game, by traversing 3 dungeons that become longer and more difficult as more floors are completed.  Players will defeat monsters, find hidden rooms and relics, and become stronger as they reach the end to defeat the Rift Dweller in combat.  At least one player has to be alive in the end for the heroes to win.

Rift Dweller's winning objective:  Kill all three of the hero characters before you yourself are defeated.  Choose one of 3 bosses kept in secret until the final room is revealed.  Construct dungeons by placing room cards that may or may not be booby-trapped or infested with monsters.

Dungeon mechanics:
Each floor begins at the entrance card, which is the first card flipped face up.  Connect other rooms to this by placing room cards face down.  Once players decide where to go, the room they select is flipped, revealing the next obstacle.

Every room has a maximum monster value.  Monster value is determined by the monsters placed in said rooms.  Each floor can only have a set number of rooms, which increases as floors progress, and also must include at least one beneficial room.

The final floor has a boss room hidden in the layout, and cannot be connected to the entrance, nor have multiple rooms connected to it (to avoid finding it too fast)

Currently in Open beta!  The steam link -> also has a google form for giving your thoughts and opinions:
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2846176764

boardgame: Western Legends: The Dark Knight Promo Card
description:
Mystery shrouds the masked vigilante. He's taken a likin' to them bats in the Spikes View Mine. A new crop of gangs wanders these parts; but with the masked vigilante always a'breathin' down their necks, they think twice about causin' trouble. Reckon there's a dispute brewin' with these clowns. He fights like nothin' I've seen round here, and where does he get all those wonderful toys?

Available during the Western Legends: Mountain Range Kickstarter campaign (2022).

Contains 1 new Dark Knight playable character card.

     Starting Location: Spike's View Cave
     Starting Bonus: Utility "Saddle" Bags, 1 Poker Card, $40


The four Dark Knight Goal cards are numbered 'DK-1' to 'DK-4'.

A Dark Knight STL miniature is available for 3D printing.

This is a doujin expansion that was only sold a game shows in Japan.  It includes rules, charts, and counters (need assembly).  Ownership of LotGH Ragnarok is required.

The expansion recreates the second and final Empire invasion of the Alliance.

description:
This card was available as a convention promo starting November 2019.

Ursa Minor
Trigger - Ursa Minor is the only Mythical Creature on your Lawn at the beginning of your turn;
Effect - Cast a Spell

Deception is an equally hilarious and inquisitive strategic game designed to determine the ingenious interrogator and masterful deceiver.

Players shuffle identity cards and then a story theme is selected. Each player takes their turn telling a story from their own lives. "Confessor" cards tell true stories, "deceiver" cards tell a lie. It's up the "Judge" each round to select who is who.

Interrogation and strategic questioning is employed to help pick out who in the group is lying. The winner will come down to the person who can tell the best stories, who can spot a liar, and who can deceive everyone in the room.

boardgame: Eclipse: Second Dawn for the Galaxy – Seekers
description:
This expansion introduces two new species to play your Eclipse - 2nd Dawn for the Galaxy with, The Magellan and The Enlightened of Lyra.

The Magellan

The Magellan are forever travelling between galaxies in their enormous worldships, collecting and exploiting relics of civilizations long gone. Their ships synthesize resources from the cosmic debris and are able to sustain the worldship during the long aeons spent in the void. The Wardens have specialized in tracking the Ancient relics, looking for their source. Just as the Ancients are rising, the great worldships arrive on the fringes of our galaxy. The Council informants claim that the pattern of their appearance suggests that the Magellan are here not just to explore and exploit, but to seek a long-standing galactic dominance.

The Magellan gain a free Discovery Tile from Technological advancement and score points at the end of the game for Discovery Tiles used as Ship Parts. They can use their Colony Ships to gain Resources.

The Enlightened of Lyra

The Enlightened preferred the reclusion of the Lyra system, slowly expanding their elaborate Shrine grid into the neighboring star systems and subliming their minds into it. The Enlightened were only recently contacted by the Seven, when the edges of the growing grid were discovered. As the Shrines connect in the mind sublimation process, they form powerful Trinities that reshape the base manifold structure, allowing for significant leaps in the civilization' progress. The Enlightened colonisation fleets have reportedly tapped into the grid, causing minor warping of the causality continuum and turning the tide of several battles.

The Enlightened construct Shrines, which allow them to unlock bonuses and score points at the end of the game. They can use their Colony Ships to reroll dice.

The Second Dawn editions of these races have numerous little balance tweaks, such as adjustments to starting resources and sectors. The Enlightened also have a completely redone Shrine mechanic.

Contents (preliminary)
Game Box
Rulebook
2x Player Tray
2x Player sheet
Cargo Hold storage tray
Species components (in 2 colours)
8x Interceptor miniature
4x Cruiser miniature
2x Dreadnought miniature
4x Starbase miniature
33x Population Cube
16x Influence Disc
3x Ambassador Tile
1x Turn Order Variant token
The Enlightened extra components:
1x Shrine Board
9x Shrine Miniature
1x Influence Disc

description:
These new rules for Space Crusade allow you to take on the Alien player equipped in the Imperium‘s dreaded Terminator armour or the head of a squad of Space Marine Scouts or mob of Ork Boyz!

If you‘re the Alien player, you can now include Genestealer Hybrids or awesome Tyranid Warriors in your Army!

To use the rules you‘ll need the appropriate miniatures from the Warhammer 40,000 range.

Originally published in White Dwarf 134. Includes Order and Equipment cards needed to use Ork Boyz instead of Space Marines.

Hermiatus, a Magos Biologus of the Adeptus Mechanicus has disappeared while working on an anti-gene that has the potential to immunise humans against infection by Genestealers. It‘s suspected that in the course of his research he accidentally became infected with the Genestealer plasm and fled in terror.

Renegade is a five-part campaign for Space Crusade which involves the Space Marine players in a desperate search to locate and capture Hermiatus.

Originally published in White Dwarf #145.

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: Murder by the Mob
description:
Murder by the Mob Murder Mystery Party game includes an optional DVD and CD. Watch the short pieces of film to reveal clues about the murder. play the CD of themed background music to create just the right ambience for your investigation!

Chicago in the late 1920’s is overrun by the mob, most of whom have been invited to 'Spudsy' Malone’s party. However, when the Don welcomed everyone to celebrate the invention of his new potato alcohol drink, he wasn’t expecting any of them to ‘do him a favour’ and shoot him in the chest. Host your own party to find out who whacked the big potato!

Contents include party planning supplies.

The full universe of Windward in a KS Exclusive big box. This includes the Base Game and Expansion.

You are a captain flying your Ship on the skies of the gaseous planet Celus. Gain the most notoriety by sailing the skies, hunting Crestors, fighting opponents. Whichever player has the most notoriety points at the end of the game is the winner. To gain notoriety, you can trade in Crestor teeth and gas at the trading post.

Players take turns to move their ships, take actions, use supply cards, and battle other players.

The Windward: Treacherous Skies expansion adds many new elements to the game: a third level of elevation, scout boats for all players to use alongside their ships, world-building events, a pirate-style faction, contract mini-quests, team play, and more. All of these elements come together to open the door to countless new paths to victory.

—description from designer

The expansion material includes the following modules:

     Treacherous Skies with the Black Market, Scouts, Treasure coins, and Treasure Hunting
     The Raider (with a Raider Ship, Mates, Trackers, and extra bases)
     Tournament Rules
     World Events (35 Event cards)
     Achievements

     Kolkra 
     Blue Crestor figure
     40 Drift Tiles (for alternate maps)
     In-Game Trays
     12 Automa Cards
     Speed Dial tracker
     16 Treasure Coin tokens

... and more

Get geared up for the ultimate hunting board game, Hunting Season! Filled with twists, turns, true to life scenarios, fun, excitement and lots of laughs is what's in store for all who dare to play!

Your adventure starts at the campground when you choose your weapon and the kind of animal you want to go after. Find your way to the general store to gear up before you head out. Did you remember your camo, your cover scent and your binoculars? How about your four wheeler? Maybe you'll need it, or maybe you won't. Either way, it's a wild adventure just getting to the hunting ground! Once there, you spot and stalk your animal, and the tension mounts as you move ever closer, but be careful. You might be spotted and miss your chance to take your shot!

Players use a combination of dice and events cards to move and determine their success in hunting their prey. Success is not guaranteed.  The player with the most points at the end of the game  wins.

La Fallera Calavera card is a promo that was given out separately with the very first purchases of the game when it was released. It consists of only one card that adds to your deck (forming a 25 card deck).

If played as a fighter, La Fallera Calavera will "revive" any fighter that lost a duel (yours or from your opponent) and you will use that fighter for the duel, removing La Fallera Calavera from the game. If played as a star, you lose the duel inmediately, but on your next duel your fighter has +7 power.

Players have fun learning the positive actions and pitfalls that lead to co-operative learning group success or disaster.

Players are randomly assigned 'group members' that will either help or hinder the players quest to complete a project (reach the end of the track). You can trade 'group members' with other players or with the unused deck.

Players then draw cards which will have various Actions or Suggestions related to the 'group members'. If the card mentions one of your 'group members' then the results of the card effect you.  Move ahead on the track, move back on the track, etc.

Player wins by getting to the end of the track first. Like Candyland or LIFE.

Players have fun while learning that all students can stop bullying... whether they find themselves in the role of the target, the bystander, or the bully.

Players draw a 'Sitiation' card that presents a bullying situation. They roll a die to see which player will be the Bullying Target, the Bully, or the Bystander. Based on your advice from 'Target Advisor', 'Bully Advisor', or 'Bystander Advisor' cards you will answer questions.

Your answers to the questions and die rolls will score you points (+/-). Most points at the end wins.

The days on Ryh are longer than those on Earth. Yet somehow it was still 33 rotations before we encountered the Oxataya - another 12 before we met any Ahzuri. So far our negotiations have gone well. Oxataya are an ancient water-dwelling clan, full of wisdom, knowledge and insight. Ahzuri are an ascetic mountain tribe of which we still know very little. We have learnt a lot from Oxataya, and their temples, but there is still much to discover. Our new research outposts and specialists have shown great promise so far. Still, only time will tell.

The Specialists Expansion adds two new local factions for players to negotiate with, a temple location to visit, and outpost boards to extend their research bases. It also adds a lot of new characters (variable player powers) and contract cards, and the new, powerful specialist dice.

—description from the publisher

description:
Compete against other 911 operators to increase your
reputation to be the next in line for promotion! The competition will be hard but beware! Don’t let your ambitions get in the way and let the city fall into chaos.

In this game, players use their units to solve incidents to keep Public Tension in check. The goal is to solve the highest scenario incident (the one in the “III” deck) while keeping the Public Tension from reaching the end. To reach that goal, players recruit units and send them to incident locations to solve them according to their unit’s power.

Each player must keep the Public Tension in check. If things get out of hand and the Public Tension reaches the end, the game ends and all players lose the game. So players should plan, communicate and work together to solve the incidents while racing to collect more reputation to win the game.

—description from the designer

Link to the eponymous video game: 911 Operator

boardgame: After the Empire: Treasure House Promo Card
description:
2022 Gen Con promo card from Grey Fox Games for After the Empire. Later available during the Tantrum House Season 8 Kickstarter campaign.

Treasure House is a new building card. It costs 1 wood, 2 Stone, 1 Iron and immediately provides 4 Gold and 2 mercenaries. At the end of the game, it is worth 3 victory points.
A promotional Ludoty Box micro-expansion with additional cards for We Didn't Playtest This at All with Chaos Pack Expansion.

description:
The MCEI astronauts have received a distress signal. It was sent from somewhere on Mars - the planet they belived to be alone on! Who sent it? Can the rescue mission reach the location on time? Will you succeed in a task on which the future on the Martian mission may depend?

Become a Martian astronaut and feel the flow of the boardgame! "Rescue Team" is a single player gamebook, showing the boardgame's challenges in a brand new way: dwindling resources, tense relations between a closed group of astronauts, the need to make tough decisions.

GO THERE, BE THE MARTIAN RESCUER, AND DISCOVER THE MYSTERY OF THE SIGNAL!

—description from the publisher

The gamebook Rescue team is a single player add-on for First Martians highlighting the challenges in a new way.

This game allows a simulation of Swiss politics.

In the first phase of the game, each player builds up their parliamentary forces by answering questions.

In the second phase, each player has to give his opinion on a political issue. He must convince the other players, and then a vote takes place, the result of which depends on the forces in the parliament, which can be modified by chance cards.

—user summary

You rule a fantasy realm during the final battle of the ancient wars. The Battle of the Deepest Valley. Your armies have been driven from the field, and your treasury drained, but you have one advantage, and one last chance at victory.

Deepest Valley is a fantasy strategy gamezine for 2 to 5 players. A complete hand-drawn hex and dice wargame in zine format. Plays in 1 hour (2 players), to 3 hours (5 players). Comes with a large fold-out map, carry case, and *lots* of dice.

"They say the valley has always been at war. That it always will be... They call it the Deepest Valley. Say it will never fill up. No matter how many bodies we throw in, or how much gold we bury them under. To the other realms it's a strategic key, a prize to win at any cost. We remember a time when there was peace in the valley. We are the children of the mountains, and the valley is our cradle. We know there can be peace in the valley again. There will be peace. Once our realm takes it back!"

—description from the designer

Tachite al Tram is a game in which you drive the historic "tram de Opcina" of the city of Triest. It is an unusual hybrid tramway and funicular railway built in 1902.

Build your route, pick up passengers, customize your tram with special powers, and pay attention to accidents and unexpected events, called "disgrazie". The "tram de Opcina" is very famous for being unlucky.

You receive points when you bring passengers from a station to the other. The first that reach 12 points became the tram-driver of the year.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Real War Tactics is a tactical turn-based game strategy, an imitation of a military conflict, where the commander will win, having assessed the situation, who will be able to make the right decisions and strategically place his combat units.

Each player will have to introduce various combat units into the game, move them around the game map, engage in close combat or make artillery attacks on enemy units.

The goals of the game are to destroy the field commander, destroy or capture the enemy headquarters.

Two or more sets of the game can be combined and take part in a grand battle.

—description from the publisher

It lurks in the night! It hungers for flesh! This summer, no one is safe from the all new Scary Pack, featuring 30 terrifying cards about skulls and spiderwebs, and shit, plus a bone-chilling poster to hang in your haunted studio apartment.

30 all-new cards about serial killers, sex gremlins, and vaginas with teeth.  Includes one of three terrifying posters to hang in your haunted studio apartment.

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Picture Card Pack 3
description:
Your eyeballs will squawk like a newborn pterodactyl emerging from its shell at our third and best pack of 30 all-new cards with pictures on them. Some are US Specific.

—description from the back of the box

Futegame is a card game that simulates a soccer match. In it, players will be able to assume the technical command of one of the four available teams and seek victory.

It is a game of comparing values that are computed with the aim of acquiring new cards with plays that enhance teams, goals or generate resources for your team. The coach who manages to score more goals than his opponent at the end of 22 rounds wins.

Blueprint is a roll and write game for 1 to 4 players.

Players take the role of architects who have to draw the plans for a house during the first phase, and complete rooms with the right objects in the second phase.

Every turn, a player rolls 3 dice, setting aside one die exclusively for their use and allowing the rest of the players to use one of the two remaining dice. Each die provides access to a supplier, which allows players to draw rooms on their floor plan, or to draw the objects inside them. Increase the value of the house by completing rooms, customer requests, or being the first to reach an achievement. To complete a room place your particular piece of furniture next to two of your other allowed pieces of furniture.

After 8 rounds, the player with the highest valued house wins.

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo 20 – Sagrada x Fog of Love Window Pattern Card
description:
A new double sided player card to build the stained glass window in. Shuffle into the deck with the other player cards during setup and proceed as normal.

A 3 and 5 level difficulty card handed out at the Floodgate Games booth at Gen Con 2022.
Rules allowing up to 4 players to play a series of 3 games against each other.  Each game uses 1 of the 3 Great Facades expansions.

Knowledge is power, and applied knowledge is freedom. Countries have risen from the ashes because of it, others have succumbed for lack of it.  Now, it's your turn. Your era. Will you rise to the occasion and grow your empire? Or be devoured by the other leaders at the table?

Empires of Influence is a board game that embodies the true struggle for power. It merges strategy and economics with trivia. Put your general knowledge to the test by answering questions about geography, culture, and economics, and compete for industries, big business deals, and state contracts. There are over 360 playable cards to enjoy, country cards, industry cards, news cards, and trivia cards.

The secret to conquering EOI lies in tapping into your instincts, flexing your decision-making prowess, and bringing your knowledge up to par. EOI exposes your mind to other cultures and expands your understanding of the world with quizzes and a general knowledge learning formula

Take a seat at the round table of world leaders with your friends and family, get comfy, and put your general knowledge to the test. You win by possessing all 8 countries and achieve world domination before the time runs out, or by amassing the most wealth, least debt, and possessing the most countries.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Space Base: U.E.C. John D Clair Promo Card
description:
This promo card is placed in the Level 3 Market deck.

Cost: 35
Sector: 9

Blue: Gain all other Blue Rewards on your board.
Red: Gain all other Red Rewards above your board.

This promo was given to attendees of the Gen Con AEG Pre-Release Event 2022.
Later available from Tantrum House Season 9 kickstarter and then available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Yeah Nah! is a create your own luck card game for 2-4 players. The game play will have you making decisions on every turn that could change the outcome of the game. It will leave you wondering if you made the right decision, Yeah Nah!

Many decisions are centred around helping yourself or slowing down your opponent on the path to victory. The strategy and layers to the game means every game can have a different path to victory. With themes and feels from all classic family card games this game is sure to be a hit for those wanting to spice up the family card game genre with a bit more depth. Easy to learn, with layers to mastery.

Players draw and discard cards in an attempt to Lock a set. Each step has you deciding between advancing yourself or hindering your opponent/s.

Win by being the first to complete & “Lock” In all 3 Sets.


     Standard 6 sided dice not included, but Dice Cards are for optional advanced game play

boardgame: Tribes of Dieu Trading Card Game
description:
TODTCG is a game about the spirit of remembrance! Play casually or competitively in different ways with any single tribe or multiple tribes & archetypes to create unique synergies, combos & field moves from your front or back row!

Each tribe represents a different theme & playstyle. The goal is to defend your tribal, then summon & sacrifice creatures & spells to reduce your opponent's tribal life points to 0, whether through battle or alternative win conditions.

TODTCG consists of 4 phases. Gathering, Preparation, Command, & Combat.

There are 8 card types that are put into 3 groups. Source Type (Chosen & Tribals) Creatures (Members, Spirits & Beastials ) & Spells (Strategy, Field & Armaments). Chosen & tribals are core cards. They are almost linked together. Chosen are flipped to protect the tribal card. Tribals act as turn by turn buffs effecting battlefield & style of play. Strategy cards are instant cards. Once you use them, they are exhausted and sent to the graveyard unless stated otherwise. Field cards activate when placed and stays in the spot they are placed until they are destroyed by attack or condition. They can be activated for multiple turns. Armaments are cards that attach to other cards as armor with special buffs. When attacked players can chose to defend with a card that isn’t exhausted.

A pack of 7 cards that was originally available during the Dice Theme Park Kickstarter campaign.

1 card is for Dice Theme Park (Dice Theme Park: Medic Administrator Promo Card) while the other 6 cards (Dice Hospital: ER – Emergency Roll: VIPs Promo) are for Dice Hospital: ER – Emergency Roll.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar (Third Edition): Death Battletome – Nighthaunt
description:
From Shyish, the Realm of Death, come the ethereal Nighthaunt – terrifying spectres driven to murder by their bitter hatred for the living. These undead are the terror weapon of the Great Necromancer, united under the cruelest and most tortured rulers ever to die. Their cold grasp reaches across the Mortal Realms, spreading a paralysis of fear amongst the living souls they once called kin, a howling host that will leave only an endless landscape of corpses and wailing spirits remains.

Within these pages you’ll find everything you need to know to command the deathless hosts of the Nighthaunt in your games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, and discover their place in the Mortal Realms. Read the abundant and sorrowful history of these tortured souls, explore the haunted empire of the amethyst underworlds, and delve into the spectral processions that drag souls into Nagash's iron grip.

This 96-page hardback book contains:


     A musty catacomb of background information on the Nighthaunt, including their history, relationship with Nagash, and spectral culture
     Sinister yet breathtaking artwork that conveys the grim majesty of the Nighthaunt
     31 warscrolls and pitched battle profiles for ghostly warriors, from swarming Chainrasps to new heroes like the Scriptor Mortis and Awlrach the Drowner
     Rules for 4 distinct Nighthaunt Processions, plus command traits, artefacts of power, spells, and battle traits like Aura of Dread and Frightful Touch
     Path to Glory campaign rules that allow you to spread the pall of Death by haunting the Mortal Realms, plus 7 warscroll battalions for use in narrative play
     Matched play content including 4 grand strategies and 6 battle tactics to unleash against your enemies
     A showcase of morbid Nighthaunt miniatures assembled in the colours of their Processions, alongside guides on how to paint them yourself


Also included is a sheet of 28 tokens and a range ruler, used for tracking the effects of the Wave of Terror ability.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Family Edition – Written by Kids Pack
description:
Fun fact: kids are funnier than adults, because their spirits haven’t yet been crushed by years of boring classes and meaningless jobs.


     30 all-new cards for the Family Edition written by actual children.
     Features jokes only a child could have authored, like “Bathing in Spaghetti-Os,” “A cow that farts cows,” and “Mom’s bad cooking.”
     Think your kid could do better? Show us what you’ve got.


—description from publisher

SALVAGE: Deep Space is a co-operative legacy tabletop game for up to four players that places emphasis on customization, narrative, and teamwork.

To uncover a compelling narrative woven from the results of their own choices, players will group together to perform missions as Salvage Suit Pilots in command of fourteen foot tall Exo-Mechs. During these incursions, gameplay will take the form of tactical-combat where players make decisions and roll dice in support of individual miniatures that navigate hexagonal tiles to salvage technology, combat adversaries, and unlock additional story details and twists. The player objectives are dependent on each individual mission where success or failure will be largely determined by a team’s willingness to work together and adapt to an evolving board state.

When not performing missions players will be prompted by both systematic and random story cues through a choose-your-own-journey style narrative book. These prompts will be evaluated for success or failure against a team’s Qualifications: attributes that were chosen during the pilot creation process. Successful navigation of these moments may result in additional missions, unlocked technology that can be bought and applied to your pilots, and additional Exo-Suits.

SALVAGE: Deep Space is a sprawling, legacy-style game that is ultimately won through completion of its story. How it is completed and the subsequent results are up to the choices of the players and their crew of pilots.

—description from the designer

Carked It! is a hilarious Australian-made card game that will help get you ready for your final days!

[Carked It!: Australian slang term: When something/someone has died ]

The game aims to make having conversations about dying and death something to enjoy, have fun with, and even have a giggle or belly laugh over

Play with a group of 3-6 friends and choose what you want to happen before and after your death from a selection of ridiculous proposals. The best proposals put forward will be crowned as winners. From learning about gilded skulls, becoming a tree egg, and deciding between death by lawnmower or by helicopter blade, to serious things like doing a will and writing posthumous revenge letters to your enemies - open up interesting and funny conversations about your inevitable end, while you still can!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Under Falling Skies: Kyiv City promo tile
description:
Kyiv (B/F)
Blue side (research 4)

"Whenever you place a gray die in an AA gun room, you can increase one worker die that hasn’t been placed yet by 1."

Red side (research 5)

"Whenever you place a gray die in an AA gun room, you can increase one other worker die (placed or unplaced) by 1."

Available in the Spielbox Issue #1 - 2023 issue.

Up to six people can participate in this game, each paying 12 tokens into the till. You roll a die and move your piece according to the number of eyes thrown. Pay attention to the mushrooms shown. Those marked with + are poisonous; if you come up with such a (red) number, you pay 2 tokens into the register and pass one roll of the dice. The other mushrooms are edible, including the ink cap when very young; if you come up with one of these digits, you receive 2 tokens from the register.

Whoever reaches space 50 first, gets 2/3 of the till, the next one gets the rest.

—user summary

boardgame: Godtear: Jaak, the Dubious Alchemist & Cauldron Cronies
description:
Strange Jaak’s potioncraft is famous throughout the land. He wanders from town to town, bringing unusual brews with legendary healing powers. Wherever Jaak goes, his Cauldron Cronies are there to keep his knapsack stocked with weird and wonderful ingredients.

Potent potions, intoxicating elixirs, clever concoctions… this Guardian champion can brew it all. He specialises in healing draughts to keep friendly champions and followers in tip-top condition.

In fact, Jaak’s the only champion in Godtear who can heal other champions. If you’re floundering in fights, with champions getting knocked out left, right, and centre, Jaak’s the goblin for you!

But it’s not such good news for Jaak’s enemies. While he’s healing allies, his Cauldron Cronies followers are busy causing mayhem on the battlefield. So supportive are the Cronies, they can even plant Jaak’s banner for him!

Inside this box is everything you need to bring the Guardian champion Jaak, the Dubious Alchemist and his Cauldron Cronies to the tabletop. Use this expansion with any Godtear starter set for a ready-to-go warband.

—description from the publisher
