boardgame: Killers: The Card Game – Cults Expansion
description:
A whole new way to play Killers - the card game. Cults is a 130 card Expansion where you create your cult, meet their Win Conditions, and grow your cult. With 10 different Cults to create you'll have all the messed up fun bringing your cult to the world.

—description from the designer

Two additional characters (Praetorian and Seneca the Elder) that were created during the Kickstarter-campaign of the Rome & Roll: Gladiators expansion and are sold separately afterwards.

The difference between each character is their starting advantage and the set of 3 advisors every character has.

This expansion also contains 4 alternative Resource trackers.

Awakening is a fully standalone game & expansion set to Wizards & Relics with all new cards & deeper strategies.

Choose a mighty Wizard, pick up an ancient Relic and battle your rivals with this easy going, fast paced, magical multiplayer dueling card game for 2-4 players!

You must defeat your rivals by secretly laying down a wizard and a relic. Players have decks of 30 cards that consist of Wizards AND relics. Each round is unique, as there are archaic ruins called Shrines, that give magical boosts/bonus’ to certain wizards.

A combination of your Wizard, Wizard Advantage, your Relic, the Shrine, and your opponents will result in a power score for each player. The highest total power wins the round.

When your wizard wins the round, it goes to the Hall of Immortality.
The number of players determines the amount of wizards needed in your Hall of Immortality to win the game. For 2 players, 5 wizards. For 3 players, 4 wizards. For 4 players, 3 wizards.

Awaken your inner wizard with this versatile expandable card game.

—description from the publisher

Psycho Chess board game is a four-person Chess game. It is an exciting game with lots of action coming from every direction. It is a strategic game but unlike standard chess, it is much more hectic. Each player has a full set of Chess tokens (16 pieces). The standard chessboard has been modified to accept the added pieces. All tokens move the same as standard chess, but some specific rules are different. It is a crazy game since not only do you have a competitor in front of you but also one on each side.


     Four brightly colored chess armies, Red, Green, Blue, and Purple battle it out on this modified chessboard. Each army has a full set of sixteen tokens, making this game PSYCHO.
     It is PSYCHO as you fight your way across the board to victory. Unlike classic chess, here the first two Kings are taken.
     If you lose your King early, all is not lost. You enter the Deadwood zone and become an antagonist. You have the opportunity to take other opponent’s tokens as well as get your King back.
     The last two Kings play a more traditional game, but until the third King is in checkmate, it is anyone’s game.


64 Tokens, four different armies, Red, Blue, Purple, and Green

The first two Kings get killed. Last two fight to Checkmate. You are not out when your King is killed, you become an antagonist with a chance to return.

—description from the publisher

Checkers taken to the next level, you control two armies as you navigate across this checkerboard, The yellow army plays on the colored squares and the black army on the black square, Yellow and black play completely separately until one becomes a KING. Kings either yellow or black can attack each other.

We added dice to regulate the play. One die tells you how many Checker tokens you can move each turn while the second die tells you how many moves each token can move during that turn. 
 If you roll 2 & 2, you can move 2 tokes twice, in this case you can move one space to set up a jump.

Can you make it through the Psycho Zone!

—description from the publisher

This modified Checker game where you get 7 tokens and need to go through a narrow pass to enter the "fighting Zone" where up to four different armies have to pass through each other to get to the other side to become Kinged.
The trick is to remember which way each checker is permitted to move, since star checkers can only move forward, yet there are 4 different directions

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Summer Cyrus Promo Cards
description:

Gear card sample: Sighing Acanthus Hat (herb, flammable, accessory)- When you draw the trap, gain a reroll token. If wearing no armor, gain 2.

The First Run Collector's Edition is enclosed in a hand-stamped kraft box, limited to 500 and includes:

1 x Hard Plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round insert and base
Large Matte Art Print
Character Art Card

First run collector's edition: Signed and numbered authenticity note

A refreshing dip along the coast of the saliva shores. Cyrus has ventured out without her Magma Lobster Armor, favoring a more lightweight, sighing attire. The primary danger on the beach are the leaping acid crabs, but it's common knowledge that they don't scavenge the beach when the tides are red.

The Skels are in danger. The destructive, supernatural Winter sweeps across the isles, forcing good folk to do anything to survive. It is a dark age of chaos, magic, and fear. Can you create a hero that will beat back the cold, or embrace its malefic power?

Ivion is a 1v1 or 2v2 dueling card game where players construct decks, or 'characters', to battle their friends on a 4x4 tile grid. Each card you draw is a brutal attack, a cunning trick, or a powerful spell. You must outwit your opponent and stay focused, for one misstep could mean a cruel end.

This first edition of Ivion was released in 2018, and has a different graphic design from the rest of Ivion. This means that it is only compatible with other Ivion products if played with sleeves.

—description from the publisher

Gela Scoglitti game uses B-35 system rules. It depicts American landings on Gela and Scoglitti beaches in Sicily, as well as counterattack of the German Herman-Goering division.

Game was published in 33 issue of "Taktyka i strategia" magazine.
family: Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
Anchor Bay is a competitive pirate themed board game.

Players embark in the randomized map and  battle monsters, navigate events, find items, and recruit crew members.  Players battle each other using a dice and target mechanism.

Each player has a ship card which is split into a grid. Each square in the grid corresponds with a stat. When attacking, the player places a bullseye shaped grid over their opponent's ship card and rolls. Each spot on the grid corresponds to a number. The receiving player's stats are reduced if their ship is hit.

The first player to return enough gold to their hideout wins.

—description from the designer

Farm Warriors is a fun, educational board game where players grow, harvest, and deliver the crops to the city to save citizens from city-wide attacks.

Several cities on the planet have been infected by terrible illnesses and we are counting on you and your healing crops to save us!

In this game, all players become farmers.

Each round of the game, a city would send a news alert describing what the evil doers did to infect their citizens and which winning crops are needed to heal the citizens. Now the race to save the world is on! Farmers would race each other around the boardgame and collect resources to grow the winning crops. Make sure to dodge the wild farm attacks and make smart trades with the other farmers. The round ends when a farmer harvests one of the winning crops before the other farmers. The winning farmer receives a city trophy and prize money for saving the citizens.

Whoever saves the most cities will be titled the ultimate farming warrior.

Let's save the world, one crop at a time.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Night Cage: Monster Pack – Pathbreaker/Light Eater
description:
Contains:
4 Light Eaters
2 Pathbreakers

Light Eaters: When a Prisoner draws a Light Eater, it activates immediately. The Light Eater’s attack causes one Prisoner who isn’t adjacent to any other Prisoners to go lights out. These Prisoners are referred to as “isolated.” The active player decides who will be affected. The Light Eater’s target can be any isolated prisoner, including the prisoner who drew it. After resolving this effect, the active Player flips the Light Eater tile and places it as a pit (if they are still lit).

Pathbreakers: When a Pathbreaker is drawn, the active prisoner must place it so one of its faces points at them. Pathbreakers do not attack when revealed. Once placed, a Pathbreaker is motion-activated like a Wax Eater, attacking when a player moves into or out of its line of sight. However, a Pathbreaker’s line of sight is limited to the two directions of its faces. When a Pathbreaker attacks, it turns every tile in a straight line from its faces into a pit regardless of walls, pits, or open spaces. If the Pathbreaker is still lit after it attacks, it turns into a pit.

Distributed at Gen Con 2022 and included in the deluxe edition of The Night Cage: Shrieking Hollow.

description:
A free promotional demonstration version of Splito.

In standard Splito, there are 109 cards, for up to 8 players. In this free demo version, there are 27 cards, for 3 or 4 players. Each player receives 6 or 8 cards at the beginning of the game (depending if there are 3 or 4 players), compared to 13 in the standard version.

Generally, most rules are the same. At the end of each turn, each player chooses a card (Objective or Value) from their hand and decides to place it between themselves and their neighbor on the left or right. At the end of the 6 or 8 rounds, each player checks the Objectives in each zone between the players, and the two common Objectives, revealed at the beginning of the game, are also attributed to the zones that have fulfilled the conditions. The score of each player is calculated by multiplying the points obtained in the zone to their left by the points in the zone to their right.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Bakufu is a competitive and fun strategic card game for 2-4 players, where your choices matter. With multiple paths to victory and game changing cards, how will you outsmart your enemies?

It's 1192 AD in Japan and the Emperor is weak. Through either war or peace, you see an opportunity to become the first Shogun of Japan.

Each turn, you can build up your castle, attack your rivals with soldiers, play characters to change the game, or choose a peaceful path to victory to establish your new Bakufu government. It's easy to turn the tide towards victory, and fun to foil your enemies' plans!

Each game is different, as players can easily change their fortunes to win through two routes:

War or Peace: You choose!

Islamstory The Messenger, is the best game based on the Messenger's life (Muhammad).  Place your cards in chronological order, so as to reconstruct the story of the Messenger’s life (peace and blessings upon him).

Carefully shuffle the pack of cards and hand out 3 to 5 cards to each player according to the difficulty level you have chosen. 3 cards: easy; 4 cards: medium; 5 cards: difficult. Place the pile of remaining cards on the playing surface and turn over the first two cards from the deck; they shall be your starting point. Place the cards facing upwards in front of you. Choose a card, announce the title of the event and put it in the right place so as to reconstruct the story of the Messenger (peace and blessings upon him). You can also read the summary of the event if you need additional clues.

Once the card is placed, flip it over and check if it respects the chronology. If the card is correctly placed, keep it on the playing surface. If it is not the case, put the card in the discarded pile and draw a new card.

The first player to correctly place all their cards in order on the timeline, wins!

—description from the publisher

What do you associate with the picture on the card? You probably know lots of answers! What is your first association? If you know, quickly press the button. Now you and the other players only have a few seconds to show your reflexes! The participants' task is to earn 10 cards by coming up with their own associations to the pictures on the cards. The player who gets 10 cards first is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Co kojarzy Ci się z obrazkiem na karcie? Z pewnością znasz mnóstwo odpowiedzi! Jakie jest Twoje pierwsze skojarzenie? Jeśli wiesz, szybko naciśnij przycisk. Teraz razem z pozostałymi graczami macie tylko kilka sekund na wykazanie się refleksem! Zadaniem w grze jest zdobycie 10 kart poprzez wymyślanie własnych skojarzeń do obrazków na kartach. Gracz, który zdobędzie je jako pierwszy, zostaje zwycięzcą!

—description from the publisher (Polish)

Was assoziieren Sie mit dem Bild auf der Karte? Sie kennen wahrscheinlich viele Antworten! Was ist Ihre erste Assoziation? Wenn Sie es wissen, drücken Sie schnell die Taste. Jetzt werden Sie und die anderen
Jetzt haben du und die anderen Spieler nur ein paar Sekunden Zeit, um eure Reflexe unter Beweis zu stellen! Die Aufgabe der Teilnehmer besteht darin, 10 Karten zu sammeln, indem sie eigene Assoziationen zu den Bildern auf den Karten finden. Der Spieler, der zuerst 10 Karten hat, ist der Gewinner!

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Too Many Bones: 12 oz. Coffee Promo Card
description:
Comes with the Too Many Bones coffee available on the Chip Theory Games webstore and at conventions.

Text:

"12 oz. Coffee
Single-use / Light (Doesn't use a Loot slot)

Use in various oz. amounts to:

Drink (12 oz.): At start of battle, add 1 X to your backup plan.

Bribe Monster: (12 oz.): Go first when playing Dangerous Darts.

Pay Keysmith (24 oz.): Open a Trove Loot.

Get Snobby (24 oz.): Persistent. Whenever you draw a Loot with "Coffee" in its name, draw another Loot.

This tastes nutty, with caramel tones."

A print-and-play card game (with small board for the position of the ball on the pitch) based on the game of rugby union.
The game is won - as is rugby itself - with the most points (5 or 7 for a try, 3 for a penalty) over two halves.  Players each play cards representing an attack (if they have possession of the ball) or defence.  The deck represents the clock, so as it runs out, so does the 40-minute half.

—description from the designer

Ivion combines the intensity of a fighting game with the strategy of deck-building card games to create an experience unlike any other. In Ivion, your deck IS your character, and you build it from a variety of classes and specializations. Each character type has numerous cards from which to choose, along with wildly different playstyles. Mix and match them to create your own, unique character!

Upon the field of battle, crush your opponent with various strikes, stabs, slashes, spells, and other mayhem at your disposal. Be careful, though, as they have numerous ways to block, dodge, parry, fizzle, and disrupt your assault. Only one can be the victor, and the battle will be bloody!

In Ivion: The Ram and the Raven, protect your clan as the stalwart Steward, or fulfill your oath as the enigmatic Watcher. Fully compatible with all other Ivion products.

—description from the publisher
30-card Gateway promo deck first handed out at Gen Con 2022.

In each Q case, you try to solve a mystery case with 32 clues, with players revealing one clue at a time until all cards have been revealed or discarded.

Choose a card from your hand and place it on the table, so all players can read or see the entire information.
We recomend you read out loud all shared info when you place it on the table. If you play a clue that happens to be irrelevant
to the case, you lose points at the end of the game, but be careful! Some clues are vital to resolve the case.

You can share and expose your theories at any moment and talk about the cards you have in your hand but you cannot show them to the other players and you may only read out loud the words written in bold or the text framed inside an image:

At the end of the game, when all clue cards have been revealed or discarded, you must check carefully all the available information and prepare a theory of what happened, working all together. Then, open the questionnaire and answer all questions. During this phase of the game, you can speak freely about your discarded cards, or the information you remember of them. Each right answer will add two points.

—user summary

After finding Greybeard's legendary treasure, The Nocturne and his crew sink somewhere in the Salt Isles. Captain Hooktip gives the order to abandon ship. What has caused the sinking? Where is the treasure?

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Tras encontrar el legendario tesoro de Barbagrís, El Nocturno y su tripulación se hunden en algún lugar de Las Islas Saladas. La capitana Puntadegarfio da orden de abandonar el barco. ¿Qué ha provocado el hundimiento?, ¿dónde está el tesoro?

—description from the back of the box

The company of the Pufferfish has arrived at the island of the Tongue of Fire in the Faraway, following the trail of Azsir's treasure. Orencio Barrilete's screams have woken everyone up. Something has stung him and it could be poisonous. You must find out all the details of what happened.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

La compañía del Pez Globo ha llegado a la isla de La Lengua de Fuego en La Lejanía, siguiendo la pista del tesoro de Azsir. Los gritos de Orencio Barrilete han despertado a todos. Algo le ha picado y podría ser venenoso. Deberéis averigur todos los detalles de lo sucedido.

—description from the back of the box

Captain Quinqué has ended up covered in feathers and tar in the middle of the weekly market in the port of La Veranda. How has someone as respected as Quinqué ended up feathered? What series of events have led to this unexpected outcome? Was it just an accident?

—description from the back of the box (translated)

El capitán Quinqué ha acabado cubierto de plumas y brea en mitad del mercado semanal del puerto de La Veranda. ¿Cómo alguien tan respetado como Quinqué ha acabado emplumado?, ¿qué sucesión de eventos han llevado a este inesperado desenlace?, ¿ha sido sólo un accidente?

—description from the back of the box

You are all spies who have infiltrated an ambassador's mansion, each working for different countries. Accomplish missions and try to sabotage your target's missions to score points.

Each player gets a map printed off on a single sheet of paper, then hides it behind a shield, clipboard, etc. Identify your target - the player to their left. As you play, you'll be trying to figure out where they go while avoiding the player on the right that's tracking you!

Each round, you'll move from one room to another, completing or sabotaging missions, then asking your target a specific question. Your target must tell you the truth, but each question can only be asked once per game. Some questions reveal more information than others, but picking the right time to ask them can be really helpful.

At the end of the game, reveal your sheets. You'll score points from completing missions, sabotaging the missions your targets completed, and bonus points if you identified where your target went. Most points wins.

—description from the designer

Four unknown descendants of the Emperor have deciphered the forgotten legends and called upon the powerful guardian spirits of Oshra. Their ritual unearthed new treasures but also brought greed into the hearts of many. Will the new guardian leaders take over the land, or will the established powers prevail?

This expansion for Hidden Leaders comes with 3 new modules:


     A new faction, four leaders, and 20 new heroes. The guardians are a powerful race. Everyone can use them, but use them sparingly or you might make the Guardian Leaders win. This adds a new layer of deduction, a new winning condition and many powerful new abilities to the game.
     Artifacts will upgrade your leader character. Each artifact gives the respective player a unique ability that can be used 2-3 times during the game. This gives every player a unique play style and exclusive strategic options.
     A bag full of nasty corruption tokens. Two times during the game, a wave of corruption allows you to secretly change the alliance of your heroes or the heroes of your friends. But be careful: some of these changes are dangerous traps that players can use to protect their heroes from attacks of their friends.


—description from the publisher

description:
Marble Bobsleigh is a dexterity game that let people of all ages play Winter Olympics indoor.

When the trigger-marbles give a ‘Go’ sign, all players quickly and precisely release their player-marbles to let them roll down the tracks to go into the finish area. Only one player can reach the goal.

Experience the dynamic and energetic play of the bobsleigh game on your table!

In Marble Bobsleigh 2nd Edition, the game board has reduced in size. Now, this game is compatible with smaller table. The game board has totally a different shape of tracks. It was more of straight lines with corners. Now, it is in a spiral form. Plus, the point system has been revised.

Ragnarok: The Board Game is a betting and meeple-rolling board game based on a global IP video game, Ragnarok Online.

In each round, players plan out how they will deploy their mercenaries to subjugate monsters. Those mercenaries are wooden meeples with different shapes. They select which and how many mercenaries to roll to win each monster card or item card. Depend on the posture of the meeples they roll, they gain combat powers. And the player with the highest combat power wins the card.

Players start with unique innate abilities which can be upgraded as they play the game.

When the round 5 ends, the play who achieves the most points by subjugating the monsters becomes the winner of the game.

Achieve items! Defeat monsters! And become the renowned adventurer!

Hackers is a quick & punchy print-and-play card drafting & hand management card game for four players, themed around the 1995 film of the same name.

Players draw, play and swap cards to hack the planet, at the end of the player with the most data wins!

The Haunted House: Gemblo is an abstract strategy game. The original game Gemblo is themed with the Haunted House.

It includes character and ghost cards which can twist the game play. The ghosts give players extra points when they are captured.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Haunted House: Toc Toc Wood Man
description:
The Haunted House: Toc Toc Wood Man is a simple dexterity balance game with 3-dimensional components. 
This game is the Haunted House version of the original game, Toc Toc Woodman (Click Clack Lumberjack).

It includes ghost cards with the ghosts from the Haunted House animated movie. 
Those cards give the players extra points or trigger special abilities when bark pieces are dropped on them.

Moji Challenge is an exciting, fast-paced card game for all those who love naughty and crazy parties!

Join your friends in taking on fun MOJI-roles and wacky challenges of three different difficulty levels! If any of you manage to succeed, you will gain “FPs” – Fame Points! Moji Party Set contains:

The MOJI CHALLENGE: CLASSIC DECK allows you to become eMOJI masters and discover how amazingly entertaining it can be to express different emotions in all sorts of situations – especially when THE emotions and situations do not match or are totally inappropriate!

The MOJI CHALLENGE: ANIMAL DECK will help you reveal your true, hidden nature! Are you a tiger or a pussycat? Discover for yourselves by meeting humorous challenges or else passing them onto your friends with a wicked smile!

The MOJI CHALLENGE: FANTASY DECK will help you turn into all sorts of monster creatures, such as: anemic zombies, decadent vampires or bloodthirsty werewolves! Worlds of wicked challenges await each and every player!

Only one of you can be victorious – at the end, the winner will set the final MOJI-challenge for all the other players!

—description from the publisher

The Haunted House: Sealing Talisman is an unplugged coding & puzzle game.

Ghosts are running all over the place! Help Shinbi and Geumbi to capture the ghosts!

Players program the movements of Shinbi & Geumbi figures so they can get to the destination.

description:
A Sherlock Holmes inspired collection of brainteasers from Professor Puzzle.

Use your powers of deduction and reasoning with this compendium of three different mystery games. Featuring over 150 riddles, puzzles and enigmas, this collection of games and quizzes will put your brain under closer scrutiny than a magnifying glass!

The Case of the Double Agent:

Pose the right questions to uncover your new alter-ego, or exercise your powers of deduction to reorder some tricky anagrams.

The Case of the Cryptic Clue:

Far from elementary – follow the clues to crack the case.

The Case of the Curious Conundrums:

Summon your inner detective to solve these testing riddles.

Roll&Ghosts is a fun and fast Roll&Write/Print n' Play game for 1 to 10+ players, for ages 9 and up, featuring art inspired by vintage cartoons from the 1920s and 1930s.
For 10-15 minutes per game, players will use their dice to explore a haunted house, loot treasures and catch spooky ghosts before fear takes over and forces them to flee with whatever they have collected.

During your turn, you will roll 7 dice and use 1 of the movement dice to enter a room in the house, then use the rest of the results to find and collect treasures, or to create a portal to trap ghosts with. Every treasure and ghost is worth points.

By spending some of the collected resources, you will be able to perform special actions, such as re-rolling the dice or changing the value of a die.

You will also have a dice reserve where you can store up to 3 results that you can spend and use at any time during the gameplay.

The game ends when the Scare-o-meter counter reaches the end.

The player with the most scored points wins the game.

You can also play using the mission cards, which will demand you to collect specific resources before the game ends.

—description from the publisher

Heed well my words, for I am Mentor. I have much knowledge to impart upon you but no time to do so. As we speak, a contingent of minions loyal to the evil sorcerer Zargon escapes our grasp. If this wicked group of villians should succeed in retreating back to Zargon's Dread Host there will be dire consequences! The people of this land tell tales of your good deeds.

Help the Realm with this critical undertaking and you shall take the first step on the road to becoming true heroes! We have tracked these sinister forces to a location nearby. I can provide you guidance using the far voice spell, but cannot intervene on your behalf lest Zargon will discover your vital mission and seek to block your path. The Quest is Calling!

Players can expect this to be an apprentice-level challenge with opportunities for new gamers to have discussions about a variety of game mechanics and characters. We added a few rules in this quest to test some ideas, teleported some expansion content over, and broke from the traditional one-page format, to provide greater context and make this a more robust tool for Zargon. You could play this game as a “Quest 0” and head into the HeroQuest core quest book better equipped, or you might choose to run this as an alternative to ‘the Trial’.

Promo card given away at GenCon 2022 head-to-head challenge.

Autobot Megatron is a 5-cost Robot that is both an Autobot and a Decepticon, but is an adversary to neither.  He provides 3 power at 5 range, and has a Block protecting all characters.  For 1 Energon, he can teleport target character to any space.

boardgame: Dogs of War: Viscount Perceval d'Hiver

A new playable dog of war that comes with its own busts and special ability.

     Viscount Perceval d’Hiver, special ability: glory (gains 3 extra VPs if supports a glorious victory)

description:
Lobo is a solitaire card game for Crab Fragment Labs' Vines (or Postas)  deck.  The Vines deck has eleven cards in five suits.  Lobo uses 1 - 10 in all five suits.

In Lobo, the Player and the Wolf start with a face up hand of four cards each.  Through a series of captures, the player is attempting to get rid of all of the Wolf’s cards.

Your goal is to empty the Wolf’s hand, by playing cards from your own hand.  Even if you cannot defeat the Wolf, you are still trying to reduce its point value as far as you can. You will play several hands against the Wolf, racing to 100 points.

On each turn, you have the choice of several actions. The Wolf does not take turns.

Perfect - play one card from your hand to capture one card of the same rank from the Wolf’s hand, then draw a card.

Sum - play more than one card from your hand, which must add up exactly to one card in the Wolf’s hand, then draw a card.

Split - play one card, and capture more than one card adding up exactly to the value of the card you play, then draw a card into the Wolf's hand.

Over - play one card from your hand to capture one smaller card from the Wolf’s hand, then draw one card into the Wolf's hand for every point of difference.

Fold - If you feel you cannot safely make any more plays, you can stop.

If the Wolf's hand is empty, you score the points remaining in your hand.

If you Fold, the Wolf scores the points remaining in their hand.

Prestige is an asymmetrical, semi-cooperative, city-building, card game in which players must collaborate to build a great ancient city to stand as a testament to their civilization's prestige for generations to come, or fail and be forgotten to time. Players will share economic resources, city-building cards, and defeat. However, if they succeed, only the leader of the tribe most represented in the city will be remembered throughout all of time.

The game can be enjoyed solo, or with up to four players, and is played using city-building cards and economic resource tokens. Each player takes the mantle of a Tribal Leader over one of six unique tribes, each with their own focus and deck of city-building cards. As Tribal Leaders, players must negotiate with one another to play cards from their own tribe, to enhance their dominion over the city, while also ensuring the city doesn't fall to chaos or destruction due to their selfishness.

Pulling from a shared deck of compiled city-building cards, players take turns either placing or discarding cards from their hand. When placed, this card will trigger positive (or negative)effects based on the type(s) of cards adjacent in the play area. However, when a card is discarded, it is considered unplayable for the rest of the game and attracts attention from prowling Invaders who wish to do harm to the city. Each action must be carefully balanced to pursue one's own personal interests while maintaining the health of the shared city.

—description from the publisher

The game is set 1870 during the German-French-War. There are the following 8 factions for 8 players:

blue-white-red.................France
black-white....................Preußen
white-green....................Sachsen
black-yellow-green.............Sachsen-Weimar-Eisenach
blue-white.....................Bayern
black-red......................Würtemberg
yellow-red-yellow..............Baden
red-white......................Hessen

Each player has his own track ans starts at space 1. Whoever gets on space 50 (iron cross) first wins the game.
Movement is determined by drawing cards. There are 10 cards: One card for each faction, a card for the North German Confederacy (NGF) and a cord for the iron cross.

If a player card is drawn, the player in question moves 1 space forward. If the NGF-card is drawn, all players move 1 space. A player standing on space 24 may only move to the iron cross, if he draws the iron-cross-card.
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach the destination spaces wins the game.

The game of creating friends out of enemies & enemies out of friends. If you like games, drinking and sabotaging your friends, this game is for you! Drinks with Frenemies is a card game that brings the worst out of you and rewards you for it!

It's an entertaining party game that will keep you on your toes as you adjust to the ever-changing rules. It's a drinking game that's not family friendly. Meaning you can play with any type of beverage you prefer. In fact, we've made gameplay rules for those who wish not to drink at all. From water to moonshine, we think there's no perfect way to ruin... uh... enhance your relationships!

It's a great icebreaker game that gets you moving, thinking and laughing. We've designed it to be instantly playable and incredibly enjoyable with rules that are simple to follow and cards that are easy to understand. It's just as much fun to watch others play as it is to play the game yourself. It's a great addition to any game night, pre-game or party and makes for a great gift for birthdays, graduations and holidays!

Admiral Zheng He: Sailing Alone is a small, solitaire expansion for Admiral Zheng He consisting of just 18 cards and 4 pages of rules, much of it historical background.

You as a Trader must contend against three other Traders who play effectively, but using simple rules.

You must also contend, for the first time with Pirates and Ambushes, that can arise at particular ports without notice. Voyager beware!

You can even add these events to the multi-player game if you wish.

-description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Ninth Edition): War Zone Nachmund – Rift War
description:
Haarken Worldclaimer, Herald of the Apocalypse, leads one of the mightiest traitor hosts every assembled. His mission, ordained by Abaddon the Despoiler himself, is to smash aside all Imperial resistance and claim the Nachmund Gauntlet in its entirety. This lawless place is home to pirates, xenos, traitors and renegades – yet Nachmund is strategically vital to the Imperium of Man, for it remains the most stable route known through the Great Rift. Should it fall, contact between Terra and the Imperium Nihilus would be all but lost.

The Imperium cannot afford to lose the Gauntlet, but its forces are already exhausted and overstretched. Even as the systems around the southern end of the Gauntlet are reinforced, sedition begins to take hold, and no amount of heroism and sacrifice may be enough to throw back the Worldclaimer...

In this 88-page hardback book, you'll find:

- The story of Haarken Worldclaimer’s conquest of the Nachmund Gauntlet, including the siege of the Chaos Knight world Dharrovar, and the Imperium’s attempts to resist them at all costs
- An in-depth War of Desperation campaign system for all modes of play – fight for control of three brutal War Zones, each with its own special rules and Legendary Mission
- Crusade rules to represent mobile Space Marine raiding forces and devoted hordes of Chaos Undivided, plus new arcane rituals for the Thousand Sons
- Additional rules content, including a Codex Supplement for the Castellans of the Rift Chapter of Space Marines, and Armies of Renown for Tzaangor-themed Thousand Sons and the Drukhari's Haemonculus Covens

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000: Chapter Approved – War Zone Nephilim Grand Tournament Mission Pack
description:
This edition of Chapter Approved is a fantastic addition to the gaming toolbox of any Warhammer 40,000 matched play fan, offering updated ways to muster your armies, secondary objectives for every faction, and missions designed specifically for this season of the game. Whether you're a tournament regular or simply like to ensure your battles are always fought on an even footing, this is the essential supplement for matched play games of Warhammer 40,000.

Inside this 136-page softback book, you'll find:

– Two mission packs, each comprising nine missions each for Incursion and Strike Force battles, each designed to offer a wide array of challenges to players
– Warzone Nephilim secondary objectives, featuring three default secondary objectives for this Grand Tournament Mission Pack, bespoke secondary objectives for every Warhammer 40,000 faction, and a host of general secondary objectives for common use
– Dynamic new rules for allocating and generating Command points, as well as changes to choosing Relics and Warlord Traits
– Guidelines for setting up and playing a matched play or Grand Tournament-level competitive game, including suggested battlefield size, time limit, and how best to determine secondary objectives
– A blank tournament scoring sheet for you to photocopy and use, with an annotated example of how to fill it out
– The full core rules needed for matched play games of Warhammer 40,000, including a glossary and explanation of rare rules, as well as an introduction to the Battle Ready standard of painting for your miniatures

Take your matched play games to new heights with a wide range of secondary objectives for every faction, new rules for Command points, and a range of balanced and competitive missions. Whether you're a seasoned veteran or a newcomer to the exciting world of matched play, Chapter Approved: War Zone Nephilim has something for you.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar (Third Edition): Season of War – Thondia
description:
As the Era of the Beast rages on, the savage realm of Ghur awakens and the bone-strewn plains of Thondia are consumed by war. A wellspring of primordial energy has burst its banks, spilling out and settling into the souls of mortals. Rival powers fight tooth and claw in the wastes, strange dreams echo with monstrous roars, and outbreaks of animalistic mutation transform common folk into wrathful beasts. Yet all this pales before the rise of a fierce new force in the cosmos: the mystical incarnates. When the dust settles, the Mortal Realms will never be the same again.

Season of War: Thondia is the first in a new series of expansion books for Warhammer Age of Sigmar, containing a mixture of detailed background lore, gripping story, and immersive rules for the conflicts shaping the narrative of the Mortal Realms.

Inside this 112-page hardback book, you'll find:

- An exciting narrative exploring the wild Realm of Beasts, as the Prime Huntress Yndrasta investigates the strange events transforming the land
- Detailed maps, history, and lore for Ghur, including a bestiary of the flora, fauna, and factions that make their homes on the alpha-continent of Thondia
- Rules for using the Krondspine Incarnate of Ghur, a powerful new unit type available to all factions, plus warscroll battalions and battleplans for recreating the story of Season of War: Thondia
- Realm rules for fighting in Thondia, including Realmsphere magic, monstrous denizens, and mysterious terrain, plus an open play battlepack for randomly generating your own Ghurish battleplan
- A narrative Path to Glory battlepack for founding your own Thondian outpost to explore the Realm of Beasts, plus Anvil of Apotheosis rules for creating Ghurish heroes
- A matched play Strife in Thondia battlepack including core battalions, battle tactics, grand strategies, campaign rules, and battleplans for matched play games

Players roll a die and then move their pawns from their starting area moving around the board finishing on one of there own finishing spaces. 
The main part of the game is using your pawns to stop other players from reaching the finish.

Kind of a more 'irritating' verion of the game Trouble.

—user summary

boardgame: In Search of the Dragon's Treasure
description:
Each player chooses a Knight in the color of their choice (red, blue, green, or yellow) and picks a weapon to place on their knight. then they spin the wheel. determining turn order from highest to lowest spin, ties re-roll.

You then spin the wheel to move around the board, journeying toward the dragons cave to steal the treasure. following instructions on tiles you land on. when you reach a spot that says stop, you must stop there till your next turn. multiple players can occupy the same space.

When you reach the colored squares in front of the dragons cave, you stop on your color. on your next turn, you must try to spin your color. you cannot proceed until you get your color, which grabs you a bag of gold to place on your knight. you then immediately spin again and move that many spaces.

The first player to reach Avalon Castle wins the game.

—user summary

A man has been brutally murdered.

A deranged serial killer is on the loose. The killer is known as The Night Stalker, because they only strike at night.

You are a detective, tasked with tracking down and arresting the killer. To do this, you will investigate crime scenes by collecting clues and evidence, to build your evidence dossier to arrest suspects.
You have narrowed down your list of prime suspects to four people. One of these is the Night Stalker. Through a process of elimination, your goal is to determine which suspect is the killer, and arrest them.
Time is running out. If you have not found and arrested the killer amongst your suspects before the game ends, the Night Stalker wins, and disappears into the shadows.

This game was an entry in the 2022 Solitaire Print and Play Contest, and is a low ink prototype for play testing.

—description from the designer

Imagine a world where punks are in control! But will it be Steampunks or Solarpunks? And what about the Chromepunks and the mysterious Cyberpunks? Battle it out, to see if solar or steam will dominate in this attractive futuristic-themed card game.

Steampunks vs Solarpunks is a game for two to five players aged 7+ which takes about half an hour to play. With a bag that constantly flips to show trumps, the game rewards careful bidding and trick-playing skills.

Each round, once the cards have been dealt, the players start by bidding to indicate the number of tricks they think they'll make. These bids are revealed simultaneously and then the round is played out. Trumps are either 'Steam' or 'Solar'. This can change several times in a round, keeping everyone on their toes. The round-winner is the most accurate bidder, with the Chromepunks used for breaking ties. Up to seven rounds are played in all.

Steampunks vs Solarpunks makes a great 'filler' game for a wide range of ages. Simple to learn, the game-play has surprising depths and keeps everyone in with a chance of winning right to the end.

—description from the publisher
A promo pack for Transformers Deck-Building Game containing 2 new characters (Ultra Magnus & Skywarp), a boss (Overlord) and a new special scheme.

boardgame: Стальная Арена: Высокое напряжение (Steel Arena: High Voltage)
description: The Steel Arena: High Voltage expansion changes the basic rules of the game, offering players four new game modes

boardgame: Dungeon Fighter: Second Edition – Betty Loudscream Promo Hero
description:
A Kickstarter-exclusive Hero.

This "vampire metal queen" has a chilling self-healing skill: her "Blood Donation" ability allows her to "kindly ask" one of her teammates to "willingly donate" some of their life energy to her.

Card text: before a fight, Betty can force one of the other party members to lose 1 HP; if she does, she will gain 2 HP in return.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Mine Artifact – Olde Bell Promo Card
description:
Olde Bell - Mine Artifact - Promo 551

Once per Adventure, you may ring the Olde Bell to cancel a Darkness or Growing Dread card. Then choose another Darkness or Growing Dread card and cancel it on the D6 roll of 4+. You max contiune to choose and cancel cards like this until you fail one of the rolls.

This was available from the Roll to Move 2022 sale at the Flying Frog webstore for orders over 100$.

A set collection game in which players try to complete a full football team squad based on the Saudi League, whoever sets the first 11 player cards that meet certain criteria wins.

After shuffling, each player will be dealt 5 cards that will be kept secretly. In a player's turn, take 2 cards from the deck and play a maximum of 2 cards. The cards are either player cards used to build the squad or action cards that are similar in function to the monopoly deal-like games. E.g: the red card to kick a player card of an opponent, the penalty card to steal someone's player.

The winner's squad must be formed in one of those plans, 4-3-3 / 4-4-2 / 5-4-1. Also four player cards have to be stars, and lastly a sequence of 3 numbers or 3 numbers alike(each player card has a number) in the same position must be acquired.

يجمع كل لاعب فريق كرة قدم مكون من 11 لاعب حسب شروط الفوز، من يجمع الفريق أولا يفوز.

بعد خلط الكروت، يعطى كل لاعب 5 كروت. في دور اللاعب عليه أن يسحب كرتين وبإمكانه أن يلعب كرتين كحد أقصى سواء كانوا من كروت اللاعبين أو كروت الأكشن.

شروط الفوز: تكوين فريق حسب أحد الخطط، مثال 4-4-3. احتواء الفريق على 4 لاعبين نجوم، وأخيرا يجب أن يكون هناك 3 لاعبين ارقامهم متسلسلة أو متطابقة في نفس المركز.

Bootleg is a card game featuring the fast-paced life of a rum runner in historic, Prohibition-era America. The alcohol is all gone, and it is up to you to give the people what they want! Challenge up to four of your friends as you each race to collect enough sets of booze to satisfy the populace of a roaring American city and catch a sweet ride to get there. Just remember, the first one there gets the business while the others get caught! Use your connections with the crime families and famous gangsters to sabotage your opponents and bolster your own operation. And if you’re feeling lucky, your whole situation could change with the roll of the dice using the exciting underground casino cards!

This simple to learn, competitive game for two to five players is a great way to liven up any game night. No two games are ever the same as you strive to pull off the most profitable bootleg operation each round!

The game is played by drawing two new cards from the deck on each turn. You may then take up to three actions playing action / casino cards, placing a booze card into your collection or cash / action card (for cash value) in your bank, or buying one of three cards from the shared Speakeasy. Additionally, you may use an entire turn (don't draw or take any other actions) to purchase either a vehicle or drop-off location. Make sure you have plenty of cash in the bank because if you don't, other Bootleggers can take booze from your collection as payment for actions taken against you!

The first Bootlegger to have three full sets of booze in their collection, a vehicle, and a drop-off location satisfies their city's craving first to win the whole round! Play can be extended by tracking the total value of the winning Bootlegger's haul and compete to be the first to a pre-set value.

The Tactical Air and Artillery Support, is an optional expansion that can be used for the tactical wargame Assault Red Horizon 41.

It introduces artillery strikes and air attacks into the battle. Destroy buildings to take your opponent´s cover and send them running. Use the artillery to smash enemy troops or hinder their movement.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Ivion: The Fox and the Forest, stay one step ahead of nature as the craft Survivalist, or bring forth Summer's wrath as the Wilder. Fully compatible with all other Ivion products.

Ivion combines the intensity of a fighting game with the strategy of deck-building card games to create an experience unlike any other. In Ivion, your deck IS your character, and you build it from a variety of classes and specializations. Each character type has numerous cards from which to choose, along with wildly different playstyles. Mix and match them to create your own, unique character!

Upon the field of battle, crush your opponent with various strikes, stabs, slashes, spells, and other mayhem at your disposal. Be careful, though, as they have numerous ways to block, dodge, parry, fizzle, and disrupt your assault. Only one can be the victor, and the battle will be bloody!

—description from the publisher

Box contains 70 square tiles and instructions for two different games to play with them.  The edges of the tiles come in 4 different terrains: Farm, Ocean, Beach, and Forest.  The terrain of adjacent tiles must match.

The QuarterMaster Game
The tiles are divided evenly among 2 - 4 players.  Players take turns adding tiles to the board.  Players get extra turns for playing next to multiple existing tiles.  The winner is the first player to play all their tiles.

QuarterMaster Solitaire
Removing 6 random tiles, the goal is to form an 8 by 8 square with specific terrains on the outer border.  The result is scored by adding the size of the largest contiguous area of each terrain.

Arcaven’s Duel is a one-on-one battle game where players can use various spells in order to defeat their opponent.  The game is fast-paced (ending in under 15 minutes) and has multiple levels of strategy that can be utilized and developed.

Each player starts with the same 7 cards (which are wizard spells).  Players flip cards (casting spells) to gain charge tokens, attack the opponent or defend an attack.  Play KEY to get unique powerful spells from the LOCK deck, before your opponent does! The first player to remove all 10 hearts from their opponent wins!

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Colossal Moats & Barbicans
description:
Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Colossal Moats & Barbicans contains enlarged room tiles as well as enlarged Moats and Barbican tiles, tile trays with lids doubling as within-game tile holders, and a score track adjusted to the setup with the larger pieces (as they are not stored on the track in contrast to the standard game). The tiles are twice as big (i.e. four times bigger in area) compared to the standard tiles.

The expansion allows the Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Collector's edition to be played with larger pieces, especially convenient for solo play and conventions. For playing the game, the expansion requires the cards, card trays, and game pieces (player markers, coins, towers, tokens, etc.) of the base game.

The expansion contains two gameplay differences compared to the base game: 
1) The Foyer tiles (i.e. the starting tile for each player) of the base game have three entrances at both sides of the tiles. The Foyer tiles of the "Colossal Moats & Barbican" expansion have three entrances on one side and four entrances on the other side, thus allowing more variation in the starting position.
2) The optional Moats & Barbicans module have different Barbican tiles compared to the base game: In the base game, the Barbican tiles are dual-layered and overlap the Foyer pieces such that each Barbican always has an external drawbridge entrance. In the Colossal expansion, the Barbican tiles do not overlap the Foyer tiles and thus allow a Barbican without an external entrance (depending on the orientation of the adjacent Foyer tile), again allowing for more variation in the starting position compared to the base game.

This expansion was available as a bundle with Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Royal Collector's Edition in the Kickstarter campaign.
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
The object of the game is to be the player with the lowest score. Points are scored by adding the point value of your cards when each hand ends. The game ends when one player's score totals 100 points or more.

Each player is dealt four face-down cards. On their turn they will either draw a card from the deck or take the top-most discarded card. They will either switch it with one of their four cards, trying to make it lower (if drawn), or discard it. If the discard is selected, they must switch it with one of the four cards. All four cards are always face-down, forcing the player to remember them.

This continues until the deck runs out or someone has collected the one B-Low card and calls for the end of the round. Points are totaled by cards saved, lower is better.
A promo pack for Transformers Deck-Building Game containing a new character (Astrotrain) and four main deck cards.

Inky the Octopus dozed off while guarding his gems. Can you sneak the treasure away without tickling his tentacles?

​Slowly and carefully, players must try to twist and turn a gem from its hole without startling Inky. If they succeed, they keep it! Each squiggly gem provides a different challenge!

When all gems have been collected, the player with the most WINS!

This expansion adds 15 unnamed monument cards and 15 monument tokens (3 for each of the nations). Each monument has a different effect (special ability) and makes each nation in the game even more unique. Monuments are built by discarding the indicated amount of support cards.

boardgame: 1815, Scum of the Earth: HEBS Weather Deck
description:
Brand new to the Historic Epic Battle System, the HEBS Weather Deck expansion introduces the variable effects of inclement weather or even glorious sunshine to ANY of your HEBS games.

Simply shuffle the Weather Deck, draw a Weather card, and place a teardrop token on its first space. That effect remains in play each round. At the beginning of every new round, move the token along—if there are no more teardrop spaces, discard it and draw a new Weather card.
family: Series: Historic Epic Battle System (Hall or Nothing Productions)

description:
What's in the expansion box:
3 Patients (Character) Miniatures
3 Character Cards
3 Boss Monsters Miniatures
3 Boss Cards
6 Elite Monsters Miniatures
3 Monster Cards

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – MojoMania Scenario Pack
description:
Welcome, one and all!

Join us in the marvelous and magnificent Mojoverse, a terrifically televised realm of nonstop entertainment and jaw-dropping drama. We have a really special show for you tonight, folks: a bunch of upstart heroes think they can triumph over the trials before them, but will they actually manage to make a scene? Or will they drop off the charts like a bunch of has-beens? Stay tuned to find out!

This 78-card scenario pack introduces three brand-new scenarios to the game, each featuring a different member of the Mojoverse. These scenarios can be played standalone or sequentially as the game’s very first three-part mini-campaign. Also included are six new modular encounter sets that can be added to any Marvel Champions scenario, expanding the game’s customization options to starry new heights. Do you have what it takes to survive the most dangerous reality TV ever?

—description from publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
The goal of this interesting psycho game is to analyse the other players by means of 36 characteristics. The player who can judge himself in the same way as the others would judge him will win.

—user summary

A co-operative board game for 1 to 6 players about the Elizabethan theatre. It can be played as a straight board game or additionally as a narrative game.
The Company of 6 key people work together to put on a series of new plays over the course of one year. Events may help or hinder the Company whose goal is to make a profit of at least £200 in order to be invited to put on a performance for the Queen herself. Do badly and you could all end up in debtors prison! So be careful in the choices that you make.

—description from the designer

This is a memo game:
All cards are put face down on the table. Then you turn as many cards as necessary face up until each color and each shape can be seen at least once. Then all players try to remember the cards, turn them face down again and roll the dice. If one player has a matching card, he places it face down without looking at it on the corresponding field of his tray. But be careful! If you make a mistake, the bag of cement is threatened and your cards go to the next player. 
The winner is the player who has the most valid cards at the end.

—user summary

Mind the Mushrooms is an 18 card solitaire, tile-laying dungeon builder, with elements of "tower defense," hidden puzzles, and resource management.

Each turn you will choose a card to add to your dungeon. Build carefully because one wrong tunnel could let in monsters that have the ability to dig through walls, open doors and crash your Dungeon Gate.

MTM can be combined with the original Paperbag Dungeon.

—description from the designer

The angry birds are ready! Flip Red, Chuck, Bomb, Matilda, Jim, Jake and Jay on the meadow and try to hit as many pigs as possible.
The player with the most stars wins the game.

—user summary

Welcome to Ukraine, the country of talented people, rich lands, and amazing nature.

The events unfold in modern Ukraine, in the post-war period, where peace and well-being already reign. You become one of the governors of 4 regions in Ukraine.

You can mine resources on your holdings and use them to rebuild industry and economy. Your region, like any other, has a predominant resource that can be mined in much larger volumes.

In the game, as in reality, each region of Ukraine does not have some of minerals or resources. So, with the help of diplomacy, you have to make trade agreements with other regions for the sale of certain resources. When the first-player's token passes to you, you become the main trader for the given season and have the right to make a deal with every other player.

To develop industry, you need to rebuild 4 buildings in your region: 3 industrial buildings and 1 regional bank.

Three Cossack workers have been working on your lands since the beginning. But to rebuild the region, you will need new workers, whom you should train.

In order to improve the economy, you must fulfill government orders, that is, invest your resources in the development of Ukraine. Instead, your region will receive growth in the foreign-exchange reserves and various bonuses from the state (bonus cards).

The term of your governance is 12 seasons. In each season, various events occur that affect the general situation of the regions – shortage of extraction of certain resources, crisis, moratoriums on construction, over-extraction of resources, etc.

Check whether you have enough inspiration to be ahead of other governors in the fight for the prosperity and development of Ukrainian lands!

The Chomp Champ! Game from Mudpuppy is a fun twist on the classic game of spoons. Collect 4-of-a-kind and grab a shark tile, all other players then race to grab a shark tile, trying not to be the player left without one. Lose 6 shark teeth tokens and you're out! A wonderful new addition to game nights everywhere!

     The jawsome game of fast-acting fun!
     Includes: 40 cards, 4 shark tiles, 30 shark tooth tokens, instructions
     Shaped box: 6.75 x 7.75 x 1.75", 17 x 20 x 4.5 cm
     3-5 players, Ages 5+


—description from the publisher

Arms Race is a chess-like abstract strategy game for two players, which uses dice instead of chess pieces.

Each of the six faces of the dice match a chess piece:
1 is the Pawn, worth 1 point
2 is the Rook, worth 3 points
3 is the Knight, worth 3 points
4 is the Bishop, worth 5 points
5 is the Queen, worth 9 points
6 is the King, worth 13 points

Pieces move and capture according to their chess counterparts, except they promote to the next number on the list when capturing.

This creates a highly dynamic and ever changing game.

—description from the designer

An unashamed remake of a classic 1960s 3M Business Strategy game. Instead of building hotel chains, you will be creating airline empires across the good ol' USA. Start with small range Ford Tri-Motor aircraft and work your way up through a longer range Douglas DC3s and then ultimately the max range Boeing 247 aircraft as your airline adds more and more city routes. Outwit your opponents with your shrewd stock moves and become the wealthiest air baron. It's all here.

Air-Kwire is a FREE Print & Play game from Deer Valley Game Company.

—description from the designer

This is a scenario with multiple puzzles and mysteries, many of which the players will not know or understand at the beginning of the game. And that's okay! The goal is to figure out what to do, how to do it, and where to do it. You'll be solving challenges that will provide more context as you push through the Scenario. Focus on what you already know and you'll get there!

It's highly recommended to have a pencil and paper nearby.

Good luck! ...You'll need it.

—description from the publisher

Ivion combines the intensity of a fighting game with the strategy of deck-building card games to create an experience unlike any other. In Ivion, your deck IS your character, and you build it from a variety of classes and specializations. Each character type has numerous cards from which to choose, along with wildly different playstyles. Mix and match them to create your own, unique character!

Upon the field of battle, crush your opponent with various strikes, stabs, slashes, spells, and other mayhem at your disposal. Be careful, though, as they have numerous ways to block, dodge, parry, fizzle, and disrupt your assault. Only one can be the victor, and the battle will be bloody!

In Ivion: The Rune and the Rime, beat back the cold as the ruthless Hunstman, or claim it as your own as the mighty Winterborn. Fully compatible with all other Ivion products.

—description from the publisher

Several cards that slot into each other to create a 3D 'cactus'.

Each player starts with 7 cards and must insert a card on their turn, matching the flower on the bottom of their card with the flower on the notch they are sliding it into.

If someone doesn't have a card matching the colour, they draw another card.

A few special abilities exit (play 2x, skip next player, reverse play, and 'prune' (get rid of an entire branch and deal it out to the players, ideally giving everyone else one more card).

If cards fall, or touch the playing surface, they must be collected and added to your hand.

First player to run out of cards wins.

Doomlings is a funny, lightweight card game where you play traits for points over multiple rounds, called ages. Occasionally there will be catastrophes that affect players hands. Win by having the highest score before the third and final catastrophe brings about the end of the world.

This version includes:

    All base deck cards
    Dinolings Expansion
    Mythlings Expansion
    Multi-Color cards Expansion
    Techlings Expansion
    The Meaning of Life Expansion
    1 Motley Dominant card
    KSE Goldbox
    KSE Playmat
    KSE Fulfilled Gold card
    Kickstarter exclusive cards

Doomlings is a funny, lightweight card game where you play traits for points over multiple rounds, called ages. Occasionally there will be catastrophes that affect players hands. Win by having the highest score before the third and final catastrophe brings about the end of the world.

boardgame: The Fun Game of Switch (Second Edition)
description:
In The Fun Game of Switch players are trying to be the first to assemble a full train by rolling to move along tracks and switch between the inner and outer track lines. It is a simple spin-and-move game with 'Switches' that can alter your direction of movement. When reaching a switch, the player stops and rolls again to see which track line to use to move further. It is a simple even-odd 50-50 determination.

The goal is to build a train, starting with an engine, then two rail cars and end with a caboose. The first player to do this wins. Building is accomplished by drawing the correct card (cardboard pieces) at random when the player lands on a "Draw Card" space. The "cards" are upside down. A locomotive engine must be drawn first, then the rail cars. The caboose is last, just like in the old kids’ story The Little Red Caboose. If the ‘card’ drawn is not the correct one, it is returned to the draw pile.

This second edition is very different than the original edition of The Fun Game of Switch in design and components. The original edition was a “spin and move” race game with a small network of tracks and switches. Just about everything is different other than the name, box cover and the use of a (thin) train switching theme.

While it appears the original edition game publication is from the 50’s (based on web info and artwork) this second edition appears to date from the latter 70’s and into the 80’s. The original publisher initially produced this game under the  Warren Built Rite Games brand but stopped after a few years. Starting around 1981 the Tee Pee Toys, Jessup Paper Box Co. produced this game and a few others under license with a few small changes to the box and inclusion of punch out player tokens on the game board rather than pawns.
publishers: Tee Pee Toys, Jessup Paper Box Co.

description:
The Awkward Guests companion app has several more cases in addition to the cases included with the game.

The companion app allows players to enter their solution to the case into the app and find out if they are right or wrong.

If one or more players have guessed the correct solution to the case, they win the game.

If a player is incorrect, they cannot guess a solution on their next turn but can continue to play the game - the solution is not revealed.

When playing without the app, players must examine the solution in the rulebook to determine if they are right or wrong, and players who guess the wrong solution are eliminated from the game.

The app is required when playing the game Solo.

The Thinning Veil is a player solo or co-operative board game of horror fantasy inspired by Celtic mythology and Irish legend. It can be played as a narrative based progressive campaign across many scenarios or as one shot games from the Game Night Scenarios book. This gives players the option of an adventure game format or a dungeoncrawl experience. This roguelike system offers elements of puzzle solving, objective based and narrative based scenarios, and compelling gameplay, exploring a world familiar yet fantastical.

Once you have selected a Hero your journey begins! Will you be valorous and pure, or villainous and corrupt? Will your legend help bring humanity to a new dawn of fresh hope or prolong the night, plunging us into ever deeper darkness? Confront treacherous and deceitful cultists with hidden agendas, fight restless spirits and veil-dwelling horrors from the Land of the Dead; combat or outwit the denizens of the Other Side of Midnight, in this exciting game of high adventure!

—description from the designer

Hell Level introduces a set of thematic miniatures. Nothing will be the same after all hell is unleashed. Advised for players that are up for a challenge and wish to finish the game on true hell difficulty. Hell Level expansion can be used with just the Core Lobotomy 2 Manhunt and with the Inferno Expansion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Fellowship of the Ring: Saga Expansion
description:
Frodo Baggins and his companions have only just begun their legendary quest to destroy the One Ring and defeat Sauron. The journey is fraught with danger, from the brutal Uruk-hai to the terrifying Balrog, to say nothing of the dreaded Nazgûl that hound them at every step. But even in the face of such peril, Frodo and the others must press on—the fate of the world depends on it!

Experience the beginning of the journey to destroy the One Ring in The Fellowship of the Ring Saga Expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game! This expansion contains six scenarios that recreate the adventures featured in the first part of the legendary The Lord of the Rings saga. You’ll also find over 50 hero and player cards, which can be used to build or enhance your decks for any The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game scenario or campaign!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
What is the real name of Daredevil? In what year was the first Nintendo released? Who are the members of the Justice League? The geek galaxy is vast, test yourself with Studio Vrac on TV series, movies, literature, video games, manga, comics, comic books! Even your WTF spirit will be challenged! Each team (of two or more players) chooses one of the four characters that will give them a special power. Players roll the die to advance, each correct answer allows them to roll the die again to advance. The goal: to reach the finish line with the maximum number of points.

Each team (of two or more players) chooses one of the four characters that will give them a special power. Players roll the die to advance, each correct answer allows them to roll the die again to advance. The goal: to reach the finish line with the maximum number of points.

Create fanciful Matchie creatures by stacking transparent ear, wings, feet, and tail cards on top of a baby Matchie tray.

Players take turns adding body cards one at a time to the Matchie trays. If you complete a Matchie and the completed Matchie matches the requirements of a creator card, you get to collect that Matchie and the creator card.

The first player to collect three Matchies wins the game.

Frog Hop comes with two game modes: co-op or versus.

In both games players try to catch as many bugs as possible before the frog timer runs out. Players turn over a disc and try to find the pictured bug from the lake using frog tongues.

The player with the most points (bugs) wins.

The companion app provides the game with many more stories to solve. The stories are all unique to the app and not found in the base game. The app only has stories and nothing else. A physical copy of the base game is still required to play.

These stories have the same format (standard information block, graphics, font, etc.) as the physical story cards in the game. Some stories are marked with the "Parental Advisory" warning, just like stories in the physical game.

There are 35 stories, but that may change with updates.

Stories can be marked as played - the electronic equivalent of setting aside a story card once it's been played.

There is also a "Random Story" button that selects a random story from among those in the app.

7 Wonders Companion is the official game aid for 7 Wonders.

It includes an exclusive wonder: Cupertino.

—user summary

This application will offer you unparalleled ease of play, with multiple options:


     A new exclusive wonder: Play with Cupertino, created specially for the occasion!
     Random drawing of wonders: For a quick game setup. You can also choose not to play with some wonders
     Efficient Scoring Calculation: Total your points in an easy way with a system that's designed and optimized specifically for 7 Wonders
     Keep track of your statistics: Record your games and look at the evolution of your scores over time
     Quick access to the science scoring total and the building chain: Don't lose any more time, and be more precise in your forecasts during the game
     Expansion Support:  Also helps when playing with Leaders or Cities! You can even enable Teamplay!


—description from the publisher


See: https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/1/blogpost/11210
Line up three of your colored balls into one of three specific configurations on a modular board made up of wooden rings to win.

boardgame: Mobile Suit Z Gundam Tactical Card Game
description:
In this tactical card game based on the Mobile Suit Z Gundam television series, players assemble a fleet of random spaceships, mobile suits (mecha), and pilots with the goal of eliminating their opponents' forces.

Each player starts with 4 random Ship Cards (or 3 in a 5-6 player game) and 8 Special Cards (Mobile Suit (MS) Cards, Pilot Cards, and Event Cards) to form their fleets. On their turn, each player draws one Special Card from the deck and has the option of playing an MS with a Pilot from their hand to their fleet, playing an Event Card, attacking an opponent's Ship with their own Ship or MS, or attacking an opponent's MS with their own MS. In combat, each player rolls a number of 6-sided dice equal their unit's Combat Strength and score a hit for each die roll less than their Hit Number. When a unit is destroyed, the victor takes the card and adds it to their score pile.

The game ends in one of three conditions, decided on by players before the start of the game:
a) The Special Card deck is depleted.
b) One player loses all the spaceships in their fleet.
c) Only one player has spaceships in their fleet.

At the game end, each player tallies up their score based on the opponent Ships, MS units, and Pilots they destroyed in combat. The player with the highest score is the winner.

In Risky Kittens, players are visiting an animal shelter full of cute and cuddly kittens… until their claws come out. Push your luck as you pet adorable kittens while trying to avoid getting scratched!

Each round, draft kitten cards. The more scratches you have, the sooner you get to pick your kitten! Roll to pet your kitten earning hearts and scratch stickers. Each kitten has a unique ability! Convert hearts to Purr Points, but if your kitten scurries away, you'll lose your hearts!

Track your scratches by applying scratch stickers to your hand!  After 6 rounds, the player with the most Purr Points wins!

Boss Dog is a family-friendly-dog-frenzie where players collect, feed, and steal each other's canine criminals to create the ultimate Boss Dog Family. Dominate your opponents and strategically heist their furry little friends as you battle it out for the top dog title. The game gets more intense with every card you play because the more dogs you recruit, the more there are to steal!

To play, first everyone chooses a Dog Boss. Then each player is dealt four cards and a dog card. Each turn, players pick up one card from the deck and play one card. Cards can be used to place food next to a dog, diffuse another player's dog with a garbage card, discard, or play an action like swiping someone's dog. Each dog has a point value and if you place enough food points to match the point level on your dog, you can collect it to join your family. Then, you grab the next dog from the deck.

Play continues until the end card is drawn and the player with the biggest Boss Dog family wins.

These 3 new additions, part offensive part defensive are here to change things up and elevate your game. Use them early on or late in the battle, it is your call - commander.

The "Extra Forces" kit/addition to the game adds 3 more units to your tank company: 2 anti tank infantry units, and one repair/medic unit.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
#GuiltyFoodTricks is a trick-taking game about posting attractive pictures of the food you're eating on social media - but be careful! If you post photos that become too sensational, it will take a toll on your physical and mental health.

In game play, each suit is a different social media platform, and higher ranked cards will be more likely to win a trick, but will be worth negative points.  Lower valued cards are more difficult to win tricks with, but will be worth positive points.

In addition, players have a special card which will allow them to not-follow suit and play from a face up cache of the undealt cards.

The player with the most points after 3 rounds wins.
publishers: AaAaAaGames (アーアーアーゲームズ)

description:
Madrino 2 is a roll-and-write game where the players work for an architecture firm, attempting to design the house of a mercurial client who only informs the firm of their desires in small drips.

In this expansion for the base game, the players will now work in teams, with one player designing the first floor of a house and the other designing the second floor.  The players have a limited ability to discuss their designs.  The game includes components for elevating the transparent second floor design and visualizing it atop the first floor design.

The team whose plans the firm decides to present to the client wins.
Promo card with stats adding the new player character - Gremlin - to the game. This card was released at Gencon 2022

Kickstarter content from the crowdfunding campaign.  It was packaged in the same box as the retail edition but had a sticker added to the box front.

Extra content:

7 roles

     Dr. Obvious
     Mysterious Military Researcher
     Grumpy Senior Technician
     Press Mesmerist
     Wrangler of Subcontractors
     Melissa, Hunter of Grant Money
     Award Winning Researcher


3 diseases

     Virulent Yellowing
     Virulent Toxicardia
     Virulent Wheezing Tremors


3 alternative shape insight tokens

     matching the insight track iconography


1 full set of monochromatic blocks

     included monochromatic stars (if pledging for Sci Hard expansion)

Kickstarter exclusive expansion that was supplied by the publisher in a separate small box. This expansion adds new type of units - fire artillery (1 unit per nation). Fire artillery combines the power of infantry and ordinary artillery and can be as powerful as a titan of the nation (maximum titan dice result is 4, maximum fire artillery dice result is also 4).

Second expansion for Flick of Faith.

To please the taste of Gods even more another expansion will be released – the Cherry Blossom. Look how the kingdom of flicking Gods expands!

Features:

     6th Player
     Japanese Gods
     Coop Mode
