description:
WHO KNOWS WHO BETTER? - Answer questions about your friends or family. The first to answer 10 questions correctly wins the game. Get ready to unveil new info about your friends.
FUN GAME NIGHT - Hilarious game to get to know your friends even better. Real-life questions leading to interesting stories and amazing facts. Which friend knows you the best?
FAMILY FRIENDLY - The game is also suitable for families. Deepen your connection while having fun. You better not forget your aunt's birthday or your father's first car!
PERFECT GIFT - Bring the game to a pool party, birthday, road trip or camping. Perfect gift for everyone that values their friendships and family bonds.

—description from the publisher

WHO KNOWS WHO BETTER? - Answer questions about your partner. The first to answer 10 questions correctly wins the game. You will learn a lot about each other.
PLAY WITH YOUR PARTNER - Play one-on-one with your partner to deepen your connection and discover new things about each other. The loser has to do the dishes.
PLAY COUPLE VS COUPLE - It's a perfect game for a double or a triple date. The couple who knows each other best wins the game. Suitable for 2 or 3 couples.
FUN GAME NIGHT - This game is not just another deck of conversation cards, but a fun and exciting game to play. Laughter guaranteed.

—description from the publisher

Solitaire Nobunaga: 1570 is a completely solo game depicting the fierce battles fought by Oda Nobunaga from the first year of the Genki era to the Honnoji Incident.
The game system uses the draw-driven system adopted in Issue 31, Bunroku: Campaign in Korea. The system is simple: draw cards from the deck one at a time, and the generals corresponding to the drawn cards either move or attack. The system allows the warlord to move around and attack, but with enough unexpected enemy behaviour to make it hard to believe it is a solo game.

Number of counters:   162
Map:     1 A2 size
Cards:     16 full-colour

Game period:     First year of the Genki era (1570) - Tensho 10 (1582) approx. 
Unit size:     One warlord / 1,000-2,000 people

Playing time:     1 - 2 hours 
Number of players:     1 player (solo play only) 
Difficulty: 2 (5 being the highest)

—description from the publisher (translated)

WHO KNOWS WHO BETTER? - Answer questions about your friends. The first to answer 10 questions correctly wins the game. Get ready to unveil shocking information about your friends.
FUN GAME NIGHT - Hilarious game to get to know your friends even better. Real-life questions for unbelievable stories and amazing facts. Which friend knows you the best?
NOT FOR KIDS - Questions that will reveal everything about you. Themes ranging from love, parties and problems with the law. Not suitable for kids.
PERFECT GIFT - Bring the game to a bachelorette party, birthday, pool party or camping. Perfect gift for adults that are not easily offended.

—description from the publisher

Read the wind (vent) and cross the islands.

Place tiles to change the conditions of islands from one moment to the next, and lead migratory birds to the furthest edges of the world.

This is an abstract game with a puzzle element that allows players to connect islands so that migratory birds can move all at once.

In addition to basic and expert rules for two players, three to four players can play in team games.

vent means wind in French.

Written by LGBTQ+ people, for LGBTQ+ people (and their favorite allies).
Challenge your chosen family and match the funniest cards to win the dirtiest, sassiest, queerest game around.
As LGBTQ+ people, we get to choose our family.
We also get to choose fellow queers to write the funniest cards for the sassiest, dirtiest game in town.

—description from the publisher

This game is in the style of Cards Against Humanity with added Dare cards.

boardgame: Killers: The Card Game – World of Serial Killers

This is a collection of Serial Killers from around the world. From South America to Asia serial killers are an international phenomenon. This expansion adds an additional 70 killers, 20 weapons, 10 new scenarios, and 10 new victims.

boardgame: Total War: ROME – The Board Game: Alexander Expansion
description: The Alexander Expansion for Total War: ROME: The Board Game adds a whole new Eastern map board and allows 1-5 players to take control of new factions. Play as Alexander the Great, or re-write the history books and stop Alexander in his tracks as Darius or Porus, or as the fierce and ambitious horse warriors from the North!
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great

boardgame: 花子さん いますか？(Hanako-san imasuka?)
description:
Toilets at night are full of dangers! Hanako appears in the toilet of the old school building! It is said that anyone who finds out about the rumor will be cursed and will be taken to the spirit world after seven days. In order to break her curse, she has to go to the bathroom of the old school building for seven days and open the door while saying "Is Hanako-san here?" Safe if no one is in the toilet. But what if I really met Hanako-san...?

How the game is played: There is a deck of cursed cards, a deck of events, a deck of days and a row of face-down cards (doors). Each player must open a door (turn over a card), it can be empty, it can be an event (an event card must be drawn) or it can be Hanako-san (a cursed card must be drawn). After seven days, the player with the least curse points will win the game, and the player with the most curse points will be chased by Hanako-san.

Based on the Japanese urban legend.

夜のトイレは危険がいっぱい！
旧校舎のトイレには花子さんが出る！？
その噂を知ってしまった人は呪われ、７日後に霊界へ連れていかれてしまうという。
呪いを解くためには７日間旧校舎のトイレへ通い、「花子さんいますか？」と言いながらドアを開けなければならない。
トイレの中に誰もいなければセーフ。
でももし本当に花子さんと出会ってしまったら……？
This expansion adds new type of units to the battlefield - fallen infantry (12 units). It also introduces new battle mechanics with special fallen unit 5 dice and 5 cards.

boardgame: Lobotomy 2: Manhunt – Villain Ward Character Expansion
description:
In the Villain Ward, insane criminal masterminds are kept to protect society. Brake them loose at your own risk!

What's in the expansion box:
3 Patients (Character) Miniatures

A Monopoly-derivative with no money but with tasks to do if you want to have the toys.

You start on Consent and move your player token clockwise around the board. Unless you get a switch card that tells you to go the other direction. As you travel around the board, you’ll land on kinky toy spaces (like sling, safe word, and chaps). When you get there, if the toy is unowned, you’ll read the card silently to yourself and decide if you want to attempt the challenge. If you don’t want to attempt to earn it, simply say “that’s not my kink,” and end your turn. If someone else owns the toy you land on, though, watch out! You might be in for a spanking, owe them a massage, or have to fetch them a drink. Don’t worry though, you may always opt out of consequences with a handy safe word token. If you abuse the privilege, though, you may be sent to the dungeon, where you’ll have to voyeur your friends having all the fun.

A player wins by collecting the most toys. Each toy card is worth a point, and having all the toys of a color gives a bonus point.

description:
This Expansion was created in collaboration with a highly popular Youtuber - Jazza. In the Jazza Ward, creative minds and artists are being exploited under the cover of personalized care. Use their talents to bring imaginative payback!

What's in the expansion box:
3 Patients (Character) Miniatures
3 Character Cards

boardgame: Spirit Island: Sturzregen durchnässt die Welt
description: This is the same spirit (Downpour Drenches the World) as is included in Spirit Island: Promo Pack 2; in Germany, it was published on its own.

boardgame: Lobotomy 2: Manhunt – Legacy Ward Character Expansion
description:
Legacy Ward includes three characters known from the original Lobotomy. Their models and their cards have been re-made to fit the new environment. The cards are different from those included in the Lobotomy 1 Character Conversion Set Add-On. In the Legacy Ward, patients who have caused trouble in the past are detained against their will. Let history repeat itself!

What's in the expansion box:
3 Patients (Character) Miniatures
3 Character Cards

Players will take on the roll of a Transportation planner for a specific Destination.

The game is played through a variable amount of rounds. As the Transportation planner you will be responsible for managing the travel efficiency and income from the tourist to your Destination and have them visit all the locations to obtain the most Vacation Points (VP) before the end of the game.

Tourist is a 2-4 player card action selection game where you will manage your money and wait to attract the most tourist meeples. If you do, you will emerge victorious! Show your family and friends what it takes to be the best Tourist location!

Players will take turns choosing a card from the 4 face up cards. from there they may play any amount of cards one at a time and take the actions on them.

The game ends when the last Tourist meeple is placed on a players board.

The winner is the player with the highest Vacation Points from their Postcard Point Board!

—description from the designer

Leviathans is a tabletop miniatures game that uses the innovative modular dice system of color-coded dice and easy-to-read physical record-keeping cards that allow for quick and easy play while retaining the depth of strategy that will encourage repeat play.

This expands on the original edition, adding new abilities, ships and factions past what was in the original edition, while advancing the timeline directly into the The Great War era.

Each game centers around moving our pre-painted miniatures across a hex board, trying to out-maneuver your opponent. Players will fire various weapons at each other's ships until either an objective is completed or the enemy falls from the sky. The base game comes with German and French fleets, additional Fleets are planned.

Leviathans prides itself as a game that is easy-to-learn, while having a large amount of tactics and replayability.

—description from the publisher

A group of competing adventurers take on a dinosaur filled jungle in search of the biggest diamonds you’ve ever seen.  Get them fast and get out quick as dinosaurs move across an expanding map every turn.  The map is built as players explore the island so no two games will play the same.  Use your actions wisely and make use of items found throughout the jungle.  Be the first to collect four of the massive gemstones and make it back to base camp for evacuation! Explore the jungle, gather diamonds, avoid dinosaurs, will you be the first one out?

At the start of a players turn, they draw 2 map cards to expand the map and 1 item card as they search the jungle. Players get 3 actions on their turn. Actions may be used to either move or pick up diamonds. Movement requires a player to roll 2 dice which allows them to move to a space with a number equal to or lower the number they rolled. The spaces are cards that are laid out by the players every turn so the map will always be different.

The first player who collects 4 diamonds and is able to get them back to base camp (the central starting card) is the winner.

—description from the publisher

This set includes miniatures of patients and cards of all characters from Lobotomy including add-ons and expansion. This set includes miniatures of patients and cards of all characters from Lobotomy including add-ons and expansion. New character cards make the old miniatures compatible with the new version of the game. The new character cards should only be compatible with Lobotomy 2 and not Lobotomy.

What's in the expansion box:
12 Patients (Character) Miniatures
1 Cat Miniature
Character Cards

5 unit card (3 for Red Army, 2 for German Army)

description: Kickstarter exclusive expansion that contains 15 Secret Mission Cards and some tokens. Each Nation gets 3 Objective Cards (emissaries) with their flag, besides the regular Secret Objective cards. Players will try to accomplish them for additional victory points, as well as race to get the special token that will stay with their army on the board. The number of tokens is limited, so the faster you complete 1 of your Nation Objective Cards, the wider choice you have.

boardgame: What on Earth Are You Talking About?
description:
You are an alien.

You and your crew have travelled far and wide in search of intelligent life, and you’ve finally found it! Sort of. You’ve observed these curious creatures on their little rock hurtling through the deep void of space, and now you think you have seen enough. You abduct a couple of humans and are ready to make first contact.

In What On Earth Are You Talking About?, two teams race to translate uniquely human concepts into something even an alien can understand.

Compete against your friends and the intergalactic language barrier as your crewmates rush to figure out what on earth you are talking about!

Each round takes between 15 to 45 minutes (depending on how many players you're playing with), and is recommended for both humans and aliens aged 17 and up.

---

'What On Earth Are You Talking About?' is split into two card types:

Earth Cards: Each featuring a peculiar human behaviour or concept. E.g. 'Climate Change', 'Doing The Worm', 'Circumcision' etc.
Alien Cards: Each populated with a string of letters that will make you think and talk like an alien. E.g. O C Q Y S R T Y N

So, with the above letters, a Human might say:

"OCEAN COMING QUICKLY! YOU SHOULD RELISH THESE YEARS. NO BEARS WORLD WIDE"

In this example, the correct guess would be 'Climate Change'.

Each correct guess earns your team 1 point, and the team who completes an orbit of Earth first... wins!
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Kickstarter exclusive expansion that contains 25 cards (5 for each nation). That crossover expansion works for Titans and for previous game Valhalla. 
Each Titan gets 5 cards with special powers. After each battle, that includes the Titan, player can add one card face-up to his play area – it means that the Titan will slowly grow in Power, unlocking new abilities. Player may use 1 card in each Battle, but each of them have a requirement to be activated – the more powers Titan has, the choice for player in battle is wider. Some Powers are easier to use, other more difficult, and it’s up to each player, how they will use the cards.

Kickstarter exclusive expansion that contains a new deck of Support Cards and 6 special dice. Players may decide to use that deck of Support Cards instead of the standard one or combine them. The new cards work similarly to the standard ones, since players may combine them to create combos in battles, but they need to meet the requirements to do so. Cards give different support in battle, bringing the special dice included in the expansion. These dice will add more strength or activate the special actions on the cards that player used. There are 6 different special actions on those cards, so it allows for more tactical maneuvering and excitement in battles.

The free Dire Wolf Game Room brings together award-winning apps and board games in a central hub that helps you find games, connect with friends, and expand your collection.

Inside the Game Room, you'll find:


     Game Lobby – Find a game on the fly in the cross-title game lobby!



     Friends List – Take your friends with you wherever you go. Who's up for a game?



     Chat – Get to know your fellow players and cut deals at the table with Global and In-Game chat!



     News – Catch all of the latest across titles in one central bulletin board!


The Game Room library currently includes support for:

Digital Board Games

- Raiders of the North Sea

It's Raiding Season! Plunder your way to Viking adventure in the digital adaptation of Renegade Game Studios' award-winning worker-placement board game!

- Root

Might makes right in the wild wood! Leder Games' award-winning board game of asymmetrical woodland warfare comes to cross-platform digital play! Combine subtle strategy and bold daring to guide your forest faction to victory!

- Sagrada

Bring your stained-glass masterpiece to life in Floodgate Games' strategy game of dice drafting and window crafting!

- Yellow & Yangtze

Lead your dynasty to victory in the digital adaptation of Reiner Knizia's acclaimed board game!

Tabletop Board Games

- Dune: Imperium

Control the Spice & control the Universe in Dire Wolf's hit boardgame Dune: Imperium. Deck-building and worker placement combine in a deeply thematic new strategy game where the fate of the Empire hangs on your decisions.

- Clank! A Deck-Building Adventure

Clank! mode provides a single-player dungeon-delve for your favorite Deck-Building Adventure, and introduces exciting mini-Quests to add surprises to multiplayer games.

- Clank! In! Space!

Clank! In! Space! mode provides a single-player campaign to thwart Lord Eradikus. Play through the multi-game encounter and save the galaxy!

Join the Dire Wolf Game Room community! It's always game night somewhere!

boardgame: Mageling: Rise of the Ancient Ones – Apotheosis
description:
Apotheosis - noun - the highest point in the development of something; culmination or climax.

Mageling: Apotheosis enables players to customize their strategies by evolving their scrolls. Like the enchantments of the Rise of the Ancient Ones expansion, evolved scrolls are not meant to change the game radically. Instead, they are meant to add new possibilities to the gameplay and obscure strategies to discover.

By visiting the new locations in Apotheosis, players can collect a new resource, Time Shards. Time Shards are used during the refresh phase to trade in scrolls in a player's grimoire for more powerful versions.

boardgame: Total War: ROME – The Board Game: Faction Booster Expansion
description: The Faction Booster Expansion for Total War: ROME: The Board Game adds 8 extra playable factions: Numidia, Hispania, Germanic Tribes, Thracia, Dacia, Egypt, Gaul, and Scythia - hugely enhancing your enjoyment and re-playability. Can you lead Egypt into a dominant Mediterranean maritime power? Or unite the Germanic tribes and sack Rome, centuries before the Goths and Huns? Can you change the course of history?

boardgame: Total War: ROME – The Board Game: Emperor Expansion
description: In the Emperor Expansion for Total War: ROME: The Board Game, take control of one of the great Roman families – Julii, Brutii or Scipii. Build your family’s interests into a mighty empire and take control of Rome from the Senate, and proclaim yourself Emperor to win the game. Keep a close eye on your rivals whilst keeping the Senate happy, at least until you are strong enough to make your move on Rome and the Senate!

boardgame: Total War: ROME – The Board Game: Battle Play Expansion
description: The Battle Play Expansion for Total War: ROME: The Board Game adds a deeper, more tactical battle experience - the equivalent of hitting "Play Battle" in the video game. Using the same army card decks from the core game, fight a battle where you deploy your army in formations and fight 4 phases over 10-15 minutes. Use this expansion in conjunction with the core game or fight standalone battles and tournaments. You can even refight famous battles from history with Battle Play!

Boba Master is a card game of memory and luck. Use tools and actions to create your favourite cup. With plenty of spills and mistakes along the way, such specialTEA cards will keep your opponents at bay. Assemble your favourite bobas with syrup and tea. Pick the right cards to finish your drinks quickly. Things may change with a single swap, so carefully race your friends and family to the top. Sip some tea and keep focus or lose the title without your notICE.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fight the Blight: Dark Past vs Doomed Futures
description:
The Blight has taken many forms throughout history. Can you crawl out from the ashes of the Dark Past, rise up and prevent the Doomed Future?

This expansion for Fight the Blight adds 2 new settings that can be played separately or together, 9 new Characters (including your favorite content creators), 82 new cards, new strategies, and endless replayability.

When the past, present, and future collide… who will be left standing!?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cold Waves: The Atlantic & North Sea 1739-1748
description:
Cold Waves is the final game of a set covering the War of the Austrian Succession at sea (plus the concurrent War of Jenkin's Ear). Though relatively unknown, these conflicts, raging from 1739-1748, comprised the first "world war". The primary naval actions took place in three theatres: the English Channel and Bay of Biscay, the Caribbean, and the western Mediterranean. Forces also clashed in India, along America's eastern seaboard, and in the Pacific. Cold Waves examines the European Atlantic coasts, the Channel, and the North Sea, where tedious blockade duty and convoy hunting was laced with the ever present fear of amphibious invasion – something both sides tried more than once.

Cold Waves is a two player strategy game. You command either the British or Bourbon (French and Spanish) fleets. Orders are handed down to you from your Admiralty, and you must try to fulfill them within the framework of the wider war, which is represented by a set of events. Your opponent will have orders of his own to fulfill, but with his spare forces he will be attempting to foil your plans. Success will earn you Prestige (victory points). You must decide when and where to commit resources, where to feint and where to strike, organising your 'sail' (ships) into squadrons, and your squadrons into flotillas commanded by leaders (commodores and admirals). Maintaining sufficient forces at battle readiness is critical, but very difficult, since ships constantly suffer wear and tear – a battle may be won and the campaign lost. Scenarios are one year (16 turns) long, playable in 3-4 hours (the number of counters actually in play on the map is quite small). The campaign game consists of a number of years pre-chosen by the players.

Every ship that appeared in the theatre is accounted for, as is every leader of note. (Although by the basic rules ships are generic 'SPs', players can use the OOBs to add detail and even fight battles using their favourite tactical system.) Squadrons are rated for movement and the number of sail they can contain. Specially named squadrons represent the great ships, like the 112-gun Real Felipe, which successfully beat off a simultaneous attack by three British ships of the line. Other counters represent special assets, like fireships, frigates, and galleys. These can be 'played' (like cards) to conduct special tasks such as search and shore bombardment. Leaders are ranked, and rated for skill and personality.

—description from the publisher
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

Klassequizen is a trivia game related to the Norwegian television show of the same name.

One player reads a question aloud, and all players answer simultaneously using chips. The card is flipped and answers are revealed.

The twist of the game is that multiple alternatives might be correct, and a player scores points for each correct answer. However, if any wrong alternative is chosen, the player is awarded no points.

boardgame: Элдмонт: В поисках золота (Elmont: In search of gold)
description:
Conquer Mount Eldmont and return the gold to the dwarves! For two thousand years Mount Eldmont remained impregnable… In its caves were stored tens of thousands of gold crystals, once stolen by an insidious Dragon. Gold rightfully belongs to the dwarf clans, and today they are ready to restore justice. Are you ready to join the battle for Eldmont? Then go ahead! Be the first in the battle for the treasures of Eldmont!

Your task is to collect as many gold crystals as possible in your chests. You have to play only for yourself, try to outwit your rivals — after all, it is your clan that should own the gold!

It's very simple to do this — lay out action cards and expose your dwarves. A certain combination of moves will give you gold crystals. Keep them in your chests so that your rivals don't guess how close you are to victory.

But don't forget, there is an insidious Dragon hiding in the depths of the caves. He has not yet noticed the arrival of guests in his domain and is sleeping sweetly on the golden mountain. But one wrong move, and you will fall into his clutches! Enlist the support of a Wizard, control the elements and beware of the monster.

The one who collects the most crystals is recognized as the winner of this game!

boardgame: Iron Storm: The First World War, 1914-1918
description:
Iron Storm is a two-player strategic wargame that allows you to recreate the entire First World War in a single afternoon. The game uses an innovative card engine system where each player uses their entire hand in one go, to activate armies, resolve operations, and support battles with combat cards. It also simulates human and economic attrition during the course of the war by gradually adding cards to each deck that will have limited use in the hand and take additional effort to remove.

—description from the publisher

description:
"Get to know the history of Savonlinna and its environs through playing"

In this Afrikan Tähti-like Finnish game centered on the eastern Finland city of Savonlinna, players travel around the town both on land and on lake Saimaa, flipping tokens that can either help or hinder them on the way, in search of the one special Note Key token; whenever a player finds this, a race back to the town begins, and the one to get there first is the winner. Main differences to Afrikan Tähti besides the map are that some of the tokens have longer-lasting and more powerful effects, the lack of flight routes, and that there is no "Horseshoe" piece that would be required to win at the end instead of the "Star", it is a simple race.

Modern history of Savonlinna begins in 1475 when the Olavinlinna castle was built on a small island in the strategic Kyrönsalmi strait on lake Saimaa, guarding what was then the eastern border of the Swedish empire. The settlement that sprung up nearby got town rights in 1639. The Note Key is an obvious reference to what Savonlinna is internationally most famous for; the Savonlinna Opera Festival, held annually each July. The back of the rules sheet gives a short description of the major attractions and landmarks in and around the city.

boardgame: Zoo King: Jaguar & Bongo Promo cards
description:
Two additional cards to add to your Animal market deck to increase variety in your games: 
the Jaguar and the Bongo.

boardgame: Power Rangers: Deck-Building Game –  Phantom Ranger & Master Vile
description:
Promo cards given away at GenCon 2022 events.

Phantom Ranger and Master Vile are a 4-cost Hero and 5-cost Villain card, both for the main deck.

Phantom Ranger can attack to deal damage, or heal if negated.  Master Vile gives your opponents stuns.
A promo pack for Transformers Deck-Building Game containing 2 new characters (Snarl & Razorclaw), and two new level 3 bosses (Scorponok & Fortress Maximus).

Gates is the long-awaited expansion to the critically-acclaimed Gate. You must have a copy of Gate in order to use this expansion.

Gates brings a lot of new content to the table including new citizens to hire, enemies to battle, heroes to help protect your gate, new locations to protect, barricades to build, and ZOGAR!!!

Gate is a solo play only tower-defense game in which you take on the role of the leader of a small medieval town that is under siege from a swarm of vile creatures from the local forest. You will have to manage the health of your town's gate, the tower, and the local farm. In addition this attack is causing fear levels to rise. If you cannot keep your gate standing or if your people's fear gets too high, you will lose the game. If however you can hold off the swarm of enemies long enough, you will be victorious!

Gate uses deck-building as the driving mechanic in the game. You will start with three starting cards, but as the game progresses you will be able to recruit new people from your town to help your cause. Each character has unique abilities and some even have special powers. The enemies in the game get increasingly more difficult with each new wave, so you will have to make good choices as to when to spend resources on fighting off your foes, upgrading your command, and repairing your town.

Not only during the holiday season Pictures X-mas expands the fun with 2 new materials:

Recognize a photo by simply feeling some items! Try to arrange a Christmas table setting, so that the other players can find out your photo!

With its 110 new photos and many natural components, Pictures X-mas is the perfect expansion for Pictures.

The ultimate transport game putting you behind the wheel.

Like many drivers in real life your Linehauler career starts with a Rigid Truck. You'll draw Manifest Cards as you play, which show your destination, cargo and the amount you’ll make. Sometimes special instructions will apply and you will need a Pilot or an Escort, you might also draw the Wide Load or Dangerous Goods Loads, which will require a permit. The Pay Day and Fifth Wheel Cards will also test your luck with bonuses and penalties.

This game is not just pick-up and drop-off, along the journey you’ll get to own the services that support the road transport industry. These income-producing properties include Fuel, Mechanical, Pilot Services, Seaports and even Airports.This game is not without challenges, with stoppages and detours, fire, floods and restrictive tunnels and bridges occupying the board and sometimes forcing heavily-laden drivers around alternate routes.

Race your competitors around Australia to become the first person to own a Road Train and return to the nearest Capital City, and be crowned Linehauler Champion!

Aura Hunters

Welcome to Lunaria. Young orphans become friends and bear the mark of the Aura Hunters.

Updates will follow...

10 new characters and 4 new bankdecks for Puzzle Strike 2 that you can mix and match with the base set.

Go on these Bold Adventures: Haunted House, Dino Size, Steam Crank, and Puzzle Pets.

boardgame: Lobotomy 2: Manhunt – Lobotomy 1 Monster Conversion Set
description:
The cards were remade, adapted to Lobotomy 2 mechanics and contain graphics in the old Lobotomy style. The set includes rules and cards that will allow you to use the monsters in 19 additional Daily Nightmare scenarios. The set includes all monster cards from Lobotomy 1, including the add-ons.

IMPORTANT: Miniatures not included.

boardgame: Mystery Games: Der Ausbruch aus Arazar
description:
In the storytelling and adventure game Mystery Games: Der Ausbruch aus Arazar, players try to break out of prison. How the story develops is entirely in your hands and your decisions! Will the escape succeed?

The players try to escape from prison together. You follow the trail of the escape legend Jack, who has systematically searched the prison for the weakest point with a plan. Any parts of the plan that proved useless he left behind in prison. Can you figure out which piece is missing, then implement Jack's last hints and freedom calls?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hot Punk Machine: Tormenta de Metal
description:
Trading card game with a sci-fi/dark fantasy theme. You build a 40 card deck, you can chose any card from the available cardpool to build your deck.

Using your intelligence and your creativity, you will duel with you opponent using the deck that you built, with your cards you have to reduce your opponent's life points to 0 or make him run out of cards to win the game.

The deluxe package includes every season from the AFL, every National season from 1929 to the 2014-15 season, and every season from the AAFC, WFL, USFL, XFL and UFL. Everything needed to play is included.  There are NO game parts to buy with this set.

As the name implies, this quick play game provides you with a final score for an Pro Football game. Allowing you to rapidly play games and watch standings unfold before your eyes. However, there is more to the game than just generating scores. There is plenty of excitement and drama along the way, keeping the game play exciting and compelling. You will have late in the game touchdowns, field goals and fantastic defensive stands.

The Final Score Football line of games differs from our other quick play line, Ultra Quick, as the games all unfold in a quarter-by-quarter manner.

Each team is rated individually for Offense, Defense, and Home Field Advantage. And while there will surely be some surprises along the way, since this is a game, after all, the accuracy of the game will astound you. We also have a normalization system for every team and league we rate, so you can play across seasons to determine the best team of all-time.

Ark Nova: Marine Worlds, an expansion for Ark Nova, introduces multiple new elements to the game, such as sea animals that each have to be played in new special enclosures that must be built adjacent to water.

Roughly half the sea animals are reef dwellers, and whenever you add a reef dweller to your zoo, you trigger the ability of all reef dwellers in your zoo. To deal with the dilution of the deck, naturally caused by adding more cards, all sea cards feature a wave icon, and whenever it is revealed in the display, you discard the first card in the row, then replenish.

A new fourth university is available on the association board, and if you take it, you claim one of six special universities from the reserve that feature one research icon and one of six animal icons. When you take this registry, you reveal cards from the top of the deck and keep the first revealed card with an animal icon that matches your chosen university.

For each of the five action cards, four alternate versions with a little twist will be available. Players draft action cards at the start of play, replacing two of their standard action cards with these new ones, increasing the asymmetry in the game.

New bonus tiles and final scoring cards will also be included.

Also the expansion will include alternate wooden markers for the 3 tracks and cute animals to mark the conservation projects you support instead of using your regular player markers.

description:
Sick of listening to your friends talk about work?

Save your evening with Una Scurtă - 600 cards (until you lend it to someone), exactly like in the show Una Scurtă, but home. And without comedians, but with pizza.

It plays with 3 to 10 players, but seriously, if you had 10 friends, you wouldn't have bought a card game.

Players take turns being judge and playing a black card (which has the premises for the joke) and all other players play a red card simultanously as a punch-line. The judge decides which red card is best and the black card is passed to the winner as a victory point. Play continues clockwise and after everyone has been judge, you play an unseen round. Each player plays a red card without a black one being revealed before. The black card is revealed after and players then vote which card was the funniest one. Play continues in this way for the agreed duration, the player with the most black cards is the winner.

Coupling, the Game is The ultimate party game in which you will discover more about your friends in 45 minutes than you did in your whole life, AND have a blast at the same time! It's a game for stronger friendships, deep conversations and a fun evening, with a healthy amount of competition, lots of laughter, a pinch of tickling and, for the most playful, a bit of singing and dancing.

The basic version of the game contains 10 mini-games on 120 COUPLING cards, which, combined with unique mechanics, make a highly dynamic game. More than 500 combinations of questions and challenges break even the toughest ice and ensure that the game is still fun even after you play it with the same group of friends for the tenth time.

You play the game in three stages:

     Like in life, every player starts out as single. Then, through random questions and fun personal stories, you couple up with two players.
     These two players now compete for your affection in the second stage through exciting challenges: charades with sounds, mouth reading, recipes and geometric shapes and lines. 
     In the third stage, it’s Couples Showdown, where each player competes along with their best partner for a glorious victory against other couples through new mini games: multiple choice cards to challenge your beliefs, personal questions that you may have always wanted to talk about, but didn't dare ask, psychological pictures to see what’s beyond obvious, and drawing things and associations to release your inner Picasso. 


First couple to get through the FINISH LINE wins and gets to sit in the glorious winning spot!

Two players can use the cards to get to know each other better. Additional blank cards can be purchased to expand the game to 12 players.

On the table, you scatter some round tokens with different animal/color/pattern. Three dice are thrown to define the animal/color/pattern to search for. Everyone searches for it and the first that founds it keeps the token. The one who gets the most tokens, wins the game.

This is the Lappi version of the CityBusiness game series.

Unlike most games in the series, this one is not centered on a single city/town but rather a wider geographic area - in this case, the whole of Finnish Lapland.

CityBusiness is a series of games, each of which is basically a light version of Monopoly localized to a city/region of Finland. The games promote local companies, councils, educational institutes etc.

The main differences between these games and Monopoly are:


     Instead of a "street" each square on the board represents a local business.
     There are no hotels or houses, but each square has two title deeds instead of just one, allowing two players to jointly own any location on the board.
     The majority of the event cards are trivia questions about the locality the game is set in.
     While the effects of the first two corner squares (Go and Jail in Monopoly) remain the same for every edition (Go and Stock Market Crash), the effect of the other two corners varies from edition to edition and is the only real difference between them (except for the trivia content, of course). They are typically also local businesses but without title deeds and thus no rent either, but some special effect instead, such as pass a turn or get an extra throw.

This is the Oulu version of the CityBusiness game series. Oulu, up in the northern Bay of Bothnia, was formerly known mainly as a seaport especially for the tar trade, but is now the 5th largest city in Finland and the hub of the 4th largest urban area, known for it's university and the vigorous industrial and business growth of the area, especially in new technologies.

This is the Turku version of the CityBusiness game series. The southwestern city of Turku is the oldest city in Finland (founded 1229), was the capital before Helsinki until 1812, and still is the 6th largest city and the hub of the 3rd largest metropolitan area in the country, the capital of it's region, and an important seaport with shipyards.

Who will be the best fisher in Pupapupa Island?

In Push Fish, you will be a fisher who come to this island to find the legendary fish with your own stories.
Take your fishing rod, and throw the baits(literally throw, this is a dexterity game!) to successfully put on your bait on the fish you want. Then, you should flick the token on your rod (of course, by your finger!) to decide you successfully hook up the fish!

Try to catch specific fish that match your mission cards, which will bring you the victory points. 6 unique characters and various item cards bring a game fun more.

When someone gathers 7 fish cards, the game enda and the player who has highest score wins. Or, when a player fish up the golden fish or Kraken wins immediately!

description:
“The Lorn Keep was once a magnificent castle that guarded the entrance to a wealthy valley kingdom. The masters of the keep grew rich collecting taxes from the merchants seeking safe passage into the valley. With time, the kingdom fell and the keep along with it. Now the ruins are a popular destination for adventurers and treasure hunters, many of whom are never seen from again.” - Jaden the Elder

Ruins of the Lorn Keep is an easy-to-learn, quick-to-play dungeon crawl. Play a warrior, a thief or a wizard searching for treasure, powerful magical items, and fame. Each character excels at one of the three different dungeon challenge types. Part dungeon crawl, part memory game, Ruins of the Lorn Keep is highly portable and can be played nearly anywhere.

During a player's turn, a card is turned face-up and dice rolled to try and beat a monster or win a treasure. Each card is worth victory points. The first player to 10 points wins.

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Heavy Firepower
description:
Destro and Scrap Iron have arrived to help Cobra bring the world to its knees! Thankfully, Roadblock, Bombstrike, and Gung Ho have arrived to reinforce the G.I. JOE team against this explosive threat. This expansion introduces three new characters to play and new enemies to take down!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Red Skies: P-38J Lightning Squadron
description:
This is an expansion set for the WWII aircraft miniatures game Blood Red Skies by Warlord Games and designed by Andy Chambers. This expansion includes a squadron of USA P-38J Lightning fighters and additional accessories to add to the core game.

The twin-engined fighter enjoyed its most extensive deployment and successes in the Pacific theatre. Though it could not outmanoeuvre the A6M Zero at sub-200mph speeds, its speed and rate of climb lent it the capability of making several high speed passes in combat, and Japanese aircraft armour was more susceptible than German aircraft to the tight groupings of the P-38s guns.

It was P-38s that engaged in the fighter-intercept mission that claimed the life of Admiral Isoroku Yamamoto, architect of the attack on Pearl Harbor. In this engagement, 16 P-38G lightnings met two G4M “Betty” transports and six escorting Zeros. Both transports were lost as well as two Zeros, for the loss of one P-38.
family: Game: Blood Red Skies – United States Army Air Forces

boardgame: Blood Red Skies: US Ace Pilot – Richard Bong
description:
Richard Ira Bong was the United States’ top scoring and most highly decorated Ace of the Second World War.

He joined the Army Air Corps Aviation Cadet Program in May 1941 and a year later was training on the new Lockheed P-38 Lightning twin engine fighter. When war broke out, Bong was sent to New Guinea and won a reputation for aggressively battling enemy aircraft at any opportunity, making Ace with five aerial victories in his first four engagements. By October 1944, Bong had a total of 30 victories and received the Medal of Honor and a ban on more combat flying. Despite orders, Bong pushed his score to 40, plus seven probables, before he was sent home in January 1945. Tragically, Bong lost his life test piloting the new generation of jets later that year.

boardgame: Doctor Who: Exterminate! The Miniatures Game – The Pting: Soldier of Fortune
description: Blessed with a toxic skin, vicious claws and an all-but-insatiable hunger for all manner of inorganic matter, the diminutive Pting can be a menace to interstellar travellers as the Thirteenth Doctor found during The Tsuranga Conundrum. Pting are believed to have been impossible to kill, and could even survive in the vacuum of space. They could even eat a bomb and barely be affected when it detonated other than temporarily sating its rapacious hunger.

Devoured (Fallen Colossus) is a dark solo narrative-driven tabletop adventure game, inspired by retro horror, 80s role-playing games and ‘choose your own adventure’ books. In Devoured you will take on the role of a lost traveler, making your way through a strange land in the hope of discovering both your identity and the role you have taken in the desolation of the land around you.

Your quest will be extremely tough. The environment is bleak and hostile. You will have to battle brutal enemies, avoid horrific traps, manage limited resources and sometimes rely on fate to get you through this overwhelming task. Only at the end will you discover the truth about yourself.

Devoured has over 100 narrative cards spread over 6 levels, 37 unique enemies to battle, 20 unique weapons, 6 possible endings and a unique health management mechanism that will require clever tactical thinking from you in order to stay alive and complete your quest. Devoured also has extra unique play modes that will keep the game fresh and exciting each time you bring it to the table.

Are you ready to be Devoured?

Accurate and easy to play, Glory days College Football brings the big time Saturday afternoon excitement to your table top.  GDCF employs a unique system, Trench Control, to help determine how well each team will perform on any given play.  Just like real life, penalties, turnovers and big plays can add an element of excitement to your game.  Choose from all-time great teams or from season sets.

There are three ways to play the game.  You can play the game entirely in Play-By-Play mode which takes about 90 minutes.  You can play Simulated Drive mode where you simulate the gist of every drive for an abbreviated version which lasts about 30 minutes.  Or you can play a hybrid mode where you can speed through the first half of each drive and grind it in from there.

This game is available in pdf format at this time.  You get the rulebook, charts, and 30 all time great college football teams from which to choose.  All you will need is the ability to print the materials, two d10, 2d6 and a d20.  More team sets will become available in the future so be on the lookout for that.

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Wolverine Hero Pack
description:
Gifted with a powerful healing factor and armed with adamantium claws that can cut through anything, the hero known as Wolverine is a force to be reckoned with. Though trained as a killer by Weapon X, Wolverine has dedicated his life to helping others as one of the X-Men, and now he tears his way into players’ games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game!

Wolverine is a combat-focused hero that can pay for attacks with his hit points and unleash a relentless assault upon the villains’ forces. With this Hero Pack, players will find Wolverine, his sixteen signature cards, and a full assortment of Aggression cards inviting them to tear through their enemies. This pack also includes a bonus modular encounter set featuring the dangerous Lady Deathstrike!

For players eager to challenge Magneto and his Brotherhood of Mutants, they can get started with the Wolverine Hero Pack! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving players the chance to start playing right out of the box.

description:
The Haunted House: Magic Mirror is an innovative puzzle game that uses reflected images in the mirror as the main mechanism. It is the Haunted House version of Mirror Garden.

Some changes have been made from the original game.

(1)    The level of difficulty has been adjusted, so younger players may also play the game.
(2)    Each player gets 3 semicircular ghost boards instead of 2 semicircular garden boards from the original game. Now, there are more options.
(3)    Players just have to figure out the order of the ghosts in a line rather than to figure out the whole formation of the flower beds in multiple layers.

How to Play:
1. Each player takes one foldable mirror and three ghost boards.
2. Open a puzzle card and players simultaneously try to create the same image on the puzzle card with the mirror and the boards.
3. They can do this by laying 1 to 3 ghost boards on the table (may overlap one another) and then place the mirror along the lines separating each sector. 
4. Once it is done, the player calls out the code and checks the answer sheet, if it were correct the player gains a point. If it were wrong, then the other player gets the point.

The Haunted House: Confusing Circle is a speed matching game that helps children to practice addition. 
“Collect ghosts with Shinbi’s powerful magic circle and magic dice before they disturb the peace of Shinbi Apartment!”

How to Play:
(1) Set the board and place ten ghost cards on each side of the decagonal board.
(2) A player rolls two dice. Then, all players simultaneously look for the ghost cards correspond to both dice.

    One die tells the number of ghosts and the other one tells the number of wisps that should be on it. 


(3) After spotting the cards, add up all the number on the board where they are attached. Shout out the total!
(4) If the math is right, the player takes all those cards. Each card gives 1 point.

The Haunted House: Raon is a word game using Korean alphabet, Hangul, tiles.

How to Play:
(1) Make a face-down deck with a boss card and ten ghost cards.
(2) Place the Hangul tiles face-up in the center of the table.
(3) Open the top card from the deck.
(Each card tells you what kind of words to make)
ex: a word start with ‘¬’ or ‘ ’, a word related to ‘plants’
(4) Use the tiles in the center to make words under a given condition.
(5) Players get points depend on the order they finish.

The biggest difference from the original Raon game is that there are now ghost cards to defeat!
The ghosts and the bosses can be defeated by making specific types of words written on each card.

boardgame: The Witcher: Old World – Kickstarter Expansion
description:
Dive deeper into the fantastic and dangerous world of The Witcher! These expansions will allow you to learn more about the nature of the terrifying beasts, join forces to fight the Wild Hunt, and experience even more amazing adventures!

This is the Kickstarter Expansion for The Witcher: Old World, and it includes Wild Hunt Expansion, Monster Trail Expansion, Ciri Expansion, Lost Mount Expansion, and Adventure Pack Expansion.

description:
A Most Dangerous Rescue is a race between you and the Yovuz Ustalar Gang as you attempt to rescue precious artifacts before the Yovuz Ustalar can use them for nefarious purposes. As you race across the globe searching for each artifact, you fight against members of the Yovuz Ustalar. Your travels take you to the ends of the earth and to the Boss of the Yovuz Ustalar. The world won’t be safe until you defeat the Boss or ensure that all the artifacts are out of the Yovuz Ustalar’s reach.

This is a solitaire card chase game, in which you will go from site to site to fight the 4 evil gang members one by one, trying to defeat them before they gain 4 powerful artifacts. Each gang member has its own strategy, so you should plan your game accordingly to their appearance order.

The game evolves around "scenes", when each turn is represented by a unique scene card. Each scene offers 4 different strategies to pick from. Strategies includes actions like "attack and research", "attack and invest", "research and fortify" etc. The evil gang member always choose his strategy first, according to his own preference and his resources, and then the player gets to retaliate.

The player needs to destroy all gang members, one by one, before time runs out. The player loses if he fails to defeat the boss before running out of time.

Occasionally, a powerful artifact will be found in a scene and the players should secure it before the Evil Yovuz Ustalar will be able to collect it.

Part of the  2022 pnp solitaire contest.

This is a cut-out-and-play game with a food/cooking theme.

The setting is a kitchen contest. Players take turns to roll dice to build up ingredient tokens, then get a chance to make recipes from a random selection of cards.

Recipes score points; first to the target score wins.

description:
Tennis World is a quick-playing tennis simulation using actual player rankings to set up matches. Then, dice and charts determine who wins the pressure points each game but don't rule out changes to your form or an injury altering the balance of the match.

The game can be played in men's and women's singles/ doubles / mixed doubles formats and each set plays out in about 5 minutes.

Augmentations and artificial intelligence, synthetic twins and temporal relocation. This world is challenging you. Your goal is to catch criminals as well as to prevent crimes. Why  accept the reality as is if you can edit it? Collect evidence, hack gadgets, buy super arms and implants. Overpower passion-filled Redport, feel megalopolis wide pulse and gain the respect of city-ruling forces!

A fully cooperative role-playing adventure. Several investigations connected by an epic plot and a legacy system. Unique features include replayable investigations and crime prevention. 1-4 players choose heroes and dive into a cyberpunk world to investigate crimes then travel to the past and prevent them.

Resolvers: Time to Shift is a cooperative game. During their turn, a player selects a desired action for their character from the list provided by the app and follows the instructions on the screen, such as moving player/npc pieces, opening new location tiles, performing skill checks, adding equipment cards e.t.c. Each player action takes time, which is tracked by the app and used to select an active player. The app also keeps all the information gathered by the players during their investigations. When players think they have accumulated enough knowledge, they can jump back in time to prevent a crime. Successfully preventing a crime opens the next campaign mission.

Replayability is delivered by an app generating detective stories. The app explains rules, tracks time and saves players progress.

Battle to be the squirrel with the most nutz stashed for winter. But don't drop your nutz!

Each player takes turns rolling the die and drawing cards into their hand. Cards have values for how many nutz they are worth. Try to get as many nutz from your hand into your stash as you can. Or use them for special advantages such as spying, stealing, or making others drop their nutz.

The player with the most nutz wins.

Our Wizards have lost their jobs. So they decided to get into the human waste removal business. Teleport poop into your competitors portal potties to put them out of business.

Players draw cards and follow the instructions on them to try to eliminate the other players by filling up their games boards with poop tokens.

The last player with an open game board wins.

boardgame: Deep Rock Galactic: The Board Game –  Goo From Above Miniature Expansion
description:
Ew, Yuck! Gooey!

Deep in the dark caves of Hoxxes IV, two formidable foes await - the Glyphid Manace and the Goo Bomber.

The Glyphid Menace burrows in cracks and crevasses, hiding from view, only emerging to rapidly fire blistering volleys of corrosive goo at mining Dwarves. You should handle it with extreme caution.

The Goo Bomber is basically a bloated grotesque sack of flying slime. Its sole purpose is to entrap foul-smelling Dwarves in sticky goo. But fear not, they can be blasted down. Just aim for the exposed slime sacks on their sides, but make sure you're not standing underneath them when you do.

—description from the publisher

Miniature expansion - "Goo From Above" includes 3 goo bombers, 2 glyphid menaces, 4 new cards, rule sheet, and 6 goo plastic tokens to incorporate into the base game.

The card game combining announcements, trumps and tricks, to play as a team or independently.

Find the techniques of Belote, Tarot and other great classics in this L'escroc  game with an additional feature: the powerful joker L'escroc  who can intervene in the game at any time and modify the final outcome!

Rules for several different variations with different win conditions included.

In Flamingo, players seek to create the best flamingo habitat in their marshland to draw the most accolades from the Audubon Society and The Flamingo Specialist Group.

Over a variable number of rounds, players choose 1-3 actions based on the dice drawn. Dice are used to gain a flamingo, gain an egg, and improve skills in nesting, feeding, and flocking. Players have 35 actions for the whole game. In order to win, they must most carefully use their dice to achieve their goals in those five areas.

While the dice are random, players can control their use and develop a better plan each turn. Further, if two dice of the same color are drawn from the bag, they may be treated as any color.

In skills, players must use die values to make equations true, using skills in logic to aid teaching if game schoolers are interested. Additionally, there is a further math component of players using multipliers with various areas to score points for other areas.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Master of War: Trading Card Game
description:
Master of War is a trading card game where your goal is to defend your Master of War and defeat the opponent one's by reducing their War point to 0 using your Soldier and War Weapon cards.

| WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THE GAME |
• 100 cards, enough to build two different decks and play with a friend
• The complete rule book

| HOW TO PLAY |
You need two players to play this game. Each player also needs a Master of War card, a Divine card, and a deck of 40 to 50 cards (compose of Soldier and War Weapon cards). You can have the same Soldier or War Weapon cards three times in a deck.
Players need to choose who goes first before starting the game
(ex; roll a dice) and then shuffle their deck.
When this is done, each player must place their Master of War (face up) and Divine card (face down) on the terrain.

| PHASES EXPLANATION |
- PHASE 1: Each player draws five cards at the start of a game. At your next turn, you draw one card unless you already got
  five in your hand.
- PHASE 2: You then place your Soldier and War Weapon cards on the terrain.
- PHASE 3: In this phase, you can attack with Soldier, use War Weapon or use your Master of War or Divine card effect.
   You cannot do any of this in the very first turn of the game.
- PHASE 4: End your turn. It’s now the opponent’s turn and after that, you restart at phase 1.

| CARD EXPLANATION |

MASTER OF WAR

This card represents the total amount of War Point you have for the game. Each Master of war is different with a unique stat and effect. This card is always placed face up before starting the game.
Functions in general
This card cannot attack other Soldier cards unless you have no more Soldier left. This card can attack an opponent Master of War directly if the following conditions are met: no Soldier in the enemy field and you have no Soldier card left. This card can use its unique effect during your phase three (or another phase depending of the effect description). A player loses the game when this card War Point reaches 0.


DIVINE

This card is used to turn the tide of a game with a one-time-only effect. This card is always placed face down before starting the game.
Functions in general
The Divine card cannot be attacked but can be affected by other card effect. This card can never be destroyed, but can only be used once. The Divine card has no stats, only a unique effect. When used, the card becomes face up.


SOLDIER

The Soldier card is the main way of protecting your Master of War and attacking enemy Soldier or Master of War. Soldier work in pair with War Weapon card most of the time. This card is always placed face up.
Functions in general
This card can attack enemy Soldier cards with their power and defend against them with their resistance stat. It’s the same thing against a Master of War. Soldier work with class effect. Each class has a unique effect, but it’s the same across the class (meaning two different Soldier with the Warrior class will have the same effect, but not the same stat). When a Soldier is destroyed, your Master of War loses the amount of War Point the card had originally. Also, when a Soldier card is destroyed, it goes to the graveyard.


WAR WEAPON

War Weapon have different kind of effects. This card is always placed face down until activation
Functions in general
This card can be used during your phase 3 (or another phase if stated otherwise). You can place up to five of them on the same turn. War Weapon end in the graveyard when used/no longer usable or if equipped on a Soldier that ends in the graveyard. This card come in three different types (equipment / turn-based/ instant effect).

Haskel Hexbane and his band of mirthless followers are as ruthless as they are determined. They are members of the Order of Azyr, a Sigmarite organisation dedicated to the rooting out and purging of evil across the Mortal Realms. This clandestine sect has sent Hexbane and his hunters deep within the Nethermaze to bring the burning light of Azyr to the very heart of that foul and unnatural place, that they might scour it in the cleansing fires of justice and Order.

Hexbane's Hunters are fast, deadly, and unrelenting, with a varied roster of warriors. The key to the warband's strength lies in the Price of Victory ability shared by its human fighters, which allows them to gain free upgrades or crusade swiftly across the battlefield when a friendly hunter is taken out of action. This box includes a complete Rivals deck, plus loads of extra cards allowing you to try out a few tricks, as well as universal cards and Grand Alliance cards to use with your other warbands.

This set contains:

     6x push-fit Hexbane's Hunters miniatures – no glue required to assemble, cast in solemn grey to stand out even when unpainted.
     6x double-sided fighter cards, one for each warrior in the warband – Haskel Hexbane, Brydget Axwold, Aemos Duncarrow, Quiet Pock, Grotbiter, and Ratspike.
     A further 66 cards to enhance your fighters and provide new tactics and challenges for your games of Warhammer Underworlds: Nethermaze, including:
     12x Hexbane's Hunters objective cards
     10x Hexbane's Hunters upgrade cards
     10x Hexbane's Hunters gambit cards (includes 1x Domain card)
     6x Universal objective cards, usable by any warband
     7x Universal upgrade cards, usable by any warband (includes 1x Map card)
     7x Universal gambit cards, usable by any warband (includes 2x Domain cards)
     12x Grand Alliance cards, usable by any warband belonging to the specified Grand Alliance
     1x Hexbane's Hunters Warband Background / Rivals Deck Card
     1x cover card


—description from the publisher

Because there where a large number of requests for one of Winsome Games' earliest maps, the England map is available again.

The Age of Steam: England expansion map is a big, expansive 3 panel map in the style of the original Rust Belt map, found in every Age of Steam game. It is best for 5 or 6 players. The expansion can be used with every licensed Age of Steam game, from the original Winsome printing, the later Warfrog printings, the early Eagle printings and the latest Eagle printing of Age of Steam Deluxe.

—description from the publisher

Take off your shoes and pop a cold one it's time for your favorite new party game. Beer Yoga is a game best enjoyed with a cold beverage, and lots of friends. Roll to see who goes first and draw your yoga card. Think you can pull off a pose with drink in hand? That's for the other players to decide! The winner has the most beer chips at the end. Substitute any beverage, or alter challenging poses to make this game great for every adult!

Kikkerland's Beer Yoga is a light-hearted party game for adults. Each game comes in a decorated 'beer can' case with one die, pose cards, beer chips and instructions.

Downward dog, shoulder stand, mountain....but can you bend with a beer? Beer Yoga is a fun game for couples nights or a fun active party. Do your friends think you're nailing that pose? Impress other players to earn points and win! Beer Yoga can be played with any soda, juice or drink of your choice - just stay hydrated! Modify poses to make this game great for every adult player in your group.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Walt Disney's Mickey Mouse and his Friends
description:
Walt Disney's Mick Mouse and his Friends is a simple roll and move game where players begin at Micky's house and travel around a path that passes famous Disney movie settings and characters, such as Geppetto's toy shop, Cinderella's Castle and Goofy's Ice Cream shop. 
Landing on a certain space by the roll of the dice can make a detour off the main path and take a short cut. First to return back to Mickey's home is the winner.

The larger boxed game was published by the National Game Company in Melbourne, Australia, credited to have been the first large-scale Australian manufacturer of locally-designed board games. The game appears an oddity in respect to other National board games in relation to the topic chosen, the use of the Shield logo when most boxed games used the Oval Logo and the suspected late year of publication around 1952 based on the board having Cinderella's Castle with the Disney movie released in 1950.

—user summary

boardgame: Dead by Daylight: The Board Game – Collector's Edition
description:
Death is not an escape.

The hit asymmetric survival horror game comes to tabletop! Take on the role of a ruthless Killer or a resourceful Survivor as you navigate sinister trials.

As a Survivor, plot your moves, coordinate with your allies, and repair generators to power the exit and escape.

As a Killer, anticipate the survivors’ movements and hunt them down to sacrifice on the Entity’s sacrificial hooks.

The Collector's game contains 17 Survivors and 16 Killers, each with their own unique mechanics and styles of play.

boardgame: Call to Adventure: Epic Origins – Elemental Promo Card
description:
Exclusive Gen Con 2022 promo card for visiting Brotherwise's booth, advertised for promoting the Call to Adventure:  Epic Origins Kickstarter that would be shipping in the following weeks after Gen Con 2022.

This card is marked in the bottom right but a dragon head logo accompanied with "GEN" to indicate it is a Gen Con exclusive promo.

Elemental is a Heritage card type (blue) (HER in bottom-left).
Its ability is:
"When you cast a "[Add 0 and activate your Heritage card] Rune, gain a Feat card (Hero or Antihero).  Or, choose one Ability rune and flip it over."

Promotional card awarded by Brotherwise Games, LLC at Gen Con 2022 at their Booth 331.

This is a Character card ("CH") for Eren:  Undercover from the Final Season of Attack on Titan.
He has a Level of 0 and Cost of 0 with no Counterattack.
The Card Number is AOT/SX04-P03 PR.  That means:  it is from the Attack on Titan Set, is part of the Schwarz side, Release is 04, and the Rarity is Promo.
Eren has a blank Trigger icon.
His Power is 3000.
He has 1 Soul Point.
He is given 1 Characteristic:  Eldian.

boardgame: Final Fantasy: The Stolen Seed (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
In this Final Fantasy themed expansion, explore some of your favourite locations from the beloved game series.

Level-up your characters, and upgrade your equipment to help complete your mission.

A battle focused scenario, with some puzzles, and other fun events, such as chocobo racing, and more.

A new way to fight: Stagger your foes to stop their attacks.

Encounter foes who have their own unique abilities.

Use the JRPG Backpack to store non-time-stamped items, securing them for the next run.

Do everything you can to retrieve The Stolen Seed.

(Even if you've never played a Final Fantasy game before, I believe you'll still find this to be an enjoyable expansion. For sure, some characters and references will be lost on you, but the game and story as a whole should still make sense as a fun fantasy campaign. No knowledge of Final Fantasy is needed to solve the puzzles or to complete your mission. I'm really interested to hear how opinions differ between those who are familiar with Final Fantasy and those who aren't).

Best of luck Time Agents.

Witt incorporates the single piece movement and the jump and capture mechanism of checkers along with allowing players to jump their own pieces as in chinese checkers. There are multiple objectives that individually could be an agreed upon as a single win condition or in various combinations where all are needed to win.

The possible winning objectives are:

     get a single peg to the row behind the opposite black line on the board (penetration).
     get a single peg into the opponent's starting area, and keeping it there without capture for a single turn (occupation).
     achieve a score of 13 (kept on two tracks on the side of the board) where a point is scored at the beginning of each turn for each of the player's pegs in the opponent's territory, even if it survived from a previous turn (accumulation)
     at the beginning of the player's turn, has a single peg in the opponents territory that has all 8 surrounding holes empty (isolation).
     eliminate all of the opponent's pieces (elimination).


There are also rules to prevent blocking (3 pegs of one player in a row) and to get players' pieces out of the set up area (one player cannot have more than 4 more pegs than the other in the set-up areas).

Beetleground is a dexterity “dewling” game.

Players take turns aiming dew that’s dripping off overhanging leaves at beetles on a stump below. Beetles they knock off the stump are added to their collection.

The player that has a better beetle collection and more dew droplets closer to the stump’s center without knocking the ladybug off the stump wins!

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Caught Cosplaying Promo Card
description:
TeeTurle/Unstable Games convention promo card

Name: Caught Cosplaying
Card type: Emotional Disaster
Text: You stop to grab a coffee on your way to the convention. No one understands your elaborate costume.

boardgame: The Game: The War Between the States
description:
The War Between the States is a gigantic timeline of facts, develops study skills, strengthens research skills, and promotes the practice of critical and creative thinking skills.  The multidisciplinary approach forces relationships between the Civil War and the following disciplines: mathematics, social science (history, politics, economics, and geography,) fine arts (art, music, drama, and dance,) language arts (creative writing, literature, and language,) science , and the occupational arts.

The game consists of a 'board' of over 346 spaces, which are displayed in sequential order all around a classroom or other large room.  Each player will create their own pawn to move, based on some historical item from the period: a cannon, a horse and carriage, a Southern Belle figurine, etc.

The goal of the game is to complete all the activities through the end of the war.  The player with the highest score wins.  No additional points are specifically assigned for speed, but players who do finish before other players are done may go back to the beginning of the board and play through more spaces to earn more points.

Each player takes their turn independently; they do not have to wait for others to catch up.  Each space on the board contains either an activity, a quiz, a research project, all in chronological order starting in the 1830's and ending at the surrender of the Confederate forces in 1865.  If a player cannot complete the task in the space after a reasonable amount of time, they are allowed to move either one space back or forward, and try again.

Each player has a Personal Score sheet and a Witness Tally sheet.  When a player completes the task on a board space, they are scored on a scale of 1 to 5 according to the completeness of the effort.  Witnesses are assigned for each session, and they function as rules enforcers while other players are taking their turns.  Witnesses also earn points for monitoring other players, and log their activity accurately on the Witness Tally sheet.

The game ends when either all players complete a course through the spaces to the end, or a pre-determined time limit has been reached.

Put on the robe of an apprentice of the greatest science of all, alchemy, and try your hand at mixing mandrake roots, wyvern wishbones and mermaid scales into potions of strength, invisibility, luck, and anything else your customers desire. Boiling cauldrons, exploding potions, trolls and gnomes, a grumpy old alchemist, a playful narrator, and an ever-happy goat await you!

Alchemist's Apprentice is a solo print-and-play dice-rolling, puzzle-solving narrative adventure brimming with fantasy. Every turn, the player will roll a set of dice representing various alchemical ingredients, and incorporate them into the potion, solving the puzzle of its formula requirements. Each ingredient has special properties, which allow the player to affect other dice and impact the volatility of the potion, bringing logic and deduction to the forefront of the game.

Every chapter of the gamebook advances the story and introduces new mechanics as the master-alchemist trusts the player with new ingredients and more complicated potions.

—description from the publisher

description:
Conquest: First Blood focuses on a single Character and their entourage, allowing players to experience the world of Conquest through the eyes of a soldier in the shieldwall rather than the eagle eye view of an army Commander.

The first iteration of the rules did a great job at providing players with a fast whirling melee whilst serving as an excellent introduction to TLAOK. However, we felt that the game system did not allow enough room for tactical play and maneuvering and so a decision was made to make Conquest: First Blood! 2.0 a fully fledged competitive, skirmish wargame!

To do that we focused on the nuances of individual Model positioning and intra-Regiment synergies. The rules now treat individual teams of soldiers as building blocks together which form powerful ad-hoc formations. It is each player’s job to find the weak point in their enemy’s formation and disentangle it through smart maneuvering and intelligent order of play.

First Blood 2.0 also offer the opportunity to create a force from a faction you are interested in without committing to a full TLAOK army. A typical First Blood 2.0 Force consists of a single Character, 2-3 Officers (known as Command Models in TLAOK) and an average of about 20 soldiers. Expect every game entity to have about double their Wounds than their TLAOK equivalents, whilst point costs have been rehauled to better reflect the impact of certain Regiments in a smaller scale (and faster) game!

東方酔符宴 (Toho Suifuen - Oriental Banquet of Drunken Sign)

Gensokyo's Spell Card Wars explode! The new mechanism of the Oriental fan board game "Oriental Drunken Talisman Banquet"  is about to start a bloody battle!

In a turn all players declare and activate spells.  They may then discard any number cards in their hands. If they do so, they draw as many cards as they discarded. When a player's HP becomes 0, he is shot down. Player that took them gets some VPs and player that has been shot down returns with max HP.

When a turn ends and there are players with 30 VP or more, the game ends. Player with highest VP wins.

—user summary

幻想鄉的符卡大戰震撼引爆！
全新機制的東方同人桌遊《東方醉符宴》
PF18 即將展開熱血激鬥！

在遊戲中選擇最符合您戰鬥風格的東方美少女角色

派出魔理沙以強大的破壞力橫掃千軍
運用射命丸文神速先制搶奪殘血對手的尾刀
整備東風谷早苗滴水不漏的防禦累積得點
選擇靈夢均衡的攻守兼備見機行事

伴隨著手氣天運的抽牌擲骰與聰明策畫的符卡組合互相搭配
才是在競爭激烈的大亂鬥中生存下來的不二法門
在這場幻想鄉大激戰脫穎而出，邁向勝利寶座吧

boardgame: Feed the Shoggoth!: Morsels of Madness
description:
Morsels of Madness is the first expansion for the humorous Lovecraftian card game Feed the Shoggoth!. Morsels of Madness contains 42 new cards to use with the core game, including:

Five new types of Minions
New Cult Factions
New Artifacts
New Spells
Plus, handy turn guides and instructions on how to use the expansion!

Note: You must have a copy of Feed the Shoggoth! in order to use this expansion. It is not a standalone game.

Pohjolan aarteet is a humorous game that shows how devastating mining law Finland has and how mining companies destroy nature while also avoid paying taxes.

Gameplay is dice rolling and exploration on a map with a light tactical card game.

Players are mining companies and they try to make mining occupations and find treasures to maximize profit while they also try to avoid new rules created by the State. Every location on the map has some event that requires a draw a card.

When all places are visited the game ends and players with the most profit is the winner. .

description:
Wizlords in Space: Cosmic Card-tastrophe is a tactical 2 player card game from Wizbiz Games!

Magic-enhancing oomph has been discovered deep in space, and wizards are battling over who can gain the most of it in order to be crowned the Wizlord! But how much oomph will it take to reign supreme? That’s not a trick question; the first player to store 6 oomph will become the ultimate Wizlord in Space!

Take turns playing and using different combinations of elemental wizards to attack opponents, ward to set up future turns, meditate to gain oomph, store the oomph already on your wizard, or use each wizard's unique cantrip. Players can also equip powerful, double-sided spell cards from a shared deck if the element on the spell matches one of their wizards.

At the end of each turn, players gain an amount of oomph that increases each time the spell deck is shuffled, making things more and more frantic as the game goes on! Will you store your oomph for safekeeping, or use its power to take out your opponent and risk losing it all? Choose wisely, and you just might be crowned the Wizlord in Space!

A Fun, Hilarious Party Drinking Game: Set Your Inner Party Animal Free! Turn game night up a notch with this adult party game. Compete with your friends to complete the most dares without getting too TIPSY!

Fill up you glass with any drink you favor.

Draw a dare card and read it out out. Complete the dare or ...don't. If you complete the dare, draw a goat card and keep your dare card for points. If you don't, draw a chicken card and take your punishment. Discard the dare card. No points for you!

When a player reaches 13 points, the game ends. The player that first reached 13 points is the winner!

This Magic: The Gathering expansion returns to the world of Dominaria just as a new Phyrexian invasion has begun, led by the praetor Sheoldred. Can the heroes of Dominaria unite to fight them back, or will they be undone by the traitor lurking among their own ranks?

Dominaria United is sold in draft boosters, set boosters, collector boosters, jumpstart boosters, and bundles. Additionally, two preconstructed Commander decks were released alongside the main set as tie-ins.

And to be the new cult leader they have to command their followers to the objective of summon up an ancient terrible god.
