description:
Great Outdoors is a small, non-essential pack of 12 extra Cards that provide a touch of variety to Spire's End: Hildegard, with 8 new illustrations by Diego Frias.


     2  Instruction Cards
     9 Story Cards for Chapter 2
     1 Story Card for Chapter 4

Зловещие призраки (Sinister Ghosts) is a fast card game in which players have to survive during the round, escaping from ghosts.
Both "Escape" cards and "Fear" cards will help players survive, thanks to which players will be able to steal from rivals.
The main thing in this game is to stay alive at any cost! Fight with intangible monsters or run. And remember: you don't need to be the fastest, it's important that someone is slower than you.
During the game, players hold ghost cards, other players' fear cards, and ghost escape cards. Every turn, players must play cards and if a player plays a ghost, then he puts it in front of him, if at the beginning of the next turn he plays an escape, then he will escape from the ghost and get a winning point. The player who has escaped from the greatest number of ghosts wins.

Tournament Kit Season 5

A strategy game for two. The players alternate putting one marble on the edge of the game board. If there already is a marble, just push it forward with your own marble. The first one to get five of their own marbles in a row, wins the game.

—user summary

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Big Red One Edition – Battle for Caen: Operation Epsom, Charnwood & Goodwood
description:
July 1944, to liberate the city of Caen and its surroundings, the brave soldiers of His Majesty launched a series of operations which required 7 Infantry Divisions, 9 Armored Divisions and 4 Armored Brigades. They were fighting the biggest battle of the Normandy Campaign.

Relive those epic battles through the 6 scenarios of this campaign. From the hills south of Caen, to the heart of the city, fight house by house, grove by grove, until victory. Break through the German defense lines, destroy the tanks of the SS Panzer Divisions, and drive the Germans out of France! Long live the King!

You must have the Heroes of Normandie base game in order to use this Scenario Pack.

You can also combine this game with any Heroes System expansions.

This is Battle Pack #2, covering Operations Epsom - Charnwood - Goodwood.

— description from the publisher
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Big Red One Edition – Battle for Caen: Operation Windsor & Jupiter
description:
The soldiers of the 3rd Canadian Infantry Division were sent to assault the Carpiquet airport. Five days later, the men of the 43rd British Infantry Division stormed the slopes of Hill 112.

These two operations took place in parallel to the larger actions undertaken to liberate the city of Caen. It is up to you to prove your bravery and tactical skills. Take up the challenge and be victorious, for the King!

This is Battle Pack #3, covering Operations Windsor & Jupiter.

You must have the Heroes of Normandie base game and the Battle Pack #2, Battle For Caen: Epsom, Goodwood & Charnwood, in order to use this Scenario Pack.

You can also combine this game with any Heroes System expansions.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Can you rise to the challenge and come up with the best description, name or sentence using a maximum of three words to be the Top Duck?

If you think you've got the feathers, then Oh My Words! is the perfect game for you.

Oh My Words! can be played by three or more players, and is suitable for anyone older than the age of six.

You must have sense of humour and like words.

How to Play


     Each player draws 6 Word cards from the game box. 
     A nominated Judge is selected. The Judge doesn't participate in a round, only imparts wise judgement.
     The nominated Judge draws a random Challenge card from the game box. This will either be a Describe, Create or Finish the sentence challenge.
     Each player must respond to the challenge using a maximum of 3 Word Cards. They place their cards down and say out their response. Suffixes can be used to modify the words. Hints are provided on the Word cards.
     The Judge chooses the best response. This could be the most accurate or funny response. The Judge has the power to decide.  
     The winner gets a point and can keep the Challenge card. 
     The next round starts by players topping up their Word cards to 6 and selecting a new Judge. 
     Play for as long as you want and at the end the player with the most points / Challenge cards wins!

The rules are simple.

Grab pen and paper. 
Write down all the names/teams on a sheet of paper. 
Toss all party, naughty and joker cards together and take turns drawing a card.

The player who draws the card reads out the assignment. When the task is completed, the player receives the corresponding number of points after their name or team.

However, if the player does not complete the task, he must drink what the card indicates.

The team or player with the least number of points loses and must draw one of 5 loser cards. The team or player who reaches 20 points first wins and the team or player with the least number of points at that point loses.

If you are playing in teams, there is mutual discussion as to who on the team should draw the loser card and also execute it.

—description from the designer

Your once-quiet village is infested with werewolves... Can you and the other villagers find them before they eliminate you?

Ultimate Werewolf Extreme is the best-selling party game where each player has a secret role: as a villager, hunt down and eliminate the werewolves; as a werewolf, convince the other players that you're innocent, while secretly eliminating them.

Choose from dozens of special roles each game for unlimited replayability!

This edition of Ultimate Werewolf includes:

Ultimate Werewolf Extreme
Ultimate Werewolf Bonus Roles
Ultimate Werewolf Pro
Name card pad
Portable deck box
"Got Villager?" bumper sticker
Moderator score pad
200 custom card sleeves
25 award certificates
4 Punchboard tokens: Ivory Tower, Charm, Sandwich, Gavel
GameTrayz Organizer

—description from the publisher

Finalist in the 2023 Boulogne Billancourt International Contest.
        Nominee for Most Innovative Game at BostonFIG 2022.
        Finalist in the Lunchtime Game Challenge on The Game Crafter.
    


Retro Tetro has malfunctioned and is threatening the homes of all of the Meeple People!  Save the video board game world from rogue polyomino buildings by diverting them carefully around the Meeple People and onto their proper places.  As the hero of the digital world, use the foresight granted to you by the polyomino cards and use precision to maximize points in the retro style puzzle game.

The game is played over 24 short rounds of one card each.  When each card is played, all players will choose which of the pieces to place onto their own personal Arcade Board following two main rules:


     Like pieces cannot touch sides.  


AND


     A piece cannot be played below one already placed.


Over the course of the game, you will gain bonus points in the form of coins by covering spaces with matching pieces on the board or from completing highlighted half rows.  If you are unable to play a piece for any reason, you will lose two of your hard earned coins for each!  You must plan efficiently to avoid this, keeping in mind that, out of the 8 possible shapes, you only get 4 of each.  Score at the end of the game for collected coins, building tall columns, covering gold squares at the top of the board and saving Meeple People.  Add in "?" cards that let you pick any piece you want and abilities that give you a chance at extra points or take on the evil Tetro Corporation in 12 challenging solo levels and Retro Tetro will entertain you for hours!

—description from the description

Storyteller is a co-operative boardgame with one Game Master, called the Storyteller, and up to three other players called “Adventurers”.

The Storyteller is the player who controls the enemies in the game, and who narrates the story and encounters throughout the game, all of which are written on their own cards.

The Adventurers engage in different encounters across the world, either combat or exploration. Combat encounters are solved using an Adventurer’s skills, attributes and items. Exploration encounters are less harmful, and mostly involve a short riddle, or a decision to be made that has immediate consequences.

—description from the designer

Beautiful Monsters is a quick-playing 2-player card game about recruiting a team of monsters, dressing them up in accessories, and entering them into a beauty contest!

Multi-use cards: Each monster can have a varying number of accessories attached, and each accessory can be attached to augment the monster's traits -OR- enhance their personality. Scoring is tight, so this choice makes a world of difference!

Hybrid open/closed drafting: You can use monsters/accessories from your hand -OR- from the Shortlist/Market (public card rows). Either way, you always end up putting a card into at least one of those rows. Will you put junk there to limit your opponent's options, or will you take a risk by leaving something useful for you to use on your next turn?

Variable scoring criteria: Each game has a unique combination of scoring criteria shared by both players. 2 criteria award points based on which monster(s) you committed to it. A 3rd criteria modifies those points based on the composition of your opponent's team or even the Shortlist/Market. Focusing your strategy on one area means neglecting another, so pick your battles carefully!

Beautiful Monsters was created and self-published from the ground up by Chris Logsdon and Alix Lytton (aka Maow House). What started as a challenge to make a game in 1 month has become their first foray into their dream of bringing to life the games they want to see in the world.

—description from the publisher

Crystal Chaos is a simple yet deceptively strategic card game for the whole family. It is a fast and friendly game for ages 10+ and designed for 3-6 players. Easy to play but challenging to master, this highly re-playable game will have you smirking, shouting, and laughing out loud. Crystal Chaos should take less than a minute to learn the rules as they're all printed on a single card. Once players get the feel of it, expect the game to last 10-20 minutes.

Each player starts with a hand of five cards, with the goal of ending a turn with only the Treasure card in their hand. On your turn, you will draw a card from the draw deck to your hand, then play a card to use its power. Cards will allow you to swap hands between players, discard additional cards, make players draw cards, peek at other players' hands, and more. Slowly gain information about where the Treasure card is (whether its in the deck or another player's hand) and manipulate the field to make it yours! The game ends either when one player has the Treasure card as their only card at the end of any player's turn, making them the winner, or when the draw deck is emptied, the player who has the Treasure card at the end of the turn wins.

—description from the designer

The legend of the Crystal Cavern goes back centuries to a king who hid all his valuables in a cave behind a mystical barrier. The cave walls almost felt alive as they bloomed with vividly colored crystals and pulsed with a soothing glow. Once filled, the cavern entrance blurred out of existence, not to be seen again for centuries, if ever.

Ever since, adventurers have been searching for the long-lost Cavern. It is said that the Cavern itself will sprout crystals up throughout the landscape to tease adventurers, leaving tiny shimmering breadcrumbs. Some say the Cavern only uses these to lead adventurers astray while others claim they are meant to lead the chosen to its bountiful treasure. Which will happen to you? Will you succumb to the Chaos magic and wander endlessly? Will you outsmart the Cavern with wit and strategy? Will you be deemed worthy and wander effortlessly into the Crystal Cavern?

—description from the back of the box
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A source book for Flames of War detailing the desert campaigns of North Africa for all combatants (British, America, German, and Italian).

It has been a while since we turned the spotlight on the war in the desert, but that is about to change with the release of the North Africa compilation and the accompanying starter and army sets.

Whether you are returning to the Mid-War period or starting from scratch, everything you need is now easily accessible in one place. The North Africa compilation includes all of the existing Mid-War books for the war in the desert:

Afrika Korps, 90th Light, and Death from Above for the Germans,
Armoured Fist and Red Devils for the British,
Avanti and more from Death from Above for the Italians, and
Fighting First and All American for the Americans.

In addition, we’ve brought back many of the odd and unusual tanks from the Mid-War Monsters book from ten years ago.

The book really does have everything a beginner needs: history and background, interesting formations, organisational information for building a force, and game stats for playing the game. Each army has a painting and basing guide, along with a beautifully illustrated catalogue of the available models. It is a one-stop gaming resource for the fighting in the Western Desert leading to the decisive Battle of Alamein and the close-fought battles in Tunisia that finally sealed the fate of the Axis in North Africa.

Each country has its own section, starting with a guide to some of the major battles they fought, their unique special rules, and the organisation of their forces. Each country has a selection of formations that you can field. These usually include light and medium tanks or self-propelled guns, and various types of infantry including armoured infantry in half-tracks for the Americans, paratroopers for each country, and rangers and commandos. This is followed by a selection of anti-tank guns, artillery, anti-aircraft guns, and aircraft to support your core formation. All-in-all, there are 33 different formations to choose from, or to mix and match to create your unique force.

In Pokémon Match Battle players are trying to be the first to win 3 match battles. It is a simple ‘spin-and-compare’ game where each player has only one Pokémon card. Players are each dealt one card at the beginning of each game and keep that card for the whole game. Games appear to be of short time length.

This game is very simple and designed for play by young kids by using simple rules, low complexity and short time length. These were distributed in August 2022 as part of a McDonald’s Happy Meal program, at least in the US. As usual for McD’s there were multiple sets. The only apparent difference between them is the color highlight and representative Pokémon on the box and also used on the plastic game spinner for that set. Each set has a sealed pack of 4 Pokémon cards.

On their turn, the player flips the ‘coin’ (cardboard circle about 1.5” diameter) to determine whether high or low will win. Next the player uses the spinner to determine which of the 5 attribute categories will be used for this turn. Then all players compare that attribute among the cards. The player with the highest or lowest (based on the coin toss) value wins the match point. The first player to win 3 matches (points) wins.

The cards that we’ve seen show a distinctive set logo in the lower left corner and show #/15, for a total set of only 15 different cards apparently. They are a mix of Basic and Stage 1. Presumably any Pokémon cards can be used with this game.

Ride Chip Trick is a trick-taking game which uses wooden discs instead of playing cards.  The black discs have positive ranks and are dealt to the players.  The red discs have negative ranks and start out of the game.

Players may play 1 or 2 chips to each trick, and the person who plays the highest sum will win the trick.  They take all chips from the trick into a score pile in front of them and a red chip into their hand.

The game is played over a number of rounds equal to the number of players, and a round is over when the red chips have all been taken.

Players earn points equal to the sum of the chips left in their hand and those collected from tricks.  However, only point totals from 1 to 25 will be scored; all over scores result in 0 points.

The player with the most points wins.
4D was an expansion for the game Birth which added rules and components for a fourth player.

You played too many games with cute creatures during the day. So now they show up in your dreams, but your dreams turn into nightmares and you have to battle these cute creatures with dice.

Xterminate Cute Creatures is a free solo roll and write game. The goal is to defeat or flee all creatures in only 25 dice rolls, and try to get as many points as you can.

Try to get a third die by taking on the snake, and collect potions or dynamite to help you reach your mission.

—description from the designer

Get the full load (minus 4 expansions) of the modern classic tile-laying game with the Isle of Skye Big Box! The Big Box contains the award-winning Isle of Skye base game (Kennerspiel des Jahres 2016), the Druids expansion, and additional scoring and tunnel tiles. A new addition is the mini-expansion Randgebiete.

In the game, you embody one leader of each of the five traditional Scottish clans. You create your own small empire in order to gain the necessary victory points. But in each game only four of the included 24 score cards are in play. This makes each game different and requires different tactics and strategies.

In each round you can buy one of the tiles of a fellow player. Face down, all players simultaneously set the prices of the pieces they offer. A high price can lead to prosperity and much needed money. However, if no buyer can be found, the seller must add the tile to his empire himself and give up the money he has invested. In the end, the player who has scored the most victory points wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

The "Randgebiete"/ borderland expansion is a module that can be added to any game, alone or together with the expansions.

Every player starts with the same 6 borderland tiles in their supply. There are two tiles each of water, mountain, and land.
Each of them has only two sides for connections and two already closed borders.
In each round, when you place your other tiles, you may also place one borderland tile connected to at least one other tile. If it’s connected to other tiles on both sides, it gets flipped to the better side with more scoring symbols (like cows, sheep) on it.
No tile may be placed touching one of the other two sides with closed borders.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Адмирал: эпоха парусных сражений (Admiral: Era of Sailing Battles)
description:
Real sea battles
Admiral is a board game with the sea, sailboats, clear and very cool mechanics, a pirate addition and paintings by Aivazovsky. A great example of how an incredibly addictive board game turns out from a small number of details, which can be played both together and four, or you can even combine two boxes and play with a large crowd. The game has basic and complicated rules, scenarios and a pirate add-on.

Detailed study of the movements and combat actions of the ships, of course. Everything is simple: for each turn you have two cards common to everyone: the number of possible moves in different wind directions, and the state of the sea (when the wind is weak, ships can generally drift, and when strong, accelerate several times). The goal of the game — almost like in checkers — is to knock out all opponents from the field. There are guns and boarding for this!

As in a classic naval battle, ships from one successful shot do not sink, but lose their mast. The larger the ship, the more guns it can shoot, attacking or defending. Moreover, in order to shoot, you need to stand sideways to the enemy, and in order to board, go to his cage and press side to side, like real ships. But boarding allows you not only to injure or sink an enemy ship, but also to capture it by transferring your mast to it! (Remember that only your ships must remain on the field to win?)

Logical repair rules
You can return the ship to your fortress to restore its masts instead of moving forward. But a fortress cannon is spent on each mast, which means that if an enemy sailboat approaches from the side of the fortress, where there are no more guns, he will be able to attack the fortress! Well, strategists and tacticians, are you ready for sea adventures?

Ulandi Wars is a unique and dramatic expandable battle card game in which players compete in Ulandi’s spectacular sci-fi universe.

A beautifully illustrated Sci-fi card game for 2 or more players set in the amazing universe of Ulandi.

Ulandi Wars is a one-of-a-kind combat card game in which players can change the victory condition of the game using our unique event card packs.

Gain control of powerful faction decks and battle your way across the galaxy to gain control of the western belt.

Choose your hero, arm them with mighty weapons, use your infrastructure cards to generate deployment tokens to deploy powerful cards from your hand to the combat zone, outwit your opponents with special upgrade event cards and fight your way to victory!

boardgame: Marvel United: X-Men – Juggernaut Promo Deck
description: Deck of 12 cards to play with Juggernaut as a hero. This was given out as a free promotional item during Gen Con and SPIEL 2022. Later part of the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter.
Micro expansion with 9 (1 black + 8 white) new cards to finish the phrase Yo Mama...

boardgame: Lobotomy 2: Manhunt – Lobotomy 1 Monsters: From the Deep
description:
These miniature from the Lobotomy expansion "From the Deep" are now playable in Lobotomy 2 with the included Daily Nightmare cards!  With this pack, you will be able to play two additional scenarios.

1 Dagon
4 Deep One
4 Siren
 Miniatures in this set are the same as those in the original Lobotomy. If you already own these miniatures, check out the 'Lobotomy 1: Monster Conversion Set' add-on!

In this quick trick-taking 2 player, 18 card wallet game, you compete against your oponent to gather evidence for your theory completing challenges and matching cards according to their value and corresponding faction (vampires, reptilians, aliens and gnomes).

Ready...Set... Act Fast!  In this fast-paced charades game, one "Guesser" is tasked with guessing the different charades that multiple "Actors" are acting.  The catch? All players are acting at the same time! Each "Actor" is armed with three sand-timers, and must ACT FAST before time runs out... If a charade is guessed correctly, that player simply flips over a new sand-timer and starts all over again. When the round is done, both the "Guesser" and "Actors" are awarded points for each correctly guessed charade.

Flip it. Act it. Guess it. Repeat! Will you act fast enough to beat your competitors?

Fulbo! a card game for up to 4 players, in which each player must help their team win by scoring the most points after ten matches.
Matches will be heated, players will get sweaty, and everyone  will try to bring those points home.

In Fulbo! players must improve their teams stats, boosting them through players, coaches and stats cards.

But watch out! Because others can try to stop you in your tracks on your way to the top, either by sending you hooligans to cause trouble, injuring your players, creating internal conflicts in your team, or meddling in your matches scoring goals or causing a power outage in the stadium .

Fulbo! The game where no one is booked for excessive celebrations.

boardgame: Legacy of Yu: Champion Promo Card
description:
1 x Champion Barbarian Card
1 x Rules Card

This promo provides three ways to increase the difficulty for Legacy of Yu:

     After setting up the game, add the new Champion Barbarian Card by placing it roughly in the middle of the Barbarian Draw Pile. He requires 4 Workers of a single colour to be defeated.
     Destroy 2 Townsfolk Cards from the top of your Ready Pile before starting the game.
     Start the game with 5 Townsfolk Cards and 2 Barbarian Cards along the top of the Board.

Contains 12 new Fate Cards + 1 Rules Card.

During Setup, shuffle all 12 new Fate Cards into the Fate Deck. Some of these cards cause the Picts to attack 2 Cohorts, while others have them not attacking at all.

Using Favours:
When using Favours to ignore Fate Cards targeting 2 Cohorts, a single Favour can only prevent 1 of those attacks. To prevent both attacks, 2 Favours would need to be used.

4 new Heroes for Raiders of Scythia from the Garphill Ancient Anthologies Promo Pack.

Hirene: Gain 3 Cards
Sarakos: Gain 2 Livestock or lose 1 to gain 1 other Plunder type
Mada: Gain 2 Cards and optionally take 1 Market action
Danube: Gain 2 Provisions and remove 1 Wound

Alaria; Valor and Company is an indie Table Top RPG, that offers a fresh take on the traditional TTRPG experience. It features Alaria, an original adventure setting introduced through a brand new RPG system named Valor and Company.

Alaria; Valor and Company is a high strategy TTRPG that encourages teamwork, planning, discovery, and adaptation. Heavily inspired by traditional table top rpgs, and squad based tactics games. Adventure through a variety of different scenarios in the isolated colony of Alaria using a refined RPG system designed to facilitate everything from exploration to wargames. Just like classic role-playing games such as Dungeons & Dragons, every adventure has a different length and success conditions.

The game comes with full roleplaying support in the form of a skills system to perform any sort of task, custom character creation rules, and a completely original combat system.

It also comes with a competitive Player vs Player mode with a collection of maps and characters for this competitive mode

Plants

﻿8 new Flora cards – two for each habitat – that are shuffled into the Animal cards and add new and interesting decisions to the gameplay.

Plants are guaranteed to photograph successfully but are not worth prestige at the end - you could use them to complete a Photography Award or even trigger the end of the game... but is it worth missing out on the prestige?

You can use these cards at any player count to add tactical depth.

Solo Mode

You can now play the game solo using the "SnapBot" that uses a deck of cards to simulate another player to compete against for solo players.

SnapBot has a range of difficulty settings and recreates different player-interactions for an exciting and variable 1-player experience.

5th Player

By adding in the 8 plants, there are enough cards in the Animal card deck to sustain a 5-player game. The game plays much the same as the 2-4 player version so there are no new rules to learn.

boardgame: Zona: Sekret Czarnobyla – Dodatkowe żetony zagrożeń
description:
A set of 2 new mutants and 2 previously unknown anomalies that will add variety to your Zona expeditions, released in Polish only.

Rules
Szybkowij, mutant tile

Bies, mutant tile

Purchawka, anomaly tile

Otchłań, anomaly tile
"Sucking up energy – After defeating the abyss, move the emission progress marker back to the "0" field on the emission track."

It’s the ultimate race around the world! Hit the skies in hot air balloons with Ryan and the Combo Crew! In this adventurous and educational board game, you will visit all seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, South America, and North America.

Spin the spinner, take turns moving your hot air balloons across the board, and collect stars along the way! The player with the most stars in the end wins. Along the way, you will learn about capital cities, flags, national flowers, and what makes each country unique! With collectible Micro Figures, Micro Figure Cards, and more, your world tour with Ryan and the Combo Crew is full of epic surprises. Each game comes with 2 collectible Micro Figures. Collect all 53! Each figure celebrates something signature to each country.

PLAYABLE FROM 4 YEARS: Génération Mimes is a board game accessible to the youngest thanks to these children's cards with images which also allow non-readers to play. It is also an EDUCATIONAL GAME because it allows the youngest to consolidate their vocabulary.

3 LEVELS OF DIFFICULTY FOR A GREAT GIFT: This board game has been designed for everyone to have fun: it is an ideal gift for children and adults alike! It's a card game that will allow beginners and mime experts to have a good time!

BONUS: HILARIOUS CHALLENGES: Act out with your eyes closed or your hands behind your back! Not easy to mimic with the challenges we have created for you for this card game! A board game mechanic that changes and allows you to go crazy with friends in the evening.

A SIMPLE BOARD GAME: The rules are accessible to all: One of the players mimes, the one who guesses wins the card and will have to mime in turn. The first to 10 cards wins the game! Play classic mode or team mode for even more fun!

A TIMELESS CONCEPT: this mime game is one of the most entertaining games to spend time having fun. Whether you are with family or friends, here to laugh or to win, Génération Mimes is the solution to have a good time!

On your turn you will:

Announce the number of question cards whose answers you think you can remember. Beware, each Question card randomly contains between 2 and 5 questions

Listen and memorize in one go all the answers to the questions asked.

Restore the answers without making mistakes and respecting the initial order.

Let memory win!

There are 3 types of themes, fun, smart and personal

There are 6 lifelines to spice up your games

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Commander 2020: Ikoria: Lair of Behemoths Commander – Enhanced Evolution
description:
Grow and develop your creatures' powers to new heights with the Enhanced Evolution deck—bring your mutated monsters back from the dead with Otrimi, the Ever-Playful; build an army of hydras with Zaxara, the Exemplary; or gain life and strength as you take down your enemies with Cazur, Ruthless Stalker and Ukkima, Stalking Shadow.

Enhanced Evolution is one of five Commander Decks from Ikoria: Lair of Behemoths (IKO). Each includes exclusive cards tailored to that deck—pick your favorite mechanics and dive in to battle!

Battle your way through the plane of Ikoria with monster-themed mechanics that grow your creatures, build your bond, and crush your opponents.

Contents: one (1) foil oversized Commander card, one hundred (100) card deck, ten (10) double-sided tokens, one (1) deck box, one (1) Life Wheel, one (1) punchout keyword counter card

description:
Find out what secrets your friends have been hiding in Judge Your Friends! Learn things about your friends you wish you hadn’t in this outrageous, fast-paced party game. 
Each round, players take on the role of Judge and Defendant. One player is the Judge and reads a scenario card aloud to the group. The other players, or Defendants, enter their plea of whether they are guilty or not guilty of that scenario (The Defendant has taken a nap at work – you know you’re guilty!).
Then, one by one, the Judge gives their ruling while banging that golden gavel – yes, it’s a golden gavel. For every correct ruling, the Judge earns $100 in cold hard cash. Play continues for multiple rounds with players pleading guilty or not guilty, other players calling friends out for lying about their pleas, and Judges raking in the dough. At the end of the game, the player with the most cash is the winner.
Judge Your Friends is great for playing in person or on your next zoom game night with easy to learn game instructions and flexible content.

—description from the publisher

The Winged Boot was the unofficial award given to British and later adopted by American airmen who “flew out” and “walked back” so they could continue their duty. Although not official, airmen would have their badges custom made in London and worn concealed under the lapels of their uniforms. This was an exclusive club that nobody signed up for. You could say, you just fell into it.

In Winged Boot, you assume the role of a downed bomber crew member. You could be anyone from the pilot to the tail gunner. The game picks up from the point of exiting the aircraft and continues until you are either killed, captured, or rescued. Once that end has been met, you can then step back in time to assume the role of one of the other surviving crewmen, from the beginning. That is, of course, if there were any.

You may find that some, or all, of your fellow crewmembers went down with the aircraft and you may or may not link up with the survivors on the ground. Either way, it is now your mission to evade capture and make your way through enemy territory and find your way back to your squadron in England.

Winged Boot is a stand-alone choose your own adventure style game, but it is designed as an add on for Bomb Run, a solitaire, B-17 bomber experience. It could easily be applied to games like B-17, Queen of the Skies and Target for Today.

Winged Boot is played from a large coil bound book of 72 pages using 21 different 2-page maps and pages of narrative driven paragraphs to help tell your story.

Winged Boot comes with an entire fresh crew that you can mix in and play with your copy of Bomb Run.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Shadowland Daredevil & Elektra
description:
When Daredevil returns to Hell’s Kitchen as the new leader of the Hand Ninja clan, he abandons law and order for a much harsher approach. With an army of Hand Ninjas by his side, can anyone stand in the way of Matt Murdock and this shadowy new incarnation of Daredevil? Can anyone stand against the Man Without Fear when he has an army to command?

Assassin and mercenary Elektra Natchios was trained by the ancient cult of ninjas known as The Hand to be a supreme martial artist and master of stealth. She is willing to work for anyone who can afford her matchless skills, bringing a swift end to those who dare stand in her way. While she sometimes joins forces with Daredevil and other heroes, she is ultimately a lone warrior who follows her own path among the shadows.

boardgame: 18 Holes: Putting, Wind and Coastlines Exclusive
description: Exclusive add on for the putting, wind and Coastlines expansion that adds more course tiles, new green cards, two new golfers and a wooden wind token.

Tactigon is a two player game with a simple ruleset and quick set up. Games typically run 10-15 minutes, so most players like to play multiple games in a sitting.

There are two basic concepts at the root of Tactigon:

Firstly, the geometry of the pieces are related to how they move.  For example, the “Prism” piece resembles a triangle, so it is able to move up to 3 spaces in a turn.

Secondly, “slower” pieces are more powerful than “faster” pieces, so each piece has key strengths that can impact your strategy.

There are also two ways to win in this game, either by controlling certain spots on the board or capturing your opponent's key piece, the Pentagon. 
 You must always be on your guard while aggressively maneuvering in order to gain the advantage.

The rules are simple and the gameplay is intense.

Wage war to muster the most powerful army by creating platoons of troops. As you create platoons, you’ll stack them in front of you in an alternating pattern. Watch out! These troops are mercenaries and your top platoon can be stolen by your opponents if they have a matching troop or a wild card. Every time a platoon is stolen its power grows. Will you build the most powerful army, or be overrun by your opponents?

On your turn you MUST perform one of four possible actions (make a platoon from your hand, make a platoon from the discard pile, attempt to steal a platoon from an opponent, or discard and draw new cards). On your turn you MAY play one tactic card. Tactics are powerful cards that could give you a small advantage or completely change the course of the game.

The player with the most powerful army at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

description:
In this tourist card game you can play vacation in the province of Salta, touring the most traditional tourist spots, take pictures of the best landscapes, enjoy the local cuisine and find the most representative handicrafts of each area.


En este juego de cartas turístico podrás jugar a vacacionar en la provincia de Salta, recorriendo los puntos turísticos más tradicionales, realizar fotografías de los mejores paisajes, disfrutar de la gastronomía local y encontrar las artesanías más representativas de cada zona.

D-DAY SOLITAIRE is a fast-playing roll n' write dice game for one to five players.

The game attempts to recreate the Normandy landings of World War II in a simple yet challenging manner. The design is meant to be a quick, fun, and historical beer & pretzels simulation. There is a map sheet of varying difficulties for each of the five Normandy beaches -- Sword, Juno, Gold, Omaha, and Utah. You play as the Allied D-Day forces that hit the beaches on June 6th, 1944.

In the multi-player variant, each player plays on one of the five beach maps against their own German A.I. opponent. The beach landings vary in difficulty; therefore, "extra points" are awarded based on the beach's level of difficulty in order to achieve a relative balance among all of the beaches. The player who achieves the highest score is declared the winner.

D-Day Solitaire is an entry in the 2022 Wargame Print and Play Game Design Contest

description:
Defiance of the Gods: The Rift is a board and card game that requires players to choose a side, Asgard or Olympus, use their deck to defend and attack strategic locations on the battleground with swords,  death rays, force shields, warships, the Hammer of Thor, the Head of Medusa and so much more… Compete for control and destroy your opponent’s capital or ruler to win!

Defiance of the Gods has been designed so that players may build decks with the cards straight out the box. We have printed some cards less frequently, they are called Singularis cards and represent very powerful Gods, Generals, Weapons,  Treasures, Locations, Terrains, Tactics, etc. These cards can only be used one per deck and as such offer some of the most powerful effects in the game. In one box you will find 275 cards with multiples of staple cards included to help you build your deck and one of each Singularis card in the complete set for the specified edition.

Your rivals issued a challenge: who among you is the biggest name in international thievery? You have to gather a crew, get moving, and make a name for yourself. Keep in mind: “Honor among thieves” is really more of a suggestion.

In this fast-paced card game from Nerdhaus Games, players live out their heist movie fantasies. Colorful characters meet improbable complications on the way to glory and riches (or serious jail time). On your turn, you'll decide to gather resources, do some legwork on a potential job, or brave the risks and claim one of these rare treasures for yourself! Your potential crew members, specialized gear, and game-changing events are also used as currency to pay for other cards, so you have to balance the game effect vs. the value it has in your hand.

Your target options are always shifting due to your rivals' actions and the passage of time in-game, so be ready for anything when your turn comes around. It might just be worth some extra attention from the authorities if it means landing that job right before your window of opportunity closes.

In the Funkoverse Strategy Game, you combine your favorite characters and go head-to-head in exciting game scenarios. Each turn, you select one of your characters and perform two actions. Each character has access to basic actions like moving and challenges as well as several unique abilities that may be performed only by spending ability tokens. Funkoverse uses an innovative “cooldown” system — the more powerful the ability, the longer it will take for the ability token to become available again — so players have to spend their ability tokens wisely. Each character in Funkoverse is unique, so players are encouraged to try out different combinations of characters and items in order to discover their favorite synergies and powerful strategies for the different game scenario.

Race to the finish line in Red Light, Green Light, or try to burst past your rivals’ defenses in The Squid Game! Each figure is a unique, playable character with their own abilities, and Front Man can change the rules of the game as you play, for both allies and rivals!

In the Funkoverse Strategy Game, you combine your favorite characters and go head-to-head in exciting game scenarios. Each turn, you select one of your characters and perform two actions. Each character has access to basic actions like moving and challenges as well as several unique abilities that may be performed only by spending ability tokens. Funkoverse uses an innovative “cooldown” system — the more powerful the ability, the longer it will take for the ability token to become available again — so players have to spend their ability tokens wisely. Each character in Funkoverse is unique, so players are encouraged to try out different combinations of characters and items in order to discover their favorite synergies and powerful strategies for the different game scenario.

Two characters in one! Includes two character cards with unique abilities so you can play as the faceless Masked Manager, or remove the mask and play as the undercover detective Jun-ho!

When playing as the Masked Manager, stay as still as possible to increase the reach of your attacks!
As the detective Jun-ho, gather Evidence Tokens to power up your abilities!

If you can guess what this means, IDKAIDC, then you just played #CultureTags!

#CultureTags is the type of party game that can be as short or long as you want it to be! The categories may include Twitter, Instagram, Church, Family and Friends, etc. There is plenty of gameplay for many types of people, which is perfect for the party crowd! Attempt to guess as many cards as possible!

To begin the game, the first team will flip the timer. The describer will then take a stack of cards from the box and immediately begin. They will begin by stating the category and showing the team the culture tag. They must then give hints without saying any of the words on the card. When the card is answered correctly, it gets placed to the side and a new card is drawn. If the team cannot guess the card, they will pass and place it separately from the cards that are guessed correctly.

When the timer comes to an end, the rest of the cards are placed in the box. The passed cards are left on the table. The cards that the team were able to correctly guessed are placed off to the side, and the team earns one point for each card that is correctly guessed.

The next team will then go through the same process, but they may choose to answer the passed cards or cards from the box. If they are able to guess the passed cards, they score two points per card. Gameplay continues like this until the players decide that the game is over.

The game comes to an end when the players decide to be finished playing. The team with the most points win the game!
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description: Imagenius: Extra Cards #1 contains fifty new cards for use with Imagenius, with new formulas to be solved and new objects to be discovered.

A strategic, competitive colouring in game.

Two players take turns to colour in areas, each player trying to make the finished sheet look like one of two objectives.

The game ends when every section is coloured in.

At the end of the game, the players show the sheet to an impartial judge (or post it on social media) to determine the winner - the person aiming for whichever objective the judge(s) think it looks most like.

Will you focus on your picture, or on ruining your opponent's? To succeed, you'll need a balance of aggression/defense, an understanding of how things might be percieved, and a smidgen of empathy.

Though the final result is determined by aesthetics rather than maths, this is essentially a perfect information abstract strategy game.

A Disney-themed board game, which was released in Greece by Ο.Β.Ε.Π.Κ. toy company. It can be played from 2-10 players and the winner is the player who reaches the final spot first. 
During the game, if a player reaches a spot/number with a red circle, he must follow the orders that mentioned in the instructions booklet. The winner of each round collects a beautiful Disney card with his/her beloved character.

This Cthulhu miniature from Lobotomy 1 is now playable in Lobotomy 2 with the included Daily Nightmare card and the new monster card compatible with Lobotomy 2!  With this pack, you will be able to play an additional Cthulhu scenario.

BIKE DELIVERY:  
A game of dice collecting and strategy

SET UP
Dice must be placed one by one on the 12 available squares of the first half of the board (the first 3 columns).
Then the players dispute the die who starts the game. Whoever is first has the right to choose the exit point before others, but whoever makes the first move is the player who came in second and so on.

HOW TO PLAY
On their turn, each player may move along the paths in 2 different ways:
1) Along the asphalt streets (gray): they may move 1 square at a time, ahead or to the sides.
2) Along the fast bike lanes (brown): 2 squares at a time, ahead or to the sides.
Each player may make their move and collect the die at the door with a number equal to the die in the center.
In this case, the Player has reached door #3 of the green house and may collect the die with number 3.

CHOOSING THE DICE
You must choose and collect dice, trying to pick up those which will provide the best total, and find the best trajectory to take them to their destination.
Choices must take into consideration the number and color of the dice, which grants special features to each of their number:
-  Blue - Increase the number on the die by 1 (+1); 
-  Red - Reduce the number on the die by 1 (-1); 
-  Green - Allows an extra move (>1); 
-  Orange - Double the number of the next die to be picked up (x 2).  
Dice that are collected go to each player's marker on the TALLY CHART.

END OF THE FIRST STAGE
When a player reaches any position of the 4th column - where there is a traffic light in the middle of the game board - the first stage of the game ends.
All the dice to the left of that column are removed and rolled again, completing the 12 squares of the second stage, from the 5th to the 8th column.
Players keep their current position and game resumes with the next player after the one who first reached the middle of the game.

IMPORTANT DETAILS
Picking up 2 dice
In some cases, a player's  movement will allow them to pick up 2 dice (or more):
- When they pick up a green die and make an extra move, they may pick up a second die;
- If they reach a square in which both door numbers allow them to pick up both dice;
- On the fast lane, if they move 2 squares and pass by doors with the same numbers of the dice, they may pick up both.
Extra movement
Picking up 2 green dice only allows for 1 extra move. On the fast lane, picking up a green die also provides an extra move (i.e., a third move).
Changing direction
If a player wants to change direction during the game, they may spend one action/movement to make that inversion.
Doubled dice
The player who picked up an orange die will pick that one with its normal value and double the next die - even if that just happens on the next round. The die with doubled value will go to the gray area of dice tally.
Blocking
A player may not occupy the same square as another player. To move, they must go in a different direction. A player may also choose not to move.
Movement from the brown lane to the gray lane
When leaving the brown lane to the gray lane, a player doesn't lose the second movement. It may be performed in the gray area. To have a second movement in the brown area, the first movement must be in that lane as well.

ENDING THE GAME
To end a game, each player must have picked up at least 1 die of each color.
When a player reaches one of the arrival points, the game ends, and the other players may still play their last turn.
Besides the scoring from the dice, some Bonuses* may also be acquired by players:
- First in Stage 1 (+2 points);
- First in the Final Stage (+2 points);
- Player with the most dice (+3 points).

The total sum of dice and bonuses determines the winner. If they are tied, the winner is the player who collected the most dice.

Everyone dreams of holidays by the sea. On the beach are beach houses, picnic baskets, colourful screens & parasols. Laughter is heard from the beach houses, while seagulls swoop & dive over the heads of sunbathers, watching for easy pickings from unwatched picnic baskets. 

Mewy (Seagulls) is a fast-paced card game for two players where you create an individual tableau by creating a beach, building beach huts & keeping seagulls at bay by lobbing pebbles towards them or sending them onto your opponent’s beach. Your goal is to score as many points as possible by creating co-ordinated rows of beach huts. You can score extra points from items found under pebble tokens. Seagulls score you negative points.

Players draw 3 cards each, keep one, give one to their opponent (face down) & discard one (face down). After drafting, each player has 2 cards to place in their beach tableau (following the placement rules).

Your beach tableau has 3 rows: beach huts (top), beach (middle), seagulls (bottom). No row can be over 10 cards long. Beach hut cards must be played above a beach card & seagulls placed below a beach card, so you have to collect beach cards before you can build up your tableau. Beach cards are just sand or depict pebbles. When you play a pebble beach card you can pick up pebble tokens (which you keep face down, so you don’t know the scoring symbols underneath). Pebble tokens score you VPs at game end.

Beach huts (numbered 1-15) are placed in ascending order & score VPs when in sets of 3 different colours or sets of the same colour. Mewy (Seagull) cards are worth negative points at the end of the game, so trying to get rid of them by scaring them off using pebbles is wise but it’s a balance as some pebbles are worth more VPs than others at game end.

The game & rules are in Polish but cards are language independent.

Designed for Fast Play Convention Miniature Games with the British Empire with the  Dervish, Zulu, Pathans and Boers.

Can be used with other colonial conflicts as well

A race game for 1-4 players, included as part of the press kit for the Sci-Fi Channel's miniseries "Tin Man" (a dystopian reimagining of "The Wizard of Oz"). Players can play as the main characters D.G., Glitch, Wyatt, or Raw, each of whom is depicted with a solid metal figurine, as they search for the Emerald of the Eclipse.

The game is composed of a folding 19.5 inch board, four pewter figurines, one six-sided die, a rule sheet, and a stack of 20 full-color cards.

Goal
Find out who is the best Formula 1 driver. Complete the most laps in the agreed time. You can also agree on a fixed number of race laps in advance. The first driver to complete all laps wins the game.

How do you play the game?
To determine who can start from pole position, each player rolls the dice. The one with the highest throw starts from pole position. The second highest roll starts from position 2, etc. The race starts when the player in pole position rolls the dice. You can move forward, diagonally or sideways. However, you cannot jump over another car. If you cannot pass another car, you lose your moves on your turn.

Special symbols on the board:

     If you end up on this symbol you can take a card. Read the card out loud and take the action immediately.
     Road narrowing; If you come to a narrowing of the road you may not use this space. If another player blocks the passage, you lose your remaining moves on your turn.


Ways to Play
You can play the game in several ways:

     You choose in advance a time period in which you race. The player with the most rounds after that time wins the game.
     You choose a number of rounds in advance that you will complete. The player who completes all the rounds first wins.


Rounds are recorded on paper.

Optional rule
If you complete a turn and are 5 spaces behind another car (not sideways/diagonally) you will end up in the slipstream. This allows you to move forward another 4 places.

Tips!
There is a 'racing line' - so the inside of the corners are often shorter in spaces, allowing you to gain speed! It is possible to block the shortest route, forcing other drivers to overtake outside! Watch out for the chicane as the track narrows to 1 square wide and cars can get blocked here. If it is blocked, you lose your remaining moves and you can only pass when there the space is free.

Start your engines!

A fun action game for two lovers (adults only).  Practice makes perfect so if you don't reach the climax together then try again and again.

Two lovers roll the dice and move around a game board and have to draw passion cards which dare them to perform sensual actions, share secrets, etc.  The game is a race to the climax space game, where the board and the passion cards create obstacles and challenges.  Players move around the track, but in different directions.  The board path is loosely in the shape of a heart, and the center of the heart is the climax space where both players are trying to reach.  Also, the second player gets to roll last, so there is a chance that both players reach the climax together (which is a perfect game, the rules state).

—description from the rulebook

Players place hens, foxes and snakes cards on the table in sets of at least two.  When any player has a set of animals in front of them, they can then also use card to get those animals to hunt: Hens eat snakes, snakes eat foxes, and foxes eat hens.  The player with the most animals in front of their space at the end of the round wins.  Each animal is worth 1 point.

boardgame: The Establishment: Inner Workings of a Secret Cabal
description:
A card-based, story-driven roleplaying game about a secret society controlling the future of a space-faring humanity.

Combining the best elements of a roleplaying game with the benefits of a card game, The Establishment's story-driven, card-based play requires no game master so all players can participate, making it perfect for both RPG newbies and advanced gamers. Like most roleplaying games, players do not complete points-based objectives to win—the goal is to lead the faction and achieve a fitting end to the story.

The game is played over four rounds. Players choose a path they agree with using storyline cards. Players debate, make deals, and support others by playing influencers. A society card, representing the whims of society, may swing the story's direction.

—description from the designer

description:
Across the sea, they have so much stuff. And it's so much better than your stuff. Finer craftsmanship. Better quality materials. Shinier. They have crowns, tiaras, and diadems — and that's just the hats. It's time to get some of that stuff. You want an easy life, and you're prepared to work hard for it, so you've rounded up some old salty dogs, plus a sourpuss and a bitter goldfish. And set sail. The sea is a harsh mistress, but a good cook, at least if you like everything really salty. There are red skies tonight, so they'll be making a batch of Sailor's Delight, which you understand to have tuna fish in it. And soon you'll be attacking merchant ships and taking their treasure. But the real treasure is the happy memories you'll be making.

Dominion: Plunder is the 15th expansion to Dominion. It has 500 cards, with 40 new Kingdom cards. It has lots of Treasures and Durations, with cards that give you Loot, and Traits that modify piles. Events return.

—description from the publisher

description:
While you are visiting the most amusement and dynamic travelling fair, you will have to collect as much tickets as possible. To make the most out of your stay, you will have to play some "fair’s games” and pile up the tickets of every booth before your opponents. Prevent your rivals from scoring, take their tickets away, challenge them and at the same time defend yourself from thieves and other attacks. Choose a strategy and hurry up to be the first one to reach the goal, but you have to be careful, because they will do everything in their hands so that it won’t be easy to reach it. Let’s get started!

Each turn you either play a card or discard as many as you want, and then you replenish your hand to five cards again.

In the fair you could find: 

     4 Different booths, which have to be placed in front of you as your collection, after that you will be able to get its 1, 2 or 3 card tickets.
     4 Types of tickets + the minstrel's tickets.
     The minstrel itself, whom could be doing any kind of roguery.
     2 Firamic crowns. First one to get each objective will receive a high ticket reward.
    4 "fair’s challenges”, which you will have to win playing with the golden cards every time in order to get the tickets. Everyone will offer you one of their cards and you could choose with whom you want to play against.
     A safety box, to protect your progress.
     Some candy, but you must be fast enough to get them! Last one to arrive will pay!
     And a thief ... and if you are lucky, maybe you could also find a security guard nearby for help.


Use your wit to create your own collection of booths, challenges and fill up your pockets with lots of tickets. It is recommended for a 2-4 player’s competition a goal of 20 tickets, and for a 5-6 player’s just 15 tickets.

Is this rivalry going to separate or gather your family and friends?

—description from the designer

Players start with a small amount of game money, then round a Monopoly-like game board where each "property" is a play on a linguistic term (Definition Drive, Antonym Avenue,  Hyperbole Heights, etc). Landing on a property gives players the chance to buy it by correctly answering a related question. Players may also land on Opportunity Knocks spaces, where they draw a card to receive either a bonus ("Your story is voted most imaginative. Collect $10.) or a penalty ("You mix up antonyms and synonyms. Pay $5.). The board's Brain Drain space is its answer to Monopoly's Jail, and Think Tank is its Free Parking.

The winner is the first player who collects all seven properties from one of the four color groups. In addition to buying properties on which they land, players may also buy, sell, or trade properties with one another to reach the goal.

Game questions focus on American English grammar, vocabulary, expressions, and  syntax, and increase in difficulty from kindergarten to high school level. They may be asked to all players in their original order, or may be chosen based on each player's age, so that younger and older players all have questions appropriate to their knowledge level.

Wordopoly is packaged in a durable blue plastic case with a double snap closure. It includes a folding game board, two six-sided dice, money in increments of $5, $10, and $20, 64 Opportunity Knocks cards, 28 deed cards, a rules manual, and six pawns.

Pentwall is an abstract strategy game using pentominoes.

Two players take turns choosing one of 12 different pentomino shapes to fill a shared 10x10 playing area with that shape of their color.

Pentomino shapes can be rotated and flipped. Once a pentomino shape is used, it may not be used by either player again.

The game ends when no more pentominoes can be placed or when all pentominoes have been used.

The player gains the territory of any area not occupied by pentominoes that has more squares of his/her own color in contact with it than his/her opponent. The player with the largest total territory size wins.

This game is distributed for free as a PnP game.

"Which bird eats with its head upside down?" "Can you tell a Seal from a Sea Lion?" "Which animals are the most poisonous?" Players build their animal knowledge through three types of stimulating challenge cards—432 questions in all!

Each card is double-sided with an advanced question on one side and a beginner on the other. Players choose a level to play throughout, which helps keep an even playing field when different ages are playing against each other. Each card represents a different color and corresponds to the spaces on the board. "Know It" cards have multiple choice and true/false questions. "Name It" cards require players to identify the animal in the picture shown. "Rank It" cards challenge players to put a list of animals in order based on criteria like size, speed and strength.

Each correct answer earns a survival token—but there's a twist. Tokens are worth various point values and players keep in secret how many points they have accumulated until the end of the game! Explore your way around the board but watch out for predators, fire, poisonous berries and quicksand! The winner of the game is the player with the highest point value overall.

From family pets to ferocious predators, discover the world's animals with this game the whole family will love!

(whole title eng. Africa Travellers). New society game. Highly funny & amazing)

Roll and move board game from 1900's about 8 famous Africa explorers:
David Livingstone
Heinrich Barth
Gustav Nachtigal
Antoine d'Abbadie
Henry Morton Stanley
László Magyar
Georg Schweinfurth
Gerhard Rohlfs.
Each player choose the explorer. Each explorer has separate unique route of 8 stops. The first player, who reach the stop no 8, wins the game.

Unlike other typical roll and move games, in this game movement to the next stop occurs only if a specific number is rolled. The game also uses money, which can be gained or lost depending on the task and rolled numbers.

Choose your favorite champion, answer the questions and get to know your favorite TV series even better!
Collect 6 character cards and be the first to arrive in the champions' canteen to become the super champion!

A Print & Play promo for Splendor and its expansion Cities of Splendor.

This file contains a set of 9 new Noble tiles that will enrich your Splendor games. There is one limitation though; you cannot use two Noble tiles that have the exact same characteristics.

description:
Put your Emoticon knowledge to the test with this new and amusing card game. Common and famous phrases are shown as Emoticons across 56 unique cards and you simply have to guess the correct phrases.

As we all interpret Emoticons differently this game is not as easy as it sounds and will result in fits of laughter as the ridiculous guesses ensue.

When a team or person successfully guesses the phrase they keep the card. The game ends when you have gone through all the cards.

The person or team with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner. (You can also just play to a set time limit or first to so many cards).

It should be easy to name 3 breeds of dogs - but can you do it under the pressure of 5 seconds twisting down, and with the other players staring at you, waiting for you to get flustered? Time's not on your side, so just say what comes to mind and risk ridiculous answers slipping out as time twirls down on the unique twisted timer! It's all in good fun with this fast-paced game where you have to "Just Spit It Out!"

The 10th Anniversary edition adds 50 new questions about the last decade on 25 new double-sided cards that have a different color scheme from the rest of the deck (blue/gray). The plastic 5-second “Twisted Timer” is dark blue with silver-gray feet.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Battle's Orphan: The Bay of Pigs Invasion April 17-20, 1961
description:
“Success has many fathers, failure is an orphan.”—Tacitus, Agricola 98 AD.

When newly-elected President Kennedy was inaugurated the CIA’s plans and Cuban exile army were ready to invade Castor's Cuba. However, the new president wanted “plausible deniability” and the CIA invasion plans were extensively modified, with all of the Cuban exile aircraft relocated to Nicaragua airbases, and removing any direct support by US armed forces. What was left was very weakened, yet still powerful Cuban exile invasion force armed with tanks, heavy weapons and well trained for sustained combat operations. All that remained was for the Cuban people to rally to their support once the invasion commenced. Can you do as well or better than your opponents? Learn and enjoy!

The game also includes variant units for use with possible US intervention, as well as a second full-sized map for the CIA's preferred invasion site at Trinidad.

The game uses a standard deck of playing cards to determine which player can activate units and how many activations can be made.

The game ends when 12 turns have been played.

The player that scores the most victory points by the end of the last turn of the game is the winner.

The game is composed of the following:

Four 11 by 17 inch map sheets

Two full and one partial sheet of 194 (total) un-mounted, double-sided counters

One Terrain Effects Chart

Ten pages of rules and 4 pages of variant rules

Players will also need a six-sided and a ten-sided set of dice, as well as a standard deck of playing cards to play the game. A custom card set, that replaces the standard deck of playing cards, is available for purchase separately.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Challenger Deck: Flash of Ferocity
description:
Untap, draw, and pass the turn—then thwart your opponent’s every move with powerful counterspells and flash in your army of creatures at instant speed. The Flash of Ferocity deck has everything you need to beat opponents on their own turn.

The Flash of Ferocity deck is tournament-ready, with 60 cards, plus a 15-card sideboard you can use to fine-tune your strategy between games.

The decklist for Flash of Ferocity is based on a top-tier strategy, so it’s the perfect springboard if you’re looking to get back into Magic: The Gathering (MTG).

A Challenger Deck makes a great Magic: The Gathering (MTG) starter deck, powerful enough to jump right into the competition at Friday Night Magic.

Contents: 60-card ready-to-play deck, 15-card sideboard, 5 double-sided tokens, 1 deck box
